settings.label: Corner radius
settings.notes: step/decimals not set on the instance, so NumberField defaults apply: step 1, decimals 0. Renders as -/+ stepper keys (hold-to-repeat, 90ms) plus a TextInput with a DoubleValidator(0..28, 0 decimals); typed entry only commits on editingFinished, then clamps+rounds. Adapter type is `real` even though decimals is 0. Launcher meaning: outer card corner in px.

settings.label: Blur
settings.unit: px (semantic only - no unit shown; SliderRow has no unit property)
settings.notes: decimals: 0, percent not set (false), so the readout is a bare integer. Help text under it: "How much the desktop behind the palette blurs while it is open. 0 keeps it sharp." JsonAdapter property is `int` but SliderRow.modified emits real - coercion happens at the adapter boundary. 0 = off.

settings.label: Weather units
settings.notes: Segmented control. Option keys and their exact visible labels: auto -> "Auto", C -> "°C" (U+00B0 degree sign), F -> "°F". Keys are NOT the labels. `auto` means follow locale.

settings.label: Show weather
settings.notes: Weather glance on the launcher home card. Independent of the Weather units choice - the units row stays enabled even when this is off.

settings.label: Show greeting
settings.notes: The "Good morning" line on the home card.

settings.label: Backdrop image ("Change…" / "Use shipped art")
settings.notes: No conventional labelled row - it is a bespoke inline Item: an elide-middle mono filename readout (basename via .replace(/^.*\//,""), or the literal "Shipped art" when empty) plus two text-link actions: "Change…" (always visible, opens the ImagePicker modal) and "Use shipped art" (visible only when heroImage.length > 0, sets heroImage to ""). Value stored is a file:// URL string (ImagePicker emits "" + fileUrl). Empty string is the sentinel for shipped art, not null. Help text: "A landscape PNG or JPG, ideally 1600px wide or more. It is cropped to a wide banner and dimmed; drag the preview to pick the part that shows."

settings.label: Strength
settings.notes: Backdrop opacity. Stored 0..1, displayed as a percentage. decimals not set on the instance (SliderRow default 2) but percent:true overrides the readout format.

settings.label: Backdrop focal point X (no label - drag the preview)
settings.notes: EASY TO MISS: has no labelled control at all. Only editable by dragging the backdrop preview (DragHandler, enabled only when heroImage.length > 0, cursor SizeAllCursor). Math: on drag start latches ox = heroPosX; then rx = prevImg.width - prevFrame.width, and only if rx > 1 sets clamp(0..1, ox - activeTranslation.x / rx). Inverted drag (image moves with the cursor). If the preview is dropped in a rewrite this setting becomes uneditable.

settings.label: Backdrop focal point Y (no label - drag the preview)
settings.notes: EASY TO MISS: same as heroPosX but the vertical axis. ry = prevImg.height - prevFrame.height, applied only if ry > 1: clamp(0..1, oy - activeTranslation.y / ry). Each axis is gated independently, so a cover-crop that only overflows horizontally leaves heroPosY untouched by the drag.

settings.label: Automatic checks (no per-control label; the eyebrow AUTOMATIC CHECKS is the only labelling, and a live blurb below restates the choice in prose)
settings.notes: THE ONLY PERSISTED SETTING ON THIS PAGE. Option display labels (Segmented model, lines 20-25): off="Off", hourly="Hourly", daily="Daily", weekly="Weekly". NOT in a SettingSection — the group is a bare eyebrow Text (line 216) inside `autoCol`, right-aligned next to UpdateStatus; there is no SettingSection anywhere in this file. Read/write is NOT a shared config store: load is a Process `ryoku-hub config get update_interval` at startup (running:true, line 44-55) whose stdout is whitelist-validated against exactly off|hourly|daily|weekly and silently dropped otherwise; write is a Process `ryoku-hub config set update_interval <k>` fired from setInterval() (line 36-42), which early-returns if the value is unchanged. Applies instantly — no draft, no Save/Apply. Default "daily" appears TWICE and both must survive: QML property init `property string interval: "daily"` (line 18) and Go defaultConfig() in /home/nero/Work/ryoku-arch/ryoku/hub/backend/config.go:25. Effect: drives page.intervalMs (line 60-66; hourly=3600000ms, weekly=604800000ms, DEFAULT BRANCH=86400000ms so daily AND any unknown value both map to 24h) and a Timer (line 68-73) with `running: page.interval !== "off"` and repeat:true calling Updates.check(). "off" is not a duration — it is expressed only by stopping the Timer. Only 2 keys exist in the backend switch: "section" and "update_interval"; configSet errors on anything else.

settings.label: Enabled
settings.notes: When false the ClockPreview dims to opacity 0.32 and the badge reads DISABLED.

settings.label: Face
settings.notes: Labels: Digital, Minimal, Analog, Flip, Rings. Mirrors the live faces in ryoku/shell/quickshell/widgets/clock.

settings.label: Accent
settings.notes: Labels: Wallust, Brand, Mono. Preview resolves brand=#F25623, mono=ink #f5f3ff, wallust=Wallust.accent singleton.

settings.label: Date style
settings.notes: Labels: Inline, Badge, Stacked. Not gated on dateShow - the row stays visible and enabled even when Show date is off.

settings.label: Size
settings.notes: decimals: 2; readout is a bare multiplier (value.toFixed(2)), no unit suffix. Not reflected in the hub preview - only on the real desktop widget.

settings.label: Background
settings.notes: Labels: None, Card, Glass. Gates the visibility of Corner radius. Clock default is 'none' while weather/calendar default to 'glass'.

settings.label: Corner radius
settings.notes: visible: draft.clockBg !== "none". NumberField decimals default 0; steppers auto-repeat every 90ms while held; manual entry clamped by DoubleValidator on editingFinished.

settings.label: Opacity
settings.notes: percent: true - stored 0.2-1.0 but read out as Math.round(value*100)+"%" (20%-100%). Not reflected in the hub preview.

settings.label: Anchor
settings.notes: Shared page.anchorOptions. Labels: Top left, Top, Top right, Left, Centre, Right, Bottom left, Bottom, Bottom right, Free (dragged). 'free' is normally set by dragging the widget on the desktop; gates X/Y visibility.

settings.label: X
settings.notes: visible: draft.clockAnchor === "free". Absolute monitor pixels.

settings.notes: visible: draft.clockAnchor === "free". Absolute monitor pixels.

settings.label: Lock on desktop
settings.notes: Prevents drag/resize of the widget on the wallpaper.

settings.notes: When false the WeatherPreview dims to 0.32 and the badge reads DISABLED.

settings.label: Design
settings.notes: Labels: Card, Minimal, Strip. Note this is a ChoiceRow while the clock's equivalent (Face) and calendar's (Design) are Dropdowns.

settings.notes: Labels are the degree glyphs °C and °F, keys are bare C / F.

settings.notes: Labels: Today, Week.

settings.notes: Also drives the preview's 4s condition-cycling Timer (running: preview.animate).

settings.notes: decimals: 2; bare multiplier readout. Not reflected in the hub preview.

settings.notes: Labels: None, Card, Glass. Gates Corner radius visibility.

settings.notes: visible: draft.weatherBg !== "none".

settings.notes: percent: true - stored 0.2-1.0, shown 20%-100%. Not reflected in the hub preview.

settings.notes: Shared page.anchorOptions (same 10 labels as clock). Gates X/Y visibility.

settings.label: X
settings.notes: visible: draft.weatherAnchor === "free".

settings.label: Y
settings.notes: visible: draft.weatherAnchor === "free".

settings.notes: Only widget that ships OFF by default. When false the CalendarPreview dims to 0.32 / badge reads DISABLED.

settings.label: Design
settings.notes: Labels: Month, Minimal, Agenda, Week, Heatmap. NOTE key/label mismatch on the last one: key is 'heat', label is 'Heatmap'.

settings.label: Accent
settings.notes: Labels: Wallust, Brand, Mono.

settings.label: Starts on
settings.notes: Labels: Monday, Sunday. Sole control in its section.

settings.label: Size
settings.notes: decimals: 2; bare multiplier readout. Not reflected in the hub preview.

settings.label: Background
settings.notes: Labels: None, Card, Glass. Gates Corner radius visibility.

settings.label: Corner radius
settings.notes: visible: draft.calBg !== "none".

settings.label: Opacity
settings.notes: percent: true - stored 0.2-1.0, shown 20%-100%. Not reflected in the hub preview.

settings.label: Anchor
settings.notes: Shared page.anchorOptions (same 10 labels as clock). Gates X/Y visibility.

settings.label: X
settings.notes: visible: draft.calAnchor === "free".

settings.label: Y
settings.notes: visible: draft.calAnchor === "free".

settings.label: Custom window rules (the list itself)
settings.notes: Repeater over store.windowRules; each element is {class, title, action, value}. Rendered as 56px-high rows (Rectangle, Theme.surfaceLo, 1px Theme.line border) inside a Flickable. NOT a SettingSection page - there are zero SettingSection groups, zero ToggleRow/SliderRow/NumberField/ChoiceRow/ColorField/SettingField/Segmented on this page. Every mutation (patch/setAction/addRule/removeRule) slices a fresh array and calls store.editList("windowRules", a). Order is significant: the Go backend names each emitted rule ryoku-user-<index+1> by array position. Empty-state text shows when length === 0.

settings.label: Match class (placeholder text; no visible field label)
settings.notes: Inline bespoke control: Rectangle (h30, Theme.radius, border Theme.ember when focused else Theme.line, ColorAnimation Theme.quick) wrapping a TextInput (id classInput, Theme.mono 13px, Theme.bright, selectByMouse, clip). Placeholder Text "Match class" in Theme.faint, visible only when text === "" && !activeFocus. Commits on onEditingFinished (focus loss / Enter) and only if text !== current - NOT per keystroke. Read via modelData["class"] with bracket syntax because `class` is a JS reserved word. Width = rrow.tfW (computed, shares leftover space with title). Free-form, no validation. Omitted from match when empty.

settings.label: Match title (placeholder text; no visible field label)
settings.notes: Inline bespoke control: Rectangle (h30) wrapping TextInput (id titleInput). Differs from class field: font.family is Theme.font (proportional), not Theme.mono. Placeholder Text "Match title", Theme.faint, visible when empty && unfocused. Commits on onEditingFinished only. Width = rrow.tfW. Free-form, no validation. Omitted from match when empty. If BOTH class and title are empty, genWindowRule returns "" and the whole rule is silently dropped from settings.lua with no UI feedback.

settings.label: Action (no visible label; Dropdown field only)
settings.source: ~/.config/ryoku/hypr.json -> settings.lua (the emitted Lua field is NOT always the action key - see notes)
settings.notes: 25 options from page.actionOptions, width/fieldWidth 150, height 30, label empty so only the field renders. Labels in order: float=Float, tile=Tile, pin=Pin, fullscreen=Fullscreen, maximize=Maximize, center=Centre (British spelling), size=Size (WxH), move=Move (X,Y), workspace=Workspace, opacity=Opacity, noblur=No blur, noborder=No border, noshadow=No shadow, norounding=Square corners, nodim=Never dim, noanim=No animations, opaque=Force opaque, xray=Blur X-ray, nofocus=Never take focus, stayfocused=Hold focus (dialogs), keepaspectratio=Keep aspect ratio, pseudo=Pseudo-tile, immediate=Immediate (tearing), idleinhibit=Block idle/sleep, suppressevent=Ignore app request. onChosen calls page.setAction(index, key) which ALSO resets the sibling value key to page.actionValueDefault(key). Backend name mapping (windowRuleField): nodim->no_dim, noanim->no_anim, noblur->no_blur, noshadow->no_shadow, nofocus->no_focus, stayfocused->stay_focused, keepaspectratio->keep_aspect_ratio; norounding emits `rounding = 0`; noborder emits `border_size = 0`; all other booleans emit `<key> = true`.

settings.label: Value - free-form text (no visible label; placeholder is an action-dependent hint)
settings.def: "" (actionValueDefault returns "" for all four text actions)
settings.source: ~/.config/ryoku/hypr.json -> settings.lua (opacity -> `opacity = <num>`, size -> `size = { w, h }`, move -> `move = { x, y }`, workspace -> `workspace = "<str>"`)
settings.notes: Rectangle+TextInput (id valInput, Theme.mono 13px), width rrow.valW = 110, height 30. visible ONLY when rrow.textValue, i.e. action is one of page.textValueActions = [opacity, size, move, workspace]. Placeholder = rrow.valueHint, which switches on action: opacity -> "0.0 - 1.0", size -> "1200x800", move -> "100,60", workspace -> "2", else "". Commits on onEditingFinished only. NO min/max/step/unit and NO validation in the UI despite opacity being a 0..1 real and size/move being numeric pairs - all four are stored as free-form strings. Go coerces at generate time: parseFloat(r.Value, 1) for opacity, parseWxH(r.Value) for size and move; workspace is passed through as a Lua string.

settings.label: Value - Block idle/sleep mode (no visible label; Dropdown field only)
settings.def: "always" (page.actionValueDefault("idleinhibit"); backend also re-clamps anything else to "always")
settings.notes: Second Dropdown in the row, width/fieldWidth rrow.valW = 110, height 30. visible only when rrow.choiceValue && action === "idleinhibit". Labels: always=Always, focus=Focus, fullscreen=Fullscreen. current falls back to page.actionValueDefault(action) when value is empty. onChosen calls page.patch(index, "value", k). Shares the same `value` config key as the free-form field and the suppressevent dropdown - one key, three different controls.

settings.label: Value - Ignore app request event (no visible label; Dropdown field only)
settings.def: "maximize" (page.actionValueDefault("suppressevent"); backend also re-clamps anything else to "maximize")
settings.notes: Same Dropdown instance as idleinhibit - the options binding ternaries between page.idleInhibitOptions and page.suppressEventOptions on action. Labels: maximize=Maximize, fullscreen=Fullscreen, activate=Activate, activatefocus=Activate focus. Note the option key is "activatefocus" (one word, no separator) - the Go validator compares against that exact spelling. visible only when rrow.choiceValue && action === "suppressevent".

settings.label: Match class
settings.group: Match row (card header, above the LOOK divider — no SettingSection)
settings.notes: Inline `component MatchField` (bordered Rectangle + TextInput), NOT SettingField. mono: true (Theme.mono font). Placeholder "Match class (e.g. kitty)". Commits on onEditingFinished ONLY (`if (text !== mf.value) mf.committed(text)`), never per keystroke — the Repeater rebuilds cards on every patch and would steal keystrokes. Width = (cardWidth - 30 - 20)/2. Border tints Theme.ember on activeFocus. Go: `Class string json:"class"`. If class AND title are both empty, genAppOverride() returns "" and the record silently generates NO window rule but still persists in hypr.json.

settings.label: Match title (optional)
settings.group: Match row (card header, above the LOOK divider — no SettingSection)
settings.notes: Inline `component MatchField`, mono: false (Theme.font). Placeholder "Match title (optional)". Commits on onEditingFinished only. Same width as classField. Go: `Title string json:"title"`. Optional second match term: emitted as `title = "…"` inside the rule's match table only when non-empty.

settings.label: Opacity
settings.def: -1 (Inherit) on add; customDefault 0.9 when switched to Custom
settings.notes: Inline `component OvNum`: label(104px) + Segmented[Inherit|Custom] + Slider + mono readout(42px). NOT SliderRow. The Segmented model is EXACTLY [{key:"inherit",label:"Inherit"},{key:"custom",label:"Custom"}]. Slider + readout are `visible: ov.custom` only. Sentinel encoding: -1 = inherit, `readonly property bool custom: ov.value >= 0`. Selecting Custom emits `ov.value >= 0 ? ov.value : ov.customDefault` → 0.9. decimals defaults to 2 (unused here, percent wins). Go: `Opacity float64 json:"opacity"` // -1 inherit, else 0..1. Emitted as `opacity = <num>` only when `Opacity >= 0 && Opacity <= 1`. Overridden entirely by Force opaque = on.

settings.label: Corners
settings.def: -1 (Inherit) on add; customDefault 8 when switched to Custom
settings.notes: Inline `component OvNum`, decimals: 0, percent: false → readout is `value.toFixed(0)`. Write path applies Math.round(v) in the onChanged handler even though slider step is 1. Segmented [inherit|custom]. Sentinel -1 = inherit. Go: `Rounding int json:"rounding"` // -1 inherit, else >= 0. Emitted as `rounding = %d` when `Rounding >= 0` — note 0 IS a valid custom value (square corners) and is distinct from -1.

settings.label: Border
settings.def: -1 (Inherit) on add; customDefault 2 when switched to Custom
settings.notes: Inline `component OvNum`, decimals: 0. Math.round(v) on write. Segmented [inherit|custom]. Sentinel -1 = inherit; 0 is a valid custom value (no border). Go: `BorderSize int json:"borderSize"` // -1 inherit, else >= 0. Emitted as `border_size = %d` (snake_case in Lua, camelCase in JSON) when `BorderSize >= 0`.

settings.label: Blur
settings.notes: Inline `component OvChoice`: label(104px) + right-anchored Segmented. options bound to page.offChoice = [{key:"inherit",label:"Inherit"},{key:"off",label:"Off"}]. Value read defensively as `card.modelData.blur || "inherit"`. Go: `Blur string json:"blur"` // inherit | off. Emits `no_blur = true` ONLY when == "off"; there is deliberately no "on" member (you cannot force blur on per-app).

settings.label: Shadow
settings.notes: Inline `component OvChoice`, options = page.offChoice (labels "Inherit"/"Off"). Go: `Shadow string json:"shadow"`. Emits `no_shadow = true` only when == "off".

settings.label: Dim inactive
settings.notes: Inline `component OvChoice`, options = page.offChoice. Label is "Dim inactive", key is the shorter `dim`. Go: `Dim string json:"dim"`. Emits `no_dim = true` only when == "off".

settings.label: Animations
settings.notes: Inline `component OvChoice`, options = page.offChoice. Label "Animations" but key is `anim` (not `animations` — that name is taken by the global appearance bool in HyprStore). Go: `Anim string json:"anim"`. Emits `no_anim = true` only when == "off".

settings.label: Force opaque
settings.notes: THE ODD ONE OUT: inline `component OvChoice` but options bound to page.onChoice = [{key:"inherit",label:"Inherit"},{key:"on",label:"On"}] — "on"/"On", NOT "off"/"Off" like the other four EFFECTS rows. A rewrite that treats all five as one uniform tri-state will silently corrupt this key. Go: `Opaque string json:"opaque"` // inherit | on. Emits `opaque = true` only when == "on". Cross-field: per the helper text it "removes transparency entirely (overrides Opacity)" — an interaction encoded only in prose and in Hyprland's rule precedence, with no UI enforcement (both can be set at once).

settings.label: Voice typing
settings.source: NOT a config.toml key. On disk this is systemd user-unit enablement: ~/.config/systemd/user/voxtype.service (+ default.target.wants symlink). Written via `ryoku-hub voxtype set '{"enabled":...}'` -> systemctl --user enable + restart voxtype.service (on) / disable --now (off). Read back via `systemctl --user is-enabled --quiet voxtype.service`.
settings.notes: Applies instantly on toggle (page.setEnabled -> apply -> Process). No save button, no dirty state. GATED: enabled: page.voiceOn || page.usable — you can always switch OFF, but can only switch ON when page.usable = (sel.cloud ? keyOnFile : sel.present). A locked toggle renders at opacity 0.45. Every apply() sends a FULL snapshot {preset, enabled, openaiKey:""} — the toggle rewrites the whole config.toml. Helper Text under it has 3 states: usable -> "Tap Super+` to dictate into whatever app has focus; tap again to stop. The pill grows a live mic wave while you speak." (Theme.dim); cloud+no key -> "Add an API key below before you can switch this on." (Theme.ember); local+no model -> "Download a model below before you can switch this on." (Theme.ember).

settings.label: (no visible label — the section title "ENGINE & MODEL" is the only label; the control is a click-to-select card list)
settings.source: ~/.config/voxtype/config.toml — written wholesale by buildVoxtypeConfig() in /home/nero/Work/ryoku-arch/ryoku/hub/backend/voxtype.go, mode 0600, atomicWrite. Selection is persisted ONLY as a TOML *comment* marker `# ryoku-preset: <key>` and read back by selectedPreset() scanning for that comment prefix.
settings.notes: Repeater over page.presets with a bespoke inline Rectangle delegate (NOT ChoiceRow/Dropdown/Segmented). Options are NOT declared in the QML — they come from `ryoku-hub voxtype get` (voxtypePresets() in voxtype.go). Selecting expands into whisper.model / whisper.language / whisper.mode / whisper.remote_model / whisper.remote_endpoint. QML fallback default page.selected: "whisper-fast"; backend selectedPreset() fallback "whisper-fast"; voxtypeEnsure() seeds whisper-fast on first run. Card visuals: active -> Theme.frameBg + Theme.ember border; hover -> Theme.keyTop; idle -> Theme.surfaceLo; ColorAnimation Theme.quick on color and border.color. TapHandler selects (page.choose); HoverHandler sets PointingHandCursor. SIDE EFFECT: choose() on a local preset that is not downloaded triggers download() instead of selecting; selection only lands later in dlProc.onExited on exit code 0.

settings.label: Whisper — Fast
settings.notes: Repeater row 1. provider "OpenAI", size "142 MB", detail "English only, quickest to load. A solid everyday default.", cloud=false, keyKind="". Meta line renders: "OpenAI  ·  142 MB" + one of "  ·  downloading…" / "  ·  downloaded" / "  ·  not downloaded" (Theme.ember when busy or missing, else Theme.faint). Trailing action Item (26x26): Spinner size 18 while busy; else Icon "trash" size 16 (present, hover tint Theme.bad) or "download" size 18 (absent, hover tint Theme.ember), idle tint Theme.faint. Click -> page.removeModel(key) if present else page.download(key). Download cmd: `ryoku-hub voxtype download whisper-fast` -> `voxtype setup --download --model base.en --no-post-install`. Remove cmd: `ryoku-hub voxtype rmmodel whisper-fast` -> os.Remove of the ggml bin. present = fileExists(ggml-base.en.bin).

settings.label: Whisper — Accurate
settings.notes: Repeater row 2. provider "OpenAI", size "1.6 GB", detail "Multilingual, higher accuracy, larger download.", cloud=false, keyKind="". Same card chrome + trailing download/trash/Spinner action as row 1. Download cmd: `voxtype setup --download --model large-v3-turbo --no-post-install`. Note language is "auto" here vs "en" for whisper-fast — the only preset that sets auto.

settings.label: OpenAI API
settings.notes: Repeater row 3. provider "OpenAI", size "cloud", detail "Cloud Whisper through OpenAI's API. Needs a key; audio leaves your machine.", cloud=true, keyKind="openai", present forced true by backend. NO trailing action at all: Icon visible only when !cloud, MouseArea enabled only when !cloud && !busy. Meta line omits the downloaded/not-downloaded suffix for cloud. Selecting it reveals the API KEY section (page.needsKey = keyKind !== ""). When cloud is selected the config ALSO hardcodes decoy locals: model="base.en", language="en".

settings.label: OpenAI API key
settings.def: "" (unset; key is omitted from config.toml entirely when empty)
settings.source: ~/.config/voxtype/config.toml, under [whisper], mode 0600. Also satisfied read-only by env VOXTYPE_WHISPER_API_KEY.
settings.notes: SECTION visible only when page.needsKey (i.e. selected preset has keyKind !== "" — today only "openai"). Bespoke inline Rectangle (h 40, Theme.radius) + TextInput with echoMode: TextInput.Password, Theme.mono 13, selectionColor Theme.ember, selectedTextColor Theme.onAccent, clip. Placeholder Text "OpenAI API key" (Theme.faint) shown when text.length === 0. Focus styling: bg Theme.surface + border Theme.ember when activeFocus, else Theme.surfaceLo + Theme.line, ColorAnimation Theme.quick. MouseArea over the field forceActiveFocus. Commits on Enter (onAccepted) or the "Save key" button — NOT live. Field is cleared to "" after every commit. WRITE-ONLY: the key is never read back into the UI, only the bool openaiKeySet. saveKey() early-returns on empty; backend treats openaiKey=="" as "keep the stored key" (re-reads remote_api_key and rewrites it), so it is never wiped by unrelated saves. Helper Text 2 states: keyOnFile -> "A key is saved. Enter a new one to replace it." (Theme.ok); else -> "This cloud engine needs an API key. It is stored locally in your Voxtype config." (Theme.subtle).

settings.label: Save key
settings.notes: HubButton icon "check", primary: true. onClicked: page.saveKey(keyInput.text); keyInput.text = "". Sits in a Row with the key field, which is sized width: parent.width - saveBtn.width - 10. Same effect as pressing Enter in the field.

settings.label: Install Voxtype
settings.notes: Only reachable when page.loaded && !page.installed (the whole Flickable is hidden then). HubButton icon "download", primary: true, horizontally centered inside a wrapper Item. Runs Quickshell.execDetached(["kitty","--hold","-e","gpk","install","voxtype-bin","--manager","aur"]) — a detached terminal, because gpk needs a tty for AUR build + sudo. --hold keeps errors on screen. The page self-heals on return via onWindowActiveChanged -> reload().

settings.label: Remove Voxtype
settings.notes: HubButton icon "trash", primary: false (ghost). Runs detached kitty sh -c: `gpk remove voxtype-bin && { systemctl --user disable --now voxtype.service 2>/dev/null; rm -f ~/.config/systemd/user/voxtype.service; systemctl --user daemon-reload 2>/dev/null; }`. Config and models deliberately survive for a reinstall. Section body Text: "Voxtype is installed (voxtype-bin). Removing it uninstalls the package; your engine choice and downloaded models stay on disk."

settings.group: (none — this page uses NO SettingSection at all; every control is a bespoke inline Rectangle + TapHandler inside the per-plugin card)
settings.label: Enabled (the switch is unlabelled — it sits at the right of the plugin name/description row)
settings.notes: PluginsPage.qml:336-352. Bespoke pill switch (46x26, radius Theme.radius, 20px knob animated on x). `card.on = place && place.enabled === true`, so any missing/non-true value reads as off. Tap calls page.place(id, "enabled", on ? "false" : "true") -> `ryoku-plugins-place <id> enabled <bool>` (string arg, --argjson'd into a real JSON bool). SIDE EFFECT: the backend runs seed_entry() when enabled=true, injecting the manifest's preset settings block (metadata.settings[].default, or legacy metadata.defaultSettings) plus a default host into the entry — the toggle is not a pure boolean write. Turning off also gates the entire rest of the card: "Show as", the placement editor, and the desktop-widget hint are all `visible: card.on`. Also fired implicitly by page.removePlugin() before uninstall.

settings.label: Show as
settings.def: placement.host, else manifest.defaults.host, else "framePopout" (QML fallback at PluginsPage.qml:295-296)
settings.notes: PluginsPage.qml:391-436. Repeater-generated chip row, NOT the shared Segmented.qml — bespoke Rectangles (h 28, Theme.keyTop when active / Theme.surfaceLo when not). Model is per-plugin and dynamic: `(card.man.hosts || ["framePopout"]).filter(h => h === "framePopout" || h === "desktopWidget")` — the option set comes from each plugin's manifest.json `hosts` array, hard-filtered to those two; any third host in a manifest is silently dropped here. Display strings: framePopout -> "Frame popout", desktopWidget -> "Desktop widget", anything else -> raw key. Whole row is `visible: card.on`. Writes via `ryoku-plugins-place <id> host <name>`, which also forces `enabled: (existing // true)`.

settings.group: (none — inside the embedded PluginPlacementEditor, visible only when card.on && card.host === "framePopout")
settings.label: Edge (chips: Top | Right | Bottom | Left)
settings.notes: PluginPlacementEditor.qml:156-180 (component embedded at PluginsPage.qml:438-444). Repeater model is the literal [{k:"top",l:"Top"},{k:"right",l:"Right"},{k:"bottom",l:"Bottom"},{k:"left",l:"Left"}]. Bespoke 24px chips, not Segmented.qml. Cannot be written alone — the tap emits changed("framePopout", [edge, ed.align, ed.hoverW(), ed.hoverH()]), a single 4-positional write that re-stamps align/hoverW/hoverH every time. Default "right" is a read-side fallback in the editor (place.framePopout.edge || "right"); the backend has no edge default (edge is required).

settings.label: (no label — set by dragging the "popout" chip along the chosen edge of the LIVE PLACEMENT stage)
settings.notes: PluginPlacementEditor.qml:108-152. There is NO discrete control for this — the only way to set it is a MouseArea drag (drag.axis = YAxis when edge is left/right, else XAxis) on the popout body preview, committed onReleased. Snap rule: a = (centre / span) < 0.5 ? "start" : "end" — the middle third of every edge is reserved for island/mixer/power (shown as a dashed "reserved" band) and collapses to whichever of start/end is nearer, so "centre" is never a persistable value. Written as part of the same compound framePopout 4-arg write. Backend default when omitted: start.

settings.label: (no control anywhere on this page — carried through on every write)
settings.notes: PluginPlacementEditor.qml:28 `function hoverW() { return place.framePopout.hoverW || 320 }`. Invisible pass-through: it has no editor here, but EVERY edge tap and every align drag-release re-writes it as positional arg 3 of `ryoku-plugins-place <id> framePopout <edge> <align> <hoverW> <hoverH>`. Backend default is also 320. A rewrite that emits a framePopout write without reading the current value will silently reset whatever another surface set.

settings.label: (no control anywhere on this page — carried through on every write)
settings.notes: PluginPlacementEditor.qml:29. Same invisible pass-through as hoverW, positional arg 4. Backend default 16.

settings.label: Graphics mode
settings.notes: THE ONLY PERSISTED SETTING ON THE PAGE. Visible option labels are "Hybrid" / "Performance" / "Passthrough" (keys are lowercase). Width clamped to Math.min(parent.width, 460). current: page.mode; onChosen(k) -> page.setMode(k) -> Process `ryoku-hub gpu mode set <k>` -> `ryoku-gpu mode <k>`. NOT a direct key write: hybrid DELETES the pin line (cmd_disable), performance writes hl.env("AQ_DRM_DEVICES", <strongest GPU>) (cmd_persist, RYOKU_GPU_FORCE=1), passthrough writes the iGPU slot ALONE (cmd_persist_solo) and errors if no integrated GPU exists. Read-back is `ryoku-hub gpu mode get` (modeProc) -> JSON {mode}; empty/absent key => "hybrid" (the shipped gpu.lua is comment-only, so hybrid is the true default). Change applies at NEXT LOGIN only (Hyprland reads env at start). On success modeSetProc stdout triggers page.reload(); on stderr, page.modeWarn shows an ember banner. The backend REFUSES passthrough when the dGPU drives the screen and the iGPU does not (wouldStrandDisplay) with a long BIOS/MUX message. Mode set also returns a "cost" field (relogin | reboot) that the QML currently ignores.

settings.label: Readiness checks / Hide readiness checks (disclosure)
settings.notes: Inline disclosure header, not a ToggleRow: Icon (name "collapse" when open, "expand" when closed, size 13) + mono 11px DemiBold letterSpacing-1 Text, both tinted Theme.cream on hover / Theme.dim otherwise via HoverHandler(chkHov, PointingHandCursor); TapHandler toggles. Reveals the checks column by animating height 0 -> checksCol.implicitHeight (Behavior NumberAnimation Theme.medium / Theme.ease) and opacity 0 -> 1 (Theme.quick), with visible: height > 0.5 and clip. NOT persisted — resets to collapsed on every page load.

settings.label: Disable passthrough
settings.notes: icon "trash", non-primary. visible ONLY when page.caps.enabled === true. onClicked: page.act(["ryoku-hub","gpu","apply","disable"]) -> runProc; stdout -> page.reload(), stderr -> page.actionError banner. Escalates via pkexec inside the backend.

settings.label: Review changes
settings.notes: icon "search", non-primary. visible when page.caps.enabled !== true && page.caps.verdict !== "incapable" && !page.planning && !page.enabling. onClicked: page.reviewEnable() -> planProc `ryoku-hub gpu apply enable --dry-run`; stdout -> page.planText and page.planning = true (opens the plan console).

settings.label: Enable passthrough
settings.source: writes the 5 managed /etc files above + installs qemu-desktop, libvirt, edk2-ovmf, swtpm, dnsmasq (pacman) and looking-glass, looking-glass-module-dkms (AUR/yay); adds user to libvirt/kvm; enables libvirtd; kvmfr static_size_mb=128
settings.notes: icon "check", primary: true. Lives in a Row with "Close", visible only when page.planning. onClicked: page.enableInTerminal() — does NOT run in-process; Quickshell.execDetached(["kitty","--class","ryoku-gpu","-e","sh","-c","ryoku-hub gpu apply enable; echo; read -n1 -rsp 'Done. Press any key to close…'; echo"]) then planning=false, planText="", enabling=true.

settings.label: Close
settings.notes: icon "close", non-primary. Second button in the planning Row. onClicked: page.planning = false; page.planText = "" — dismisses the plan console without enabling.

settings.label: Recheck
settings.notes: icon "refresh", non-primary. visible only when page.enabling. onClicked: page.recheck() -> enabling = false; page.reload() (re-runs capsProc + modeProc).

settings.label: Retry
settings.group: (no SettingSection — floating error column under the hero card)
settings.notes: icon "refresh", primary: true. Lives in the caps-probe failure Column (visible when page.capsError !== ""), anchored to hero.left/hero.right/hero.bottom + 16 margin. onClicked: page.reload(), which first clears capsError so the retry shows progress instead of a frozen error.

settings.label: Passthrough status line (verdict readout)
settings.notes: Row: 7px dot (radius 3.5) + wrapping Text at width parent.width-17, both coloured by page.ptColor. Colour map: ready=Theme.ok; needs-relogin/needs-reboot=Theme.ember; incapable=Theme.bad; default=Theme.bad if capsError else Theme.subtle. Each verdict has an exact copy string in page.ptText (interpolating page.dgpuName), all six must survive verbatim.

settings.label: Readiness checks dossier rows (Repeater over caps.checks)
settings.source: `ryoku-hub gpu caps` -> caps.checks[] (built by backend/hwcaps.go buildChecks)
settings.notes: Repeater model: page.caps.checks || [] — a BACKEND-GENERATED list, not a static QML model, so the row count varies per machine (iommu-isolation and display-owner only when a dGPU exists; session only when libvirt is installed; tooling has 3 mutually exclusive shapes: ok/installed, warn/QEMU not installed, warn/missing:<list> (passthrough only)). Delegate is the inline `component CheckRow` (height 30): 7px dot + 13px Theme.cream label on the left, mono 11px Medium value on the right, both tinted by check.level (ok=Theme.ok, warn=Theme.ember, else Theme.bad; Theme.dim when check is null). NOTE: the Check JSON carries a `hint` field with the actual remediation text for every failing check and the page NEVER RENDERS IT — a rewrite should preserve this gap deliberately or fix it, not lose the field.

settings.label: Graphics mode explainer (per-mode helper text)
settings.notes: Theme.dim 12px wrapping Text with three exact strings, one per mode: hybrid = "Hybrid keeps the built-in GPU primary for battery; apps can still use <dGPU> on demand."; performance = "Performance pins <dGPU> as primary: fastest, more power draw."; passthrough = "Passthrough runs the desktop on the built-in GPU so <dGPU> is free for a VM." Followed by a fixed Theme.faint 12px line: "A change takes effect on your next login."

settings.label: Passthrough section intro
settings.notes: Theme.subtle 13px wrapping Text: "Free <dGPU> from the desktop and bind it to vfio so a virtual machine can own it for near-native performance. This sets up the host only; you run the VM yourself (libvirt + Looking Glass). Everyday VMs in ryovm need none of this." Load-bearing scope boundary — the page is graphics hardware only; running VMs lives in the ryovm app.

settings.label: Enabled
settings.def: false (seed_entry writes `enabled: ((.[$id].enabled) // false)`; note ryoku-plugins-place `host` and `desktopWidget`/`framePopout` subcommands instead default it to true when the entry is absent)
settings.notes: Written as the string "true"/"false" by the QML (`v ? "true" : "false"`) but jq --argjson parses it into a real JSON bool. Side effect: setting it to true also runs seed_entry, which injects the manifest preset block (default host + every metadata.settings[].default) for missing keys only. Checked state reads `page.sel.placement.enabled === true`, so a missing entry reads OFF. No round-trip optimism: the toggle only moves after placeProc exits -> page.refresh() -> reselect().

settings.label: Show as
settings.def: manifest.defaults.host (falls back to "desktopWidget" in ryoku-plugins-place seed_entry; "" in the QML when unset)
options: <any other string in manifest.hosts> (rendered verbatim as its own key — the option list is NOT a fixed enum, it is manifest.hosts mapped 1:1)
settings.notes: HIDDEN unless manifest.hosts.length > 1 — a single-host plugin never shows this row. Options are data-driven from manifest.hosts; only framePopout and desktopWidget get pretty labels, anything else falls through to the raw key. Writing host also force-defaults enabled to true if the entry did not exist.

settings.group: (plugin-declared, from manifest.metadata.settings[].group — group headers are rendered by PluginSettingsForm itself, one per distinct `group` string, in schema order; fields with group "" get no header)
settings.label: (plugin-declared, field.label, falling back to field.key)
settings.control: custom — PluginSettingsForm dispatches on field.type: choice->Dropdown, toggle->ToggleRow, slider->SliderRow, image->bespoke inline file-picker row, text/anything-unknown->bespoke inline TextInput row
settings.kind: enum|bool|real|string|string(path) — determined at runtime by the plugin's manifest, NOT statically knowable
settings.def: field.default (per-field, from manifest.metadata.settings[].default; seeded into plugins.json by seed_entry on enable)
settings.min: field.min (SliderRow only; falls back to 0 when the schema omits it)
settings.max: field.max (SliderRow only; falls back to 1 when the schema omits it)
settings.step: field.step (SliderRow only; falls back to 0.01 when the schema omits it)
settings.unit: none — SliderRow has a `percent` property but PluginSettingsForm never sets it; readout precision is field.decimals, default 2
options: (plugin-declared) field.options[] mapped {value -> key, label -> label}; the option set is entirely defined by the installed plugin's manifest and cannot be enumerated from this file
settings.source: ~/.config/ryoku/plugins.json (via `ryoku-plugins-place <id> settings <json>`, one single-key object per change, jq-merged into the existing settings object)
settings.notes: THE BIG ONE. This is one QML element that expands into an unbounded, per-plugin set of real settings rows. PluginSettingsForm keeps a local `values` copy (deep-cloned from placement.settings) so edits paint before the disk round-trip; `_val` falls back to field.default when the key is absent. Slider writes Math.round(v) when field.decimals === 0. Toggle accepts both real bool true and the string "true" as checked (legacy tolerance). Text row commits on editingFinished only (not per keystroke) and has a placeholder from field.placeholder. Image row writes the raw file:// URL string from the FileDialog. Whole form is hidden when schema.length === 0.

settings.label: (unlabelled segmented: emblem kind)
settings.def: none (QML placeholder page.model literal, line 18); "builtin" from backend defaultFastfetchModel(); shipped config.jsonc ships "kitty-direct" which reads back as "image"
settings.notes: Model key is logo.kind; disk key is logo.type. WRITE mapping (ffBuildLogo): image->"kitty-direct", ascii->"file", builtin->"builtin", none->"none". READ mapping (ffNormalizeLogo) is many-to-one and LOSSY: kitty|kitty-direct|kitty-icat|chafa|sixel|iterm|raw -> image; file|file-raw|data|data-raw -> ascii; builtin|small -> builtin; anything else -> none. Selecting builtin/none hides the source row and the three NumberFields, and forces width/height to 0 on write. Live: swaps the Hub's 84x84 emblem tile only (readout preview always runs `fastfetch --logo none`).

settings.label: (file path readout) + "Choose image" / "Choose .txt" button
settings.notes: Row visible only when kind==image||ascii. Elided Text shows the path or "No file chosen" (Theme.faint when empty). Button label is "Choose .txt" when kind==ascii, else "Choose image"; opens asciiDlg or imageDlg. imageDlg nameFilters: ["Images (*.svg *.png *.jpg *.jpeg *.webp)","All files (*)"]; asciiDlg nameFilters: ["Text (*.txt *.ascii *.art)","All files (*)"]. Accept runs `ryoku-hub fastfetch import-logo <path>`, which COPIES the file into ~/.config/fastfetch/ under a FIXED name: ryoku-logo.<lowercased ext>, or rasterizes an .svg to ryoku-logo.png at 512x512 (rsvg-convert, else magick, else convert; error if none). The printed path becomes logo.source and importProc.pendingKind sets logo.kind. Not a free-text field: there is no way to type a path.

settings.label: Width
settings.notes: NumberField decimals:0, step defaults to 1 (not set explicitly). Visible only when kind==image||ascii. Written with omitempty, and ffBuildLogo forces Width=0 for builtin/none, so the key vanishes for those kinds and reload re-defaults to 28. NOT visible in the Hub preview (readout preview runs `--logo none`; the emblem tile is a fixed 84x84 Image) — only "Preview in terminal" shows its effect.

settings.label: Height
settings.notes: decimals:0, step 1 implicit. Visible only when kind==image||ascii. Forced to 0 for builtin/none and omitted (omitempty), so reload re-defaults to 14. Unit label says "col" although height is rows. Not reflected in the Hub preview.

settings.label: Pad
settings.notes: decimals:0, step 1 implicit. Visible only when kind==image||ascii. Model key is logo.padding (a scalar); on write ffBuildLogo emits the OBJECT {"top":5,"right":5,"left":<value>} with top/right HARDCODED — a user's hand-set top/right padding is clobbered on every Save. On read, padding accepts either a scalar or an object and only lifts padding.left. Padding is omitted entirely for builtin/none. Not reflected in the Hub preview.

settings.label: Readout accent
settings.def: 226;52;42 (#e2342a — brand vermilion, backend const ffAccent; also the QML placeholder page.model.accent)
settings.notes: Model stores an ANSI triple "R;G;B"; ColorField speaks hex, converted by page.accentHex()/page.hexToTriple(). Written to disk as display.color.keys = "38;2;<R;G;B>" (ffApplyAccentToDisplay) AND baked into every rebuilt custom line's format string (ffTaglineFormat uses the accent on the ■ bullet; ffHeaderFormat on the leading ──). Read back by ffDisplayAccent, which accepts display.color as either a string or {keys:...} and regexes out the first R;G;B; falls back to 226;52;42 when missing/unparseable. Invalid accents are also rejected at build time (ffKeysColor) and fall back to the brand default. ColorField supplies 8 HARDCODED presets, all dark surface colours inherited from its shell-surface origin and nonsensical as accents: #1a1b26, #16161e, #1b1612, #1e1e2e, #181825, #11111b, #0f1115, #232136. Hex field validator: /#?[0-9A-Fa-f]{6}/. Only display.color.keys is editable; display.key.width, display.separator and display.percent survive only as opaque preserved JSON.

settings.label: (per row) move up / move down
settings.notes: Repeater over page.model.rows; each row emits a RowMoveBtn "↑" and "↓" calling page.moveRow(index, -1/+1), a plain array swap clamped at both ends. Row order IS the modules[] order on disk. No drag-arrange.

settings.label: (per row) enable / disable
settings.def: true (every row loaded from disk is Enabled:true; ffNormalizeModule hardcodes it)
settings.notes: RowMoveBtn glyph "●" (enabled, tint Theme.ember) / "○" (disabled, tint Theme.faint); the whole row Rectangle drops to opacity 0.45 when disabled. THIS IS A SET-MEMBERSHIP TOGGLE, AND IT IS DESTRUCTIVE: buildFastfetch does `if !r.Enabled { continue }`, so a disabled row is omitted from modules[] on Save and is gone forever on the next load — there is no disabled-but-remembered state on disk. Disabled is a session-only state.

settings.label: (per row) inline text / key editor
settings.notes: Bespoke inline TextInput in a Rectangle (height 28, border warms to Theme.ember on focus, selectByMouse). Visible only when page.rowEditable(kind) — i.e. kind is "tagline", "header" or "module"; hidden for title/break/colors/raw. Commits on editingFinished only (not per-keystroke) via page.setRow(index, f, text) where f="key" for modules and "text" otherwise. page.rowPlaceholder() returns "LABEL" for module and "text" otherwise but is DEAD CODE — TextInput has no placeholder and rowPlaceholder is never called. For kind==module the text is written straight to modules[].key; an EMPTY key is silently ignored on write (`if r.Key != ""`) so the old key in Raw survives — a key cannot be cleared. For tagline/header the text is re-wrapped into a rebuilt ANSI template: tagline -> "\x1b[38;2;<accent>m■\x1b[0m \x1b[38;2;143;135;112m<text>\x1b[0m"; header -> "\x1b[38;2;<accent>m──\x1b[0m \x1b[1;38;2;243;237;225m<LABEL>\x1b[0m \x1b[38;2;58;46;36m<rule>\x1b[0m" where rule = (32 - runelen(label)) "─" chars, floored at 4.

settings.label: (per row) remove
settings.notes: RowMoveBtn glyph "✕" -> page.removeRow(index). No confirmation, no undo beyond the bar's Revert.

settings.label: (per row) row name
options: module -> the module's key, else Title-cased type
settings.notes: 120px elided Text showing modelData.label || modelData.kind. The label is INFERRED on load by ffNormalizeModule, never written to disk. Row kinds the model can hold: title | tagline | header | module | break | colors | raw. Kind inference is heuristic: a bare JSON string module -> break; type "title" -> title; type "custom" -> header if the ANSI-stripped format contains "─" ELSE tagline; type "colors" -> colors; anything else -> module with Module=type.

settings.label: Add a row
settings.def: "" (current is hardcoded to "", so the field always shows the placeholder)
settings.notes: placeholder "Add…". 19 options built by page.addOptions. Labels: __header="Section header", __break="Spacer", __colors="Colour swatches" (no hint); then all 16 page.catalog modules, each carrying hint:"module" (rendered dim/right-aligned/mono by Dropdown). Catalog key->label: cpu=CPU, gpu=GPU, memory=Memory, disk=Disk, kernel=Kernel, os=OS, host=Host, uptime=Uptime, packages=Packages, shell=Shell, terminal=Terminal, wm=WM, de=Desktop, battery=Battery, localip=Local IP, board=Board. addFromMenu() appends: __header -> {kind:header, enabled:true, text:"SECTION", label:"Section header"}; __break -> {kind:break, enabled:true, label:"Spacer"}; __colors -> {kind:colors, enabled:true, label:"Colour swatches", raw:{type:"colors", symbol:"circle"}}; a module -> {kind:module, enabled:true, module:key, key:LABEL.toUpperCase(), label:LABEL.toUpperCase()}. Always appends to the END; no insert-at-position. This is an action menu wearing a Dropdown — it never displays a selection.

settings.label: Enabled
settings.group: <selected monitor name> (SettingSection title is dynamic: page.sel.name; literal fallback "DISPLAY" when no selection)
settings.def: hardware-derived: !mon.disabled from `ryoku-monitor list` (clone() sets disabled = mon.disabled === true)
settings.notes: INVERTED: the visible label is "Enabled", the disk key is `disabled`. onToggled: (v) => setField(selected, "disabled", !v). When disabled=true the Lua emitter drops every other field: `hl.monitor({ output = "X", disabled = true })` — mode/position/scale/transform/vrr/mirror are all discarded. A disabled monitor is also excluded from bbox()/normalize()/mirrorOptions().

settings.label: Resolution
options: DYNAMIC / HARDWARE-DERIVED — NOT a static enum. Options are built at runtime by page.modeOptions(mon) from mon.modes, which is `availableModes` from `hyprctl monitors all -j` via `ryoku-monitor list`.
Option key format: "<W>x<H>@<rate>" (rate kept as the raw decimal string captured by the regex, e.g. "2560x1440@165.00300")
Parsed by regex /^(\d+)x(\d+)@([\d.]+)/ — entries that do not match are dropped silently
Sorted: descending by pixel area (w*h), ties broken by descending refresh rate
Fallback current value when no advertised mode matches: "<mon.width>x<mon.height>@<mon.refresh>" — a synthetic key that is NOT in the options list, so the Dropdown renders its placeholder ("Select…")
Related non-UI mode keywords the helper writes but this page never offers: "highrr" (the catch-all/persist default) and "preferred" (used by mirror/extend)
settings.def: hardware-derived: page.pickCurrentMode(mon) — first option matching w+h+round(rate), else first matching w+h, else the synthetic "<w>x<h>@<refresh>" fallback
settings.notes: fieldWidth: 190. onChosen calls page.setMode(), which is the only control that writes FOUR draft keys at once: mode, width, height, refresh. width/height/refresh are derived (never sent to disk directly) but drive footW()/footH() and therefore the canvas tile size and the MonitorTile "W×H" sub-label.

settings.label: Scale
settings.def: hardware-derived: mon.scale from `ryoku-monitor list` (the LIVE accepted scale, which may already differ from what was requested)
settings.notes: decimals: 2. No unit suffix. NumberField clamps + quantises via clampq() on both stepper and manual text entry (DoubleValidator bottom=0.5 top=3 decimals=2); holding a stepper repeats every 90ms. HAZARD: Hyprland silently snaps scale to the nearest 1/120 multiple that yields whole logical pixels for the panel's w AND h (see snap() in ryoku-monitor), so the value shown here is a REQUEST, not the applied value — e.g. 1.5 on a 2560 panel becomes 1.6. The helper's own snap range is 120/120..360/120 (1.0–3.0), so the UI's 0.5 floor is below anything autoscale can produce. Also feeds the derived GDK_SCALE written into monitors.lua: floor(scale) only when every enabled monitor shares one identical whole scale >= 2, else 1.

settings.label: Rotation
settings.notes: Complete inline literal — exactly 4 options, no flipped/transform 4-7 variants exposed. UI keys are STRINGS ("0".."3"), disk value is an INT (parseInt on chosen, String() on read-back). Only written to Lua when != 0. Bit 0 drives the canvas footprint swap: footW/footH swap logical w/h when (transform & 1).

settings.label: Adaptive sync
settings.def: hardware-derived: coerced in clone() as (mon.vrr === true ? 1 : (mon.vrr | 0)) → "0" (Off)
settings.notes: Complete inline literal — exactly 3 options. UI keys are STRINGS, disk value is an INT (parseInt). Only written to Lua when != 0. Note the bool→int coercion in clone(): the helper's `list` passes hyprctl's vrr through raw, which can be a bool; write_monitors_conf (the persist path) can only ever emit `vrr = 1`, so a 2/Fullscreen choice survives Apply but is flattened to 1 by a later `persist`/`autoscale`.

settings.label: Mirror of
options: DYNAMIC: one option per OTHER monitor in the draft, key = label = that monitor's connector name (e.g. "DP-1", "HDMI-A-1")
Excluded from the list: the currently selected monitor itself (i === page.selected) and any monitor with disabled === true
settings.notes: fieldWidth: 190. Self-referential dynamic enum — the option list depends on page.selected and on other rows' Enabled state, so it must be recomputed whenever selection or any disabled flag changes. TRI-STATE SPELLING HAZARD: the helper's `list` emits "none", the draft/UI uses "", and the Lua emitter omits the key entirely — three spellings of the same state. apply_specs treats both "" and "none" as absent. Note the page does NOT read mirrorOptions() through `void page.tick` at the binding site (the function does it internally). Setting a mirror also flips the MonitorTile "MIRROR" badge.

settings.label: X
settings.configKey: position (serialised jointly as "<x>x<y>", e.g. "2560x0" — X and Y are NOT separate disk keys)
settings.notes: decimals: 0. Draft key `x`. PEER INPUT: the drag-arrange canvas writes this same value via dragMonitor()/endDrag(), and endDrag() then snaps + normalize()s it, so the field can change without the user touching it. Drag can push x negative transiently (the field's from:0 does not constrain dragMonitor); normalize() re-bases the whole layout so the minimum enabled x is 0. Catch-all/hotplug default in monitors.lua is position = "auto"; mirror/extend also write "auto" — this page can never produce "auto".

settings.label: Y
settings.configKey: position (serialised jointly as "<x>x<y>" — X and Y are NOT separate disk keys)
settings.notes: decimals: 0. Draft key `y`. Same peer-input/normalize()/negative-transient caveats as X.

settings.label: Profile name… (placeholder; the row has no visible label)
settings.notes: Bespoke inline control, NOT SettingField: a Rectangle (h 32, radius Theme.radius, ember border on focus) wrapping a bare TextInput (id nameField, selectByMouse, 12px side margins) with a manually-drawn placeholder Text "Profile name…" that shows only when text === "" && !activeFocus. Committed by Enter (onAccepted) or the adjacent Save HubButton. saveProfile() rejects a whitespace-only name (name.trim() === "" → silent return), trims, then clears the field. HAZARD: free text becomes a filename with no sanitising beyond trim — a name with "/" or ".." reaches `ryoku-monitor save NAME`.

settings.label: Save (profile)
settings.notes: icon: "check". Runs `ryoku-monitor save <name> <specsJSON>`, which applies the layout live AND writes the named profile AND rewrites monitors.lua. Also sets page.committed (clearing the dirty flag), clears nameField, and starts profileRefresh (300ms) to re-list profiles. ASYMMETRY: unlike Apply, save does NOT write monitors-applied.json.

settings.label: Mirror (quick action)
settings.group: (header quick-actions Row — outside any SettingSection, top-right, aligned to the "N displays detected" text)
settings.notes: icon: "display". enabled only when page.monCount > 1. Runs `ryoku-monitor mirror` (no arg → source is the focused monitor, else the first enabled one), which forces every OTHER enabled display to mode=preferred, position=auto, scale=1, mirror=<src>, then persists. BYPASSES THE DRAFT: hits hardware immediately and then listRefresh (700ms) reloads, silently discarding any unapplied edits. Listed here because it rewrites persisted config; also noted in nonSettingSurfaces.

settings.label: Extend (quick action)
settings.notes: icon: "display". enabled only when page.monCount > 1. Runs `ryoku-monitor extend`: clears mirroring on every enabled output (mode=preferred, position=auto, scale=1, mirror=none) then calls cmd_autoscale --no-profile, which RM's monitors-applied.json. Bypasses the draft; listRefresh (700ms) reloads after.

settings.label: DPI auto-scale (quick action)
settings.notes: icon: "refresh". enabled when page.monCount > 0. Runs `ryoku-monitor autoscale --no-profile` — the ONLY caller in this file that passes an argument to quick(). DESTRUCTIVE: --no-profile deletes monitors-applied.json so DPI buckets win at next login instead of the user's last Apply. DPI buckets (from the helper, not surfaced in this UI): <=120→1x, <=150→1.25, <=230→1.5, <=290→1.75, >290→2x; VM guests and Virtual-* connectors pinned to 1x; DPI <40 or >700 → 1x. Bypasses the draft; listRefresh (700ms) reloads after.

settings.label: Apply (action bar)
settings.notes: icon: "check", primary: true, enabled only when page.dirty. THE COMMIT POINT for every staged setting above — runs `ryoku-monitor apply <specsJSON>`, which applies live, rewrites monitors.lua with the chosen modes (not highrr), and snapshots {monitors:[...]} to monitors-applied.json so the layout is recalled for this hardware set at next login. HAZARD: sets page.committed optimistically BEFORE the Process finishes and never inspects exit status — a failed apply still flips the bar to "Layout matches your displays".

settings.label: Revert (action bar)
settings.notes: icon: "close", enabled only when page.dirty. Discards the whole draft by calling page.reload() (re-runs `ryoku-monitor list` + `profiles`). Touches no config. Note it also resets page.selected to 0 if the selection index is now out of range.

settings.label: Layout
options: <dynamic: every xkb layout code from `ryoku-hub hypr layouts`, i.e. the "! layout" block of /usr/share/X11/xkb/rules/base.lst, falling back to evdev.lst; option = {key: code, label: name, hint: code}>
settings.notes: Edits only the FIRST comma-slot of the composed kbLayout string via page.setLayouts(k, secondaryLayout()). fieldWidth 240; placeholder is the raw current code (shown until the async model lands). Choosing a layout also rewrites kbVariant to keep the positional comma form aligned. Once input has ever been saved the Go backend pins kb_layout/kb_variant/kb_options unconditionally (o.inputSaved) so keyboard.lua, which loads earlier, cannot win.

settings.label: Style
options: <dynamic: every xkb variant code for the CURRENT primary layout from `ryoku-hub hypr variants <layout>`; label is regex-derived by variantLabel(): "French (AZERTY)" -> "AZERTY", hint = code>
settings.notes: This is the xkb VARIANT, relabelled 'Style'. Applies to the primary layout only; setVariant() writes "<v>," when a second layout exists so xkb aligns variants to layouts positionally. Model refetched by refreshVariants() on kbLayout change, guarded by variantsProc.forLayout so the same layout never refetches. placeholder "Default".

settings.label: Second layout
settings.notes: Edits the SECOND comma-slot of the same kbLayout string ("fr,us"). Not an independent key — shares configKey with 'Layout'. Selecting a value reveals the 'Switch layouts' row; clearing it back to None leaves any grp: token behind in kbOptions. placeholder "None".

settings.label: Switch layouts
settings.notes: visible ONLY when page.secondaryLayout().length > 0. Not a standalone key: it owns the grp: family inside the single comma-separated kbOptions string via setOption(grpIds, k). CONTRACT TRAP: page.grpIds is ["grp:alt_shift_toggle", "grp:win_space_toggle", "grp:caps_toggle"] — grp:caps_toggle is a recognised family member (so it is stripped out of the 'Extra options' free-text field) but is NOT offered as a choice here, so it is invisible in the UI and silently dropped the moment this picker is touched.

settings.label: Caps Lock
settings.notes: Owns the caps family (page.capsIds) inside the shared kbOptions string. current = pickFrom(capsIds) — the first recognised token wins if several are present.

settings.label: Swap Alt and Super
settings.notes: Set-membership toggle over the single token page.swapId = "altwin:swap_alt_win" inside kbOptions. checked = pickFrom([swapId]) === swapId; toggling calls setOption([swapId], v ? swapId : "").

settings.label: Compose key
settings.notes: Owns the compose family (page.composeIds) inside the shared kbOptions string.

settings.label: Extra options
settings.notes: Inline `component TextFieldRow` defined at InputPage.qml:194 (label left, 240px TextInput right, placeholder "raw xkb options, comma-separated…", 320x38 implicit). Escape hatch: shows every kbOptions token NOT in knownIds() (caps + compose + grp + altwin families) via extraOptions(); setExtra() re-merges known tokens with the typed ones. Commits on editingFinished (NOT per keystroke) and re-binds text to the draft on focus loss so Reset/Revert refresh the shown text after a manual edit. Placeholder only renders when empty AND unfocused; focus selects all.

settings.label: Apply system-wide
settings.notes: icon "check". enabled: !store.dirty && !sysApplyProc.running — must Save first. Privileged (polkit prompt). Passes the composed comma layout string and an empty model slot. localectl also converts the X11 keymap to the nearest console keymap, so one call covers both the SDDM greeter and the TTY. Paired readout below.

settings.label: Login screen and TTY keymap status
options: idle -> "Login screen and TTY keep their own keymap" (Theme.faint)
ok -> "Applied to login screen and console" (Theme.ok)
settings.notes: Transient state page.sysApplyState, set from sysApplyProc exit code, auto-cleared to idle after 6000ms by the sysApplyClear Timer. Elided 12px text sharing the row with the Apply button.

settings.label: Sensitivity
settings.notes: decimals: 2 (readout via toFixed(2), percent false).

settings.label: Follow mouse
settings.notes: Stored as int; ChoiceRow keys are strings, so current = String(store.followMouse) and the write is parseInt(k). Hyprland also defines 3 (full-loose) — not exposed.

settings.label: Natural scroll
settings.notes: Label collides verbatim with the TOUCHPAD 'Natural scroll' row — distinct key (mouse-level input.natural_scroll vs input.touchpad.natural_scroll). Do not dedupe.

settings.label: Scroll speed
settings.notes: decimals: 1. Label collides verbatim with the TOUCHPAD 'Scroll speed' row — distinct key. Do not dedupe.

settings.label: Middle-click pastes
settings.notes: Lives in the input JSON domain but is emitted into the Lua `misc` section as misc.middle_click_paste, not `input`.

settings.label: Disable while typing
settings.notes: UI order differs from the store/Go field order — this row sits 4th in TOUCHPAD, between 'Tap and drag' and 'Click by finger count'.

settings.label: Scroll speed
settings.notes: decimals: 1.

settings.label: Swipe between workspaces
settings.notes: GATE: the next four rows are `visible: store.workspaceSwipe`. Has NO config leaf of its own — false emits nothing, true emits a whole `hl.gesture({ fingers = N, direction = "horizontal", action = "workspace" })` line (genGesture, hypr.go:1307). The base config ships no gesture, so off = absent.

settings.label: Swipe fingers
settings.notes: visible: store.workspaceSwipe. Stored int, ChoiceRow keys are strings: current = String(store.swipeFingers), write is parseInt(k, 10). Not a config leaf — it is the `fingers` argument of the generated hl.gesture line, and the backend floors it at 3.

settings.label: Natural swipe direction
settings.notes: visible: store.workspaceSwipe. Default true, so this toggle ships ON — the shipped baseline is inverted/natural.

settings.label: Swipe past the last workspace to add one
settings.notes: visible: store.workspaceSwipe. Longest label on the page — it elides at the 460px cap; keep the row width.

settings.label: Swipe distance
settings.notes: visible: store.workspaceSwipe. decimals: 0. Unitless in the UI (px in Hyprland).

settings.label: Repeat rate
settings.notes: step not set in InputPage -> NumberField default step 1, decimals default 0. Steppers repeat every 90ms while held; the field also takes manual entry clamped to from/to on editingFinished.

settings.label: Repeat delay
settings.notes: decimals default 0.

settings.label: Enable frame
settings.notes: Shell keeps frameBorder intact when off; shell substitutes effectiveFrameBorder=50.

settings.label: Border thickness
settings.notes: step/decimals not set on the row; NumberField defaults step:1 decimals:0.

settings.label: Opacity
settings.notes: percent:true renders the readout as a percentage; stored value is the 0.2-1 real.

settings.label: Inner roundness
settings.notes: Shell-wide inner corner radius.

settings.label: Edge melt
settings.notes: Only SliderRow on the page that is NOT percent; decimals explicitly 0 (SliderRow default is 2). Integer-valued slider.

settings.label: Name
settings.notes: placeholder "Ryoku", fieldWidth 200. Commits on Enter or focus loss (committed signal), not per-keystroke.

settings.label: Text mark
settings.notes: placeholder "力", fieldWidth 200. Swatch falls back to 力 when the field is empty, but the empty string is still what is persisted.

settings.label: (logo image path — no label; row of readout + Choose image + Clear)
settings.notes: Inline Row, not a SettingField. Elided path Text reads "No image (using the text mark)" when empty, else the raw value. HubButton "Choose image" (icon image) opens the FileDialog logoPicker (title "Choose a logo image", nameFilters ["Images (*.svg *.png *.jpg *.jpeg *.webp)", "All files (*)"]) and writes String(selectedFile) — a file: URL. HubButton "Clear" (icon close) is enabled only when markImage !== "" and writes "". Preview accepts both file:-prefixed and bare paths.

settings.label: Tint image to accent
settings.notes: Drives the swatch: ColorOverlay(Theme.brand) over the Image when true, raw Image when false.

settings.label: Colour
settings.notes: ColorField ships live swatch + manual hex field + an 8-preset strip hardcoded IN the component (not the page): #1a1b26, #16161e, #1b1612, #1e1e2e, #181825, #11111b, #0f1115, #232136. QML color type; serialised as a string in JSON. Frame, pill and island all read this one colour.

settings.label: Font
options: JetBrainsMono Nerd Font (JetBrains Mono, hint nerd)
SauceCodePro Nerd Font (Source Code Pro, nerd)
BlexMono Nerd Font (IBM Plex Mono, nerd)
DejaVuSansMono Nerd Font (DejaVu Sans Mono, nerd)
Noto Sans CJK JP (Noto Sans JP, cjk)
<current custom family> (synthesised, hint custom, unshifted to front when the saved family is not in the catalog)
settings.notes: fieldWidth 200. NOT a static enum: fontOptions = fontCatalog (25 entries above) filtered to page.installedFonts, with the current selection always kept. installedFonts is seeded to [JetBrainsMono Nerd Font, Inter, Noto Sans, Noto Sans CJK JP, Fraunces, Space Grotesk] and then REPLACED asynchronously by an fc-list scan, so the visible option set changes after the page opens. hint renders dim/right-aligned in the list.

settings.label: Location
settings.notes: placeholder "Auto (from IP)", fieldWidth 200. Empty = auto.

settings.label: Style
settings.notes: fieldWidth 170. All 10 options carry a hint string. Note the label case: "iNiR" for key inir. barStyle === "delos" is what gates the ISLAND section's visibility.

settings.label: Roundness
settings.notes: CONDITIONAL: whole ISLAND SettingSection has visible: draft.barStyle === "delos". Invisible on the other 9 bar styles but still written/reset.

settings.label: Workspaces
settings.notes: Set-membership via toggleIslandModule("workspaces", v). Gated on barStyle === "delos". Enable APPENDS to the end of the ordered list; disable splices out.

settings.label: Clock
settings.notes: toggleIslandModule("clock", v). Gated on barStyle === "delos".

settings.label: Date
settings.notes: toggleIslandModule("date", v). Gated on barStyle === "delos".

settings.label: Now playing
settings.notes: toggleIslandModule("media", v). Label "Now playing" but key "media". Gated on barStyle === "delos".

settings.label: Window title
settings.notes: toggleIslandModule("title", v). Gated on barStyle === "delos".

settings.label: Status glyphs
settings.notes: toggleIslandModule("status", v). Gated on barStyle === "delos".

settings.label: Tray
settings.notes: toggleIslandModule("tray", v). Gated on barStyle === "delos".

settings.label: (no visible label) island edge
options: top (default; full option set not enumerated on this page — see shell pill DelosIsland/IslandDock)
settings.notes: HAS NO UI ON THIS PAGE but is in shellKeys — so it is dirty-tracked, written by every flush(), restored by Revert, and RESET by "Reset to defaults". Written by the shell itself when you drag the Delos island (ryoku/shell/quickshell/pill/DelosIsland.qml + Singletons/IslandDock.qml). A rewrite must keep it round-tripping.

settings.label: (no visible label) island position along edge
settings.notes: HAS NO UI ON THIS PAGE but is in shellKeys (flushed/reverted/reset). -1 = unset/centred sentinel. Persisted by the shell's island drag.

settings.label: (no visible label) island hidden
settings.notes: HAS NO UI ON THIS PAGE but is in shellKeys (flushed/reverted/reset). Toggled by the shell (Super+Esc opens it per the shell Config comment).

settings.label: Stash
settings.notes: toggleSidebarPane("sidebarLeftPanes", "stash", v). Only member offered for the left side today — the list is ordered and enable appends to the end (pane tab order).

settings.label: Open on hover
settings.notes: Label says "Open on hover", key says "clickless" — true = hover, false = click.

settings.label: Notifications
settings.notes: toggleSidebarPane("sidebarRightPanes", "notifications", v). Ordered list; enable appends to end.

settings.label: Calendar
settings.notes: toggleSidebarPane("sidebarRightPanes", "calendar", v).

settings.label: Media
settings.notes: toggleSidebarPane("sidebarRightPanes", "media", v).

settings.label: Weather
settings.notes: toggleSidebarPane("sidebarRightPanes", "weather", v).

settings.label: Recording
settings.notes: toggleSidebarPane("sidebarRightPanes", "recording", v). Full default right list order: notifications, calendar, media, weather, recording.

settings.label: Style
settings.notes: Note key/label mismatch: key "line" is labelled "Monitor". 7 options in one full-width ChoiceRow. VizPreview falls back to "bars" for unknown styles.

settings.label: Position
settings.notes: Key is US "center", label is UK "Centre".

settings.label: Enabled
settings.notes: Bare key name "enabled" — collides conceptually with the shell's frameEnabled/barEnabled but lives in visualizer.json. Also drives the preview badge LIVE PREVIEW/OFF. This is the key the shell deck flips externally (see adoptExternal).

settings.label: Bars
settings.notes: Only NumberField on the page with a non-1 step (4). No unit. Preview clamps to 2..96 for its own render, which does not match the 16..128 range.

settings.label: Segments
settings.notes: No unit. Only meaningful for style "segments" but is always shown/editable.

settings.label: Height
settings.notes: Bare key "height"; bound to the preview as heightFrac. Fraction of screen height.

settings.label: Bar width
settings.notes: Label "Bar width" vs key "thickness".

settings.label: Reflection
settings.notes: max 0.3 — the percent readout therefore tops out at 30%, not 100%. Preview only reflects for style bars + position bottom.

settings.label: Smoothing
settings.notes: Runtime-only: not reflected in VizPreview.

settings.label: Sensitivity
settings.notes: Label "Sensitivity" vs key "gain". percent:true on a 0.5-2 range means the readout reads 50%-200%. Runtime-only: not in VizPreview.

settings.label: Frame rate
settings.notes: TYPE COERCION: stored as int, but ChoiceRow keys are strings — current: String(draft.fps), onChosen: parseInt(k). A declarative binding must preserve both directions. Runtime-only: not in VizPreview.

settings.label: Adaptive quality
settings.notes: Runtime-only governor: not reflected in VizPreview.

settings.label: Idle wave
settings.notes: Runtime-only: not reflected in VizPreview.

settings.label: My rices / Browse (mode switch)
settings.notes: RicesPage.qml:193-200. THE ONLY tab-like control in the file. model literal is [{key:"mine",label:"My rices"},{key:"store",label:"Browse"}]. current is derived, not stored: `page.browseMode ? "store" : "mine"`. onSelected calls showBrowse(k==="store"). NOT persisted — ephemeral page.browseMode (default false = mine). SIDE EFFECT: showBrowse(true) lazily fires loadCatalog() only when catalog.length===0 && !catalogLoading, so the store fetch happens on first entry only. Segmented.qml hardcodes segW 86, height 34.

settings.label: Name this rice (for example, My Setup)
settings.configKey: name (→ also derives "slug" via backend slugify)
settings.notes: RicesPage.qml:245-263. Bespoke inline TextInput (NOT SettingField) inside a 46px Rectangle at :227-283. The quoted string is placeholder Text shown only when text==="" (Theme.faint). selectByMouse:true, clip:true, focus bound to page.capturing (grabs keyboard when the wizard opens). Commits two ways: onAccepted (Enter) and the Save button, both → page.capture(text) → `ryoku-hub rice capture <name> all`. capture() early-returns on falsy name; backend also rejects with "a rice needs a name" after slugify. Border animates Theme.line→Theme.ember on activeFocus (ColorAnimation, Theme.quick).

settings.label: Save current setup
settings.notes: RicesPage.qml:210-217. primary:true, icon "plus", enabled: !page.capturing. tooltip (exact): "Snapshot your whole desktop look as a new rice you can re-apply later." Does NOT capture — only sets page.capturing = true, which reveals the inline name wizard and hides the blurb Text at :285.

settings.label: Restore original
settings.notes: RicesPage.qml:218-224. icon "refresh", enabled: page.hasActive (computed at :33-38 by scanning rices for .active). tooltip (exact): "Revert every part of the desktop to how it was before you applied a rice." Runs `ryoku-hub rice restore baseline` (hardcoded slot). Backend restores backupStores = hypr.json, shell.json, launcher.json, theme.json plus wallpaper.path. onExited → page.reload(). No confirmation.

settings.label: Save (commit the capture)
settings.notes: RicesPage.qml:265-272, id saveNow. primary:true, icon "check". → page.capture(nameInput.text) → `ryoku-hub rice capture <name> all`. The literal "all" is HARDCODED at :79 and expands backend-side to every riceHyprLayers key (input, windowRules, layerRules, appOverrides, keybinds, autostart, env). onExited → capturing=false + reload(). Backend snapshots look (hypr appearance/cursor/anim/plugins, ~30 shell keys, launcher hero keys), copies wall.<ext>, hero.<ext>, palette.json.

settings.label: Cancel (abandon the capture)
settings.notes: RicesPage.qml:273-281, id cancelNow. No icon. Sets page.capturing = false AND clears nameInput.text — the field does not survive a cancel.

settings.label: Try again (reload the store catalog)
settings.notes: RicesPage.qml:381-386. icon "refresh". Lives inside the Browse empty/error state Column (:366) so it is visible ONLY when !catalogLoading && catalog.length===0 — i.e. it is the sole recovery path from a failed `ryoku-hub rice catalog`. → page.loadCatalog().

settings.label: Rice tile (My rices grid)
settings.notes: RicesPage.qml:332-340. Repeater over page.rices (from `ryoku-hub rice list` stdout JSON) → RiceTile delegate. width: Math.max(280, (grid.width - 14*2)/3) — hardcoded 3-up. onOpened → page.selectedSlug = modelData.slug (drills into the detail; NOT an apply).

settings.label: Rice tile (Browse / store grid)
settings.notes: RicesPage.qml:394-410. Repeater over page.catalog (from `ryoku-hub rice catalog`) → RiceTile with store:true. width: Math.max(280, (storeGrid.width - 14*2)/3). onOpened is OVERLOADED: if modelData.installed → browseMode=false + selectedSlug=modelData.id (navigate); else → page.install(modelData.id) → `ryoku-hub rice install <id>`. Note it keys off `.id` here vs `.slug` in the My-rices grid. installProc.onExited → reload() + loadCatalog().

settings.label: Apply this rice / Applied
settings.notes: Wired at RicesPage.qml:428 (onApplied), button in RiceDetail.qml:255-261. Label FLIPS on state: rice.active ? "Applied" : "Apply this rice". primary:true, icon "check", enabled: !rice.active. Emits detail.applied(detail.layerKeys) → page.applyRice(selectedSlug, layers) → `ryoku-hub rice apply <slug> <layer...>`. applyProc.onExited clears selectedSlug (bounces you back to the grid) + reload().

settings.label: Duplicate
settings.notes: Wired at RicesPage.qml:429 (onForked), button in RiceDetail.qml:262-266. icon "plus". → `ryoku-hub rice fork <slug>`. onExited clears selectedSlug + reload().

settings.label: Set wallpaper
settings.notes: Wired at RicesPage.qml:431 (onWallpaperRequested → wallPicker.open()), button in RiceDetail.qml:267-271 (icon "image"). Picker instance at :438-445 uses ImagePicker DEFAULTS: title "Choose a backdrop", startFolder file://$HOME/Pictures, foldersOnly false, nameFilters [*.png,*.jpg,*.jpeg,*.bmp]. onPicked strips the scheme with ('' + p).replace('file://','') then page.setwall(path) → `ryoku-hub rice setwall <slug> <path>`; setwall no-ops if path falsy or selectedSlug==="". Picker self-closes (active=false) on both picked and canceled. setwallProc.onExited → reload().

settings.label: View config
settings.notes: Wired at RicesPage.qml:432 (onViewConfigRequested → configViewer.open(page.configText)), button in RiceDetail.qml:272-276 (icon "search"). Opens the ConfigViewer modal (:459) on the manifest JSON already fetched by loadFiles() (`ryoku-hub rice files <slug>` → .config). Read-only, selectable mono text, scrim-dismiss.

settings.label: Export
settings.notes: Wired at RicesPage.qml:433 (onExportRequested → exportPicker.open()), button in RiceDetail.qml:277-281 (icon "download"). Picker at :447-457 OVERRIDES the defaults: foldersOnly:true, title "Export to a folder", startFolder "file://" + home. NOTE: `home` is NOT a RicesPage property — it resolves to ImagePicker's own `readonly property string home` (scope leak); inlining the picker in a rewrite silently breaks the start folder. foldersOnly works by setting nameFilters to the sentinel ["ryoku-no-match"]. onPicked → page.exportTo(path) → `ryoku-hub rice export <slug> <dest>`; result path is parsed from stdout into page.exportedTo.

settings.label: Delete
settings.notes: Wired at RicesPage.qml:430 (onRemoved), button in RiceDetail.qml:282-286. icon "trash". → `ryoku-hub rice delete <slug>`. NO CONFIRMATION DIALOG and no undo — one tap destroys the rice. Not styled as destructive (no Theme.bad), sits last in the same Flow as Duplicate/Export. onExited clears selectedSlug + reload().

settings.label: Show in files
settings.notes: Wired at RicesPage.qml:434 (onRevealRequested), control in RiceDetail.qml:308-331. Bespoke 30px Rectangle + Icon "folder" + Text + HoverHandler/TapHandler (NOT a HubButton). Visible only when exportedTo !== "". → page.reveal(path) → `xdg-open <path>` (revealProc, :185).

settings.label: Back (to the grid)
settings.notes: Wired at RicesPage.qml:427 (onBack → selectedSlug = ""), control in RiceDetail.qml:82-100. Bespoke 34px Rectangle with a chevron Icon rotated 90°, hover-tinted, HoverHandler + TapHandler. The only way out of the detail besides an apply/fork/delete bounce.

settings.label: ALSO SETS (behavior layers carried by the rice)
settings.notes: RiceDetail.qml:231-250, layerKeys computed at :26 as Object.keys(rice.layers). READ-ONLY TODAY: rendered as a joined comma string (layerKeys.join(", ")), with NO per-layer toggle — yet the whole plumbing supports selection (applied(layerKeys) → applyRice(slug, layers) → `rice apply <slug> <layer...>`, and the backend allowlists per key). Option keys are the backend's riceHyprLayers (rice.go:75). The Column is visible only when layerKeys.length > 0. A rewrite must NOT invent per-layer checkboxes (behavior change) nor drop the layer argv (silently stops applying behavior).

settings.label: WHAT IT TOUCHES (files this rice writes)
options: Colour palette (wallust cache) — fixed-palette rices only
settings.notes: RiceDetail.qml:179-227. Repeater over detail.touches (page.touches, fetched by loadFiles()). Each row: Icon tinted by kind (asset→Theme.brand, output→Theme.faint, config→Theme.ember), label, and path suffixed with "  (unchanged)" when !provided; whole row drops to opacity 0.45 when !provided. Full row set enumerated from riceTouches() in backend/rice.go:606-649.

settings.label: Changes … (summary line)
