settings.notes: RiceDetail.qml:56-73 changeSummary() + :111-117. Joins present parts with a double-space-middot-double-space ("  ·  ") and prefixes "Changes ". Each part is conditional: look.hypr non-empty→windows, look.shell non-empty→shell + bar, rice.color→colours, assets.wallpaper→wallpaper, assets.cursor→cursor, assets.hero→launcher art. Mono font, 11px, letterSpacing 0.5.

settings.label: Custom layer rules (the list itself)
settings.notes: NOT a fixed setting: an unbounded ORDERED array of {namespace, action, value} objects, edited via Add rule / per-row delete / Clear all. Order is load-bearing: index i generates the Lua rule name `ryoku-layer-<i+1>` (hypr.go genLayerRule line 1106). New rule seeded by addRule(): {"namespace": "", "action": "blur", "value": ""}. Whole list round-trips through HyprStore.snapshot()/adopt() as part of ONE whole-document save. Applied on Save only (editList never calls queuePreview).

settings.label: Namespace (per-rule field 1; placeholder "Namespace")
settings.notes: Bespoke inline control, NOT SettingField: a bare TextInput inside a Rectangle (width lrow.nsW, height 30, Theme.mono 13px, selectByMouse, ember border on activeFocus). Free text, no validation, no completion. Placeholder Text child shows "Namespace" only when text=="" && !activeFocus. Commits via onEditingFinished (blur/Enter) ONLY, and only if text changed -> page.patch(index, "namespace", text). Matches the layer-shell namespace (e.g. launcher, overview, bar). Empty/whitespace namespace => genLayerRule returns "" and the rule silently generates nothing, but IS still stored in hypr.json.

settings.label: Action (per-rule field 2)
settings.notes: ALL 7 options, in page.actionOptions declaration order (key -> visible label -> emitted Lua prop, hypr.go genLayerRule): blur -> "Blur" -> `blur = true`; blurpopups -> "Blur popups" -> `blur_popups = true`; ignorealpha -> "Ignore alpha" -> `ignore_alpha = <float>`; noanim -> "No animations" -> `no_anim = true`; dimaround -> "Dim around" -> `dim_around = true` (plain bool; the dim AMOUNT is decoration:dim_around, not settable here); xray -> "Blur X-ray" -> `xray = true`; abovelock -> "Show above lockscreen" -> `above_lock = true`. Dropdown is width/fieldWidth 160, height 30, no label. Choosing routes to setAction(index, key) — NOT patch() — which ALSO rewrites .value as a side effect (see value row). Legacy key "noshadow" is still accepted by the backend but deliberately absent here and dropped at generation.

settings.label: Value (per-rule field 3; placeholder is the action's valueHint, e.g. "0.0 - 1.0")
settings.def: "0.5" when action is switched to ignorealpha; "" for every other action (setAction clears it)
settings.min: 0.0 (hint text only — NOT enforced in QML)
settings.max: 1.0 (hint text only — NOT enforced in QML)
settings.notes: CONDITIONAL FIELD, easy to miss: visible only when needsValue, i.e. action is in page.valueActions === ["ignorealpha"] (a one-member list today — dimaround USED to carry a value and no longer does, per the line-23 comment). Bespoke inline TextInput (width lrow.valW 110, height 30, Theme.mono 13px, selectByMouse, ember border on activeFocus), NOT a NumberField/SliderRow — so the 0.0-1.0 range is a placeholder STRING (lrow.valueHint), never clamped or validated client-side. Stored as a STRING even though it is numeric. Commits via onEditingFinished only -> page.patch(index, "value", text). Backend coerces with parseFloat(r.Value, 0.5), so junk text silently falls back to 0.5.

settings.label: Corner radius
settings.notes: store.edit("rounding") -> live preview via `ryoku-hub hypr preview` (hyprctl eval), throttled 80ms; persisted on Save. NumberField step/decimals default to 1/0.

settings.label: Corner softness
settings.notes: decimals: 1.

settings.label: Border thickness
settings.notes: Borders tab's ANIMATED BORDER help text says the rotating gradient needs this above 0 — an undeclared cross-tab dependency.

settings.label: Tiling layout
settings.notes: Gates the two hyprscrolling rows below it.

settings.label: Column width
settings.notes: visible only when appearance.layout === "scrolling". Routed through store.editPlugin -> NOT live-previewed, lands on Save. hyprscrolling has no `enabled` key: it follows the layout choice. Sits in SHAPE next to live rows but behaves differently.

settings.label: Scroll to follow focus
settings.notes: visible only when appearance.layout === "scrolling"; editPlugin path, applies on Save.

settings.label: Drag to resize at window edges
Window title bars
settings.notes: Gates the 4 rows below + the section's help text ("Adds a title bar with window buttons. Applies on Save.").

settings.label: Bar height
settings.notes: visible only when plugins.hyprbars.enabled.

settings.label: Title text size
settings.notes: visible only when plugins.hyprbars.enabled.

settings.label: Blur the bar
settings.notes: visible only when plugins.hyprbars.enabled.

settings.label: Close and maximise buttons
settings.notes: visible only when plugins.hyprbars.enabled.

settings.label: Active
settings.notes: Right-hand column of the Look tab.

settings.label: Dim inactive windows
settings.notes: Gates "Dim strength".

settings.label: Dim strength
settings.notes: visible only when appearance.dimInactive.

settings.label: Enabled
settings.notes: Does NOT gate the other BLUR rows — Size/Passes/X-ray/Vibrancy/Noise stay visible when off. Preserve that.

settings.label: Size
settings.notes: Always visible (not gated on blurEnabled).

settings.label: Passes
settings.notes: No unit. Always visible.

settings.label: Vibrancy
settings.notes: decimals: 2.

settings.label: Noise
settings.notes: decimals: 3 — the only 3-decimal readout on the page (SliderRow's readout box is a fixed 42px).

settings.label: Window shadows
settings.notes: Does NOT gate Shadow range / Shadow sharpness.

settings.label: Shadow range
settings.notes: Always visible (not gated on shadowEnabled).

settings.label: Shadow sharpness
settings.notes: No unit. Always visible.

settings.label: Animations
settings.notes: Gates "Wobbly windows" and "Open / close".

settings.label: Wobbly windows
settings.notes: visible only when appearance.animations.

settings.label: Open / close
settings.notes: visible only when appearance.animations. Note key "gnomed" vs label "Gnome" — the key is not derivable from the label.

settings.label: Glow behind windows
settings.notes: Gates Range + Colour.

settings.label: Range
settings.notes: visible only when appearance.glowEnabled. Note min is 4, not 0.

settings.label: Colour
settings.notes: visible only when appearance.glowEnabled. ColorField = swatch + hex TextInput (validator /#?[0-9A-Fa-f]{6}/, 6-digit only, no alpha) + a strip of 8 component-hardcoded presets: #1a1b26, #16161e, #1b1612, #1e1e2e, #181825, #11111b, #0f1115, #232136.

settings.label: Liquid glass windows
settings.notes: Gates the 3 rows below + help text ("A liquid-glass blur and refraction on windows. Applies on Save.").

settings.label: Preset
settings.notes: visible only when plugins.hyprglass.enabled. Key "high_contrast" vs label "Contrast" — not derivable.

settings.label: Blur strength
settings.notes: visible only when plugins.hyprglass.enabled; decimals: 1.

settings.label: Glass opacity
settings.notes: visible only when plugins.hyprglass.enabled.

settings.label: Active window
settings.notes: WHOLE SECTION visible only when page.scheme !== "follow" — and scheme is set on the WALLPAPER tab (theme.json), not in this store. Unreachable in the default (follow) state.

settings.label: Inactive window
settings.notes: Same cross-tab gate on page.scheme !== "follow".

settings.label: Rotating gradient border
settings.notes: Gates "Rotation speed". Section help text (always visible) warns it needs Border thickness > 0 (a Look-tab setting).

settings.label: Rotation speed
settings.notes: visible only when appearance.animatedBorder; decimals: 0 (integer-looking but stored real/float64).

settings.label: Image border around windows
settings.notes: Gates the image chooser row, scale, smooth + help text ("Tiles an image around each window as its border. Pick an image, then Save.").

settings.label: Choose image (path readout + button)
settings.notes: Bespoke Row, NOT a standard setting row: an ElideMiddle Text showing the path (or "No image chosen" in Theme.faint when empty) + a HubButton{label:"Choose image", icon:"image"} that opens the page-level ImagePicker. visible only when plugins.imgborders.enabled. ImagePicker.onPicked -> store.editPlugin("imgborders","image",p) then active=false; onCanceled -> active=false.

settings.label: Border scale
settings.notes: visible only when plugins.imgborders.enabled; decimals: 1.

settings.label: Smooth scaling
settings.notes: visible only when plugins.imgborders.enabled.

settings.label: Theme
options: DYNAMIC — options come from `ryoku-hub hypr cursors` at runtime (installed icon sets scanned under ~/.icons and $XDG_DATA_HOME/icons for a cursors/ dir); NOT statically enumerable
settings.notes: Store prop is cursorTheme but the JSON key is cursor.theme (HyprStore.cursorProp() maps cursor<Key>). fieldWidth: 240, width Math.min(parent.width,460), placeholder: store.cursorTheme so an uninstalled/unlisted theme still renders. Applies to the running session at once and to apps opened next (persisted as env in settings.lua).

settings.label: Size
settings.notes: step: 4 — the only non-1 NumberField step on the page.

settings.label: Hide after idle
settings.notes: Help text below: "0 seconds keeps the cursor always visible." (0 = sentinel, not just a minimum).

settings.label: Realistic cursor motion
settings.notes: Gates Style + Shake + help text ("...Applies on Save.").

settings.label: Style
settings.notes: visible only when plugins.dynamicCursors.enabled. Default is "tilt" (the middle option), not the first.

settings.label: Shake to find (magnify)
settings.notes: visible only when plugins.dynamicCursors.enabled. The related plugins.dynamicCursors.magnify (default 4) has NO control on this page but is still written.

settings.label: Colours
options: custom — BACKEND-ONLY 5th state: currentScheme() can return it (a theme owns a fixed palette) and it has its own help text, but applyScheme() rejects it, so it is NOT selectable and the ChoiceRow shows no active segment
settings.source: ~/.config/ryoku/theme.json (writes {followWallpaper, scheme}); side effects: ~/.cache/wallust/colors.json, ~/.cache/wallust/hypr-colors.lua, ~/.config/kitty/current-theme.conf, regen of settings.lua, hyprctl reload, pkill -USR1 kitty
settings.notes: NOT part of the HyprStore draft: page.setScheme() runs `ryoku-hub hypr scheme <k>` immediately — no Save/Revert, does not mark dirty, and this tab has no action bar. follow => FollowWallpaper=true + `ryoku-shell wallpaper repaint`; light/dark => locks a curated embedded palette (schemes/light.json, schemes/dark.json). Controls whether the Borders tab's FIXED COLOURS section exists at all. Help text varies across all 4 states.

settings.label: Wallpaper (image grid selection)
settings.configKey: (no key — a path in a state file)
settings.notes: Flow + Repeater over page.wallpapers, delegate = 172x104 Rectangle thumbnail (Image PreserveAspectCrop, sourceSize 360x220, async, cache:false), ember 2px border + Theme.ember when active, hover scale 1.03, TapHandler -> page.applyWall(). Model is shelled out: `find ~/Pictures/Wallpapers -maxdepth 1 -type f \( -iname *.jpg -o *.jpeg -o *.png -o *.webp \)` sorted by mtime desc — an unbounded runtime list, not an enum. Applies at once (retheme via wallust); no Save. Empty-state text: "No wallpapers in ~/Pictures/Wallpapers."

settings.label: Brightness
settings.notes: Slider is 0.05..1 but binds `page.brightness < 0 ? 1 : page.brightness/100`, so it shows a false 100% until brightnessctl returns and can display values below its own `from`. onModified -> setBrightness(Math.round(v*100)). No Save; applies at once. Failure -> page.comfortError = "Couldn't set brightness." (auto-clears after 6s).

settings.label: Warm the screen
settings.source: none — `~/.config/hypr/scripts/ryoku-cmd-nightlight on <temp>` / `off`; state read via `... status` ("on <temp>" | "off"), which is really `pgrep -x hyprsunset`
settings.notes: The on/off value IS a running process, not config. Applies at once, no Save. Failure -> page.comfortError = "Couldn't change the night light." (6s auto-clear). Help text claims it "Stays on until you turn it off, across sessions."

settings.label: Temperature
settings.configKey: (no key — a bare number in a state file)
settings.unit: K (implied — NOT displayed; readout is a bare number, decimals: 0)
settings.source: ~/.local/state/ryoku-nightlight — written only as a side effect of the script's `start`, i.e. only when the light is turned on
settings.notes: Always visible (not gated on the toggle) but a NO-OP while off: setNightTemp() only restarts the 300ms nightDebounce `if (page.nightOn)`. Default 4000 is duplicated in QML (property nightTemp: 4000) and in the script (default_temp=4000). `status` only reports the temp while hyprsunset runs, so a temp chosen while off is lost on reload.

settings.label: Environment variables (the list itself)
settings.def: [] (empty — Go defaultOverrides() sets Env: []EnvVar{}; HyprStore draft property env: [])
settings.source: ~/.config/ryoku/hypr.json (editable truth, JSON key "env", array of {key,value}) → rendered to ~/.config/hypr/settings.lua as one `hl.env("NAME", "value")` line per entry
settings.notes: No fixed schema: an ordered, user-authored array of NAME=value pairs with no enum of allowed keys and no per-key defaults. Rendered by a Repeater over store.env with an inline Rectangle delegate (48px tall, Theme.surface, 1px Theme.line border, Theme.radius). Mutated only via page.add() / page.patch(i,key,val) / page.remove(i), each of which hands store.editList("env", freshSlice) a NEW array (slice + Object.assign copy) rather than mutating in place. editList does NOT preview — list changes land on Save only. Backend skips any entry whose key is blank after TrimSpace, so blank rows are never written to settings.lua even though they mark the store dirty. Duplicate NAMEs are allowed; the last hl.env wins. Emitted after the cursor's own hl.env(XCURSOR_THEME/XCURSOR_SIZE/HYPRCURSOR_THEME/HYPRCURSOR_SIZE) block, so a user entry with those names overrides the Cursor domain's.

settings.label: NAME (per-row variable name)
settings.notes: Bespoke inline control, NOT a SettingField: a TextInput inside a Rectangle (width = 42% of the fields area via rowRect.keyW = round(fieldsW * 0.42), height 32, Theme.surfaceLo, Theme.radius). Theme.mono font, pixelSize 13, selectByMouse, clip. Placeholder Text child reading "NAME" in Theme.faint, visible only when text === "" && !activeFocus. Border animates Theme.line → Theme.ember on activeFocus (ColorAnimation, Theme.quick). Commits on onEditingFinished ONLY (never onTextChanged) and only when text !== modelData.key. Free text: no validation, no uppercase enforcement, no dedup check, no reserved-name guard. Example given in the intro copy: MOZ_ENABLE_WAYLAND.

settings.label: value (per-row variable value)
settings.notes: Bespoke inline control, NOT a SettingField: a TextInput inside a Rectangle (width = fieldsW - keyW, i.e. the remaining ~58%, height 32, Theme.surfaceLo, Theme.radius). Theme.mono font, pixelSize 13, selectByMouse, clip. Placeholder Text child reading "value" in Theme.faint, visible only when text === "" && !activeFocus. Border animates Theme.line → Theme.ember on activeFocus. Commits on onEditingFinished ONLY and only when text !== modelData.value. Free text; an empty value is legal and writes hl.env("NAME", "").

settings.label: Start Rashin with the desktop and keep the dashboard available
settings.notes: THE ONLY REAL SETTING ON THIS PAGE. Not a direct config write: onToggled runs `ryoku-rashin enable` / `ryoku-rashin disable` via Process (enableProc/disableProc), and that CLI does two things — writes cfg.Enabled to rashin.json (SaveConfig, atomic tmp+rename) AND `systemctl --user enable|disable --now ryoku-rashin.service` (falling back to a detached `serve` spawn / SIGTERM of runtime daemon.pid when systemd is absent). Writing the key alone would NOT start/stop the daemon. `enabled: false` default comes from Go defaultConfig() in ryoku/rashin/backend/config.go:32, not from any QML defaults literal. checked binds to page.daemonEnabled; onToggled optimistically assigns page.daemonEnabled = c, then onExited -> page.refresh() re-reads truth from `ryoku-rashin status --json` and can silently snap the switch back. Exit codes and stderr of enableProc/disableProc are ignored. Gated by `enabled: page.installed` (whole row dims to 0.45 opacity when the binary is missing). The CLI's `enable --at-boot` (loginctl enable-linger) is NEVER reachable from this page.

settings.label: Daemon
settings.notes: Inline `component StatusRow` (label left, mono value in a hairline pill right, border+text tinted by `level`). installed ? (running ? 'running' : 'stopped') : 'not installed'. Tint: Theme.ok when running, Theme.dim when stopped, Theme.faint when not installed.

settings.label: Vault files
settings.notes: StatusRow. vaultExists ? (vaultFiles + ' files') : 'empty'. Tint: Theme.subtle when the vault exists, Theme.dim when empty. vaultFiles falls back to 0 unless the JSON value is typeof number.

settings.label: Hermes agent
settings.notes: StatusRow, three-state. Tint encodes the tier: Theme.ok when configured, Theme.ember when installed-but-unconfigured, Theme.dim when absent.

settings.label: Agents wired
settings.notes: StatusRow fed by readonly wiredSummary. Counts are computed in JS: of the agents reported present, how many are wired. Tint is three-tier: Theme.ok when wired === present (and present > 0), Theme.ember when some are wired, Theme.dim when none. Agent set per docs/product copy: Hermes, Claude Code, codex, opencode, Oh My Pi — but the page never enumerates them, it only counts what the CLI reports.

settings.label: Set up Hermes agent
settings.notes: icon: 'sparkles'. primary: !page.hermesConfigured (the button de-emphasises itself once Hermes is configured). enabled: page.installed. Launches an EXTERNAL wizard in a floating kitty window — floated by the hyprland rule 'float-ryoku-rashin-setup' matching class=ryoku-rashin-setup (ryoku/hyprland/modules/window_rules.lua:96). Installs Hermes if absent, wires it to the vault, points other coding agents at the same map; an existing Hermes install is left untouched.

settings.label: Open dashboard
settings.notes: icon: 'display'. primary: true (always). enabled: page.installed. Port 3600 is HARDCODED in the QML and does not read rashin.json's `port` key.

settings.label: Low power mode - strip every heavy effect at once (blur, shadows, animations). The potato switch: implies all four toggles below, so a weak GPU runs the shell lag-free.
settings.notes: Master switch. The page writes ONLY the raw key — it does not flip the other toggles. The implication is applied downstream by each consumer singleton as `lowPower || <individual flag>` (Performance.qml in pill/visualizer/widgets/launcher, Motion.qml) and by decoration.lua (`low_power or perf_flag(...)`). Scope of the implication is reduceMotion/disableBlur/disableShadows/freezePillWhenIdle/freezeVisualizerWhenIdle only — it does NOT imply any unload* key. onToggled additionally calls page.reloadCompositor() -> `hyprctl reload`.

settings.label: Reduce motion - make transitions instant (no per-frame animation repaints)
settings.notes: Shell-side only; no compositor reload. Consumed by Motion.qml (pill/launcher/overview) as `reduce: perf.lowPowerMode || perf.reduceMotion`.

settings.label: Disable blur - shell effects and the compositor backdrop blur (the biggest GPU saving)
settings.notes: Dual consumer: shell singletons (blurDisabled) AND hyprland/modules/decoration.lua (`no_blur`). onToggled calls page.reloadCompositor() -> `hyprctl reload` so Hyprland re-parses without a relogin.

settings.label: Disable shadows - shell drop shadows and the compositor window shadow
settings.notes: Dual consumer: widgets Performance.qml (shadowsDisabled, drops per-tile drop shadows) AND decoration.lua (`no_shadow`). onToggled calls page.reloadCompositor() -> `hyprctl reload`.

settings.label: Hide desktop widgets while windows cover the desktop (frees their memory; they reappear on an empty desktop)
settings.notes: ON by default — one of only two default-true keys. Consumed by Go, not QML: ryoku/shell/ipc/widgetwatch.go `perfFlagDefault("unloadWidgetsWhenCovered", true)`. The Go default must stay in sync with the QML JsonAdapter default. NOT implied by lowPowerMode.

settings.label: Freeze the visualiser when no audio is playing
settings.notes: ON by default — load-bearing: the visualizer singleton comment states the idle animation otherwise leaks on this Qt/NVIDIA stack. Consumed as `visualizerFrozen: lowPower || adapter.freezeVisualizerWhenIdle` in visualizer/Singletons/Performance.qml (default true there too).

settings.label: Unload the visualiser to free memory when silent (brief delay when audio resumes)
settings.notes: Consumed by Go: ryoku/shell/ipc/audiowatch.go `perfFlag("unloadVisualizerWhenSilent")` (missing key -> false). NOT implied by lowPowerMode.

settings.label: Freeze the glowing bead animation while the bar is idle
settings.notes: Consumed as `pillFrozen: lowPower || adapter.freezePillWhenIdle` in pill/Singletons/Performance.qml. Group title is "BAR" but the key and the singleton say "pill" — the label calls it the bar.

settings.label: Unload the launcher to free its memory when idle (brief delay on the next open)
settings.notes: Consumed by Go: ryoku/shell/ipc/idlewatch.go `perfFlag("unloadLauncherWhenIdle")`. NOT implied by lowPowerMode.

settings.label: Unload the workspace overview to free its memory when idle (brief delay on the next open)
settings.notes: Consumed by Go: ryoku/shell/ipc/idlewatch.go `perfFlag("unloadOverviewWhenIdle")`. NOT implied by lowPowerMode.

settings.label: Lock skin (chosen by clicking a tile — there is NO visible label, no SettingSection, no ToggleRow/SliderRow/ChoiceRow/Dropdown/Segmented/ColorField/NumberField/SettingField anywhere in this file; the bento grid itself IS the control)
settings.source: ~/.config/qylock/theme (bare newline-terminated slug, no key, no JSON/conf schema — written by `ryoku-hub lock set` via setLockSkinIn in ryoku/hub/backend/lock.go:229). Two coupled privileged side-writes: /usr/share/sddm/themes/ryoku (recursive `cp -a` of the skin, then chown root:root + chmod a+rX) and /etc/sddm.conf.d/99-ryoku.conf (contents `[Theme]\nCurrent=ryoku\n`). Paths overridable by env: RYOKU_SDDM_THEMES_DIR, RYOKU_SDDM_CONF, RYOKU_QYLOCK_THEMES.
options: clockwork/orbital — the ONLY literal slugs in the codebase are these two curated/vendored entries (lockCurated map, ryoku/hub/backend/lock.go:53). Name "Orbital", Theme "Clockwork", Tags ["Clockwork"].
clockwork/tape — second vendored/offline-baseline slug. Name "Tape", Theme "Clockwork", Tags ["Clockwork"].

<dynamic> — EVERY other option is discovered at runtime, not declared. Options = every folder containing a Main.qml under themes/ in the GitHub tree of Darkkal44/qylock @ branch main, fetched per page load via `ryoku-hub lock catalog` -> https://api.github.com/repos/Darkkal44/qylock/git/trees/main?recursive=1 (overridable via RYOKU_QYLOCK_API / RYOKU_QYLOCK_RAW). The slug is the path under themes/, one or two levels deep (e.g. "clockwork/orbital"). THE OPTION SET CANNOT BE ENUMERATED FROM SOURCE.
last-of-us — inferred to exist: named in the lockGifAlias table (lockcatalog.go:63) mapping folder -> Assets gif "the_last_of_us".
windows_7 — inferred to exist: named in lockGifAlias, maps to Assets gif "win7".
pixel-* — family, inferred: lockSkinTags() (lock.go:204) special-cases the "pixel-" prefix to tag "Pixel" (comment cites "pixel-coffee").
R1999* — family, inferred: lockSkinTags() special-cases the "R1999" prefix to tag "Reverse 1999".

<any hand-dropped folder> — scanLockSlugs() (lock.go:144) also picks up any local folder under ~/.local/share/qylock/themes with a Main.qml, one or two levels deep, even if it is not upstream. Uncurated slugs get a name guessed from the leaf (separators -> spaces, each word capitalised) and a summary read from Description= in the folder's metadata.desktop.
settings.notes: This is the page's ONLY persisted setting. Selecting is fire-and-apply, not edit-then-save: page.select() is a no-op if the tile is already active or any op is in flight (pendingSlug !== ""), otherwise it runs `ryoku-hub lock set <slug>` when installed, or `ryoku-hub lock install <slug>` when not — install downloads every file then activates in the same step. Applying always pkexecs (the SDDM greeter half writes system paths); the user gets a polkit password prompt. On exit != 0 the page sets page.error to "Install failed: " / "Couldn't switch skin: " + stderr (or "exit N"); either way pendingSlug/pendingInstall clear and the catalog re-runs. There is no reset/restore-default affordance in the UI — the default clockwork/orbital exists only as a fallback inside ryoku/lockscreen/qylock/quickshell-lockscreen/lock.sh (used when $1 is absent AND ~/.config/qylock/theme does not exist, and again when the named theme has no Main.qml on disk).

settings.label: Framerate
settings.notes: Option labels are "30 fps" / "60 fps" / "120 fps". fieldWidth: 200. TYPE TRAP: the JsonAdapter property is `int fps` but the Dropdown is string-keyed — read is `current: String(adapter.fps)`, write is `adapter.fps = parseInt(k)`. Must stay an unquoted JSON number; the consumer does `cfg_get '.fps' 60`. Writes to disk immediately via cfg.writeAdapter() on choose; no Save button. Env RYOKU_SCREENRECORD_FPS overrides the file at record time. Only 30/60/120 are exposed but any int can live in the file (env/hand edit) — an unknown value makes the Dropdown fall back to its placeholder "Select…".

settings.label: Framerate mode
settings.notes: Option labels carry the whole recommendation: cfr = "Constant (smooth, recommended)", vfr = "Variable (smaller, can look choppy)". fieldWidth: 200. Default is cfr on purpose (file header comment: VFR files often import/play back as ~30fps and look choppy). Passed to gpu-screen-recorder as `-fm`. Env RYOKU_SCREENRECORD_FRAMERATE_MODE overrides. Also rendered UPPERCASED into the UNDER THE HOOD readout.

settings.label: Quality
settings.notes: Option labels: "Medium", "High", "Very high", "Ultra" (note the key is very_high with an underscore, the label is "Very high"). fieldWidth: 200. Passed to gsr as `-q`; the wf-recorder fallback maps it to qp/crf (very_high -> qp=20/crf=20). Env RYOKU_SCREENRECORD_QUALITY overrides.

settings.label: Codec
settings.notes: Option labels: h264 = "H.264 (most compatible)", hevc = "HEVC / H.265 (crisper)", av1 = "AV1 (best, newer GPUs)". Key is `hevc`, not `h265`. fieldWidth: 200. Passed to gsr as `-k`. Env RYOKU_SCREENRECORD_CODEC overrides. Not applied on the wf-recorder fallback path (see hazards).

settings.label: Encoder
settings.notes: Option labels: gpu = "GPU (hardware)", cpu = "CPU (libx264)". fieldWidth: 200. Passed to gsr as `-encoder`. Env RYOKU_SCREENRECORD_ENCODER overrides. No cross-field validation against Codec (cpu + av1/hevc is selectable and nothing disables it).

settings.label: Show the cursor in recordings
settings.notes: Only setting on the page that is not a Dropdown. Read via `cfg_bool '.cursor' true` and passed to gsr as `-cursor true|false`. NO env override exists for this one (unlike the other five). Not honoured by the wf-recorder fallback path.

settings.label: Autostart commands (the list itself)
settings.source: ~/.config/ryoku/hypr.json (editable truth; key "autostart"), rendered into ~/.config/hypr/settings.lua as an hl.on("hyprland.start", ...) hook
settings.notes: The page's ONLY setting. A Repeater over store.autostart; each element is an object {"command": string}. There is no SettingSection and no tab bar on this page — group/tab are genuinely absent, not unfound. Mutated exclusively through three page-local JS helpers: add() pushes {"command": ""}; remove(i) splices; patch(i, val) copy-on-writes element i's .command. All three route through store.editList("autostart", a), which does NOT call queuePreview() — so the list never previews live and only lands on Save. ORDER IS SEMANTIC: genStartHook (hypr.go:1183) emits one hl.exec_cmd(...) per element in array order, so list order is a persisted setting, yet the UI offers no reorder/drag affordance. Default [] confirmed twice: HyprStore.qml:93 (property var autostart: []) and defaultOverrides() hypr.go:303 (Autostart: []Autostart{}). loadOverrides also re-nils to []Autostart{} at hypr.go:365.

settings.label: Command (per-row text field)
settings.source: ~/.config/ryoku/hypr.json → autostart[i].command; emitted to ~/.config/hypr/settings.lua as hl.exec_cmd(<lua-escaped command>)
settings.notes: Bespoke inline control, NOT a SettingField: a raw TextInput (id: cmdIn) inside a hand-rolled Rectangle chrome. Theme.mono font, pixelSize 13, selectByMouse: true, clip: true. Placeholder text "command to run", shown only when text === "" AND !activeFocus (a child Text, not a real placeholder property) in Theme.faint. Focus ring is bespoke: border.color animates Theme.line → Theme.ember on activeFocus (ColorAnimation, Theme.quick). CRITICAL COMMIT SEMANTICS: commits via onEditingFinished (focus-loss / Enter) — NOT per-keystroke — because patch() replaces the whole array, which rebuilds the Repeater's delegates mid-type and destroys the focused TextInput. The file's header comment states this explicitly. No validation, no dedup, no shell-syntax check; duplicates are permitted. Blank/whitespace-only commands are ACCEPTED and persisted to JSON but silently skipped when rendering Lua (genStartHook: strings.TrimSpace(a.Command) == "" → continue), so a blank row appears saved yet does nothing.

settings.group: WI-FI (bespoke header bar at line 243-282, NOT a SettingSection: an eyebrow Text "WI-FI" + hairline Rectangle + right-aligned Scan HubButton)
settings.label: Wi-Fi
settings.source: none — no file on disk. Writes to the live NetworkManager radio state via `Networking.wifiEnabled = v` (Quickshell.Networking singleton, guarded by `typeof Networking !== "undefined" && Networking`). NM itself persists the radio state; the hub writes no json/conf for this.
settings.notes: The ONLY genuine setting on this entire page (line 286-298). Reads back from `page.wifiOn` = `Networking.wifiEnabled`, so it is a live mirror of NM state, not a stored draft — there is no defaults object literal and no QtObject draft anywhere in this file, and no Save/Apply step. Turning it off cascades: `onWifiOnChanged: if (!wifiOn) stopScan()` kills the scan spinner, the Scan HubButton hides (`visible: page.wifiOn`), the Binding driving `wifiDev.scannerEnabled` goes false, the network Flickable hides and the "Wi-Fi is off." empty state shows. `startScan()` early-returns when off.

settings.label: Custom shortcuts (whole list)
settings.def: [] (empty). Go defaultOverrides() hypr.go:304 `Keybinds: []Keybind{}`; QML draft HyprStore.qml:94 `property var keybinds: []`
settings.source: ~/.config/ryoku/hypr.json (editable truth; key `keybinds`) -> regenerates ~/.config/hypr/settings.lua on Save via genKeybind() hypr.go:1160
settings.notes: NOT declared in KeybindsPage.qml — implemented in KeybindsEditor.qml (private child, used by no other file). Repeater over store.keybinds; each element is {keys, action, value}. Mutated via page.add() (pushes {"keys":"","action":"exec","value":""}), page.remove(i) (splice), page.patch(i,key,val) (slice + Object.assign). All route through store.editList("keybinds", a) which does NOT live-preview — lists land on Save only (HyprStore.qml:195-200). Row identity is array index only; no id field. Backend SILENTLY DROPS rows whose keys are blank, whose action is exec with blank value, or whose action is unrecognised.

settings.label: (no visible label — key combo field; placeholder "SUPER + J")
settings.notes: Bare TextInput inside a 200x32 Rectangle (KeybindsEditor.qml:186) — NOT a SettingField. Theme.mono 13px DemiBold, selectByMouse, clip. Commits on onEditingFinished only (not per-keystroke). Free text, no key-capture widget and no syntax validation against Hyprland key names — the only checking is conflict comparison. Border goes Theme.ember on focus, Theme.gold when the row conflicts. Placeholder Text overlay shows only when text=="" && !activeFocus.

settings.label: (no visible label — action dropdown; Dropdown label is "")
settings.def: exec (row is created with action:"exec"; renderer falls back `rowItem.modelData.action || "exec"`)
settings.notes: EXACTLY 4 options, from `readonly property var actionOpts` KeybindsEditor.qml:69-74. Dropdown width 230, fieldWidth 150, label "" (no leading text). Any action outside these 4 makes genKeybind() return "" and the bind is silently dropped from settings.lua. Selecting a non-exec action hides the value field but does NOT clear the stored value.

settings.label: (no visible label — command field; placeholder "command to run")
settings.notes: Bare TextInput in a Rectangle (KeybindsEditor.qml:234). CONDITIONALLY VISIBLE: `needsValue` = action==="exec" || action===undefined (the undefined branch tolerates legacy rows saved without an action key). Width is hardcoded arithmetic: parent.width - 200 - 230 - delBtn.width - 40. Commits on onEditingFinished. Border Theme.ember on focus (never gold — conflict styling is not applied to this field). An exec row with a blank value is silently dropped by genKeybind().

settings.tab: Connections › Bluetooth (NOT the default subtab — ConnectionsPage.qml defaults to sub: "wifi"; siblings are Wi-Fi and Hotspot, selected via the shared Segmented control with model keys wifi/bluetooth/hotspot)
settings.group: (none — this file uses NO SettingSection at all; the toggle sits bare in the 48px header band, right-aligned in a Row with the Scan pill, spacing 14)
settings.label: (empty string — label: "" — a bare unlabelled switch; the word BLUETOOTH in the header band is separate static Text, not the ToggleRow's label)
settings.def: n/a — no defaults object literal and no QtObject draft exist in this file; checked is a live binding to page.adapterOn (adapter.enabled === true), so the shown value is whatever BlueZ reports at open
settings.source: none — nothing is written to disk. Writes adapter.enabled on the BlueZ D-Bus adapter object via Quickshell.Bluetooth (Bluetooth.defaultAdapter). The blocked path additionally shells out to `rfkill unblock bluetooth`.
settings.notes: THE ONLY setting-shaped control on the page. Bindings: visible: page.hasAdapter (adapter !== null) — the switch is hidden entirely when bluetoothd/org.bluez is absent, replaced by the Start service pill; width: 56 (hard override of ToggleRow's implicitWidth 320, so the label column collapses); anchors.verticalCenter. onToggled: (v) => page.setAdapterEnabled(v) — NOT a plain assignment. setAdapterEnabled(v) is a three-way entry point: (1) if !adapter, return; (2) if v && (page.blocked || unblockProc.running): start unblockProc (`rfkill unblock bluetooth`) if not already running and RETURN without touching adapter.enabled — unblockProc.onExited then sets adapter.enabled = true; (3) otherwise adapter.enabled = v. page.blocked = adapter.state === BluetoothAdapterState.Blocked (rfkill/airplane mode/laptop radio key blocks the radio at the kernel and BlueZ refuses Powered=true, which is why a plain toggle looks dead). Track/knob visuals come from ToggleRow (46x26 track, 20px knob, Theme.ember when checked, Theme.onAccent knob, Theme.quick animations, opacity 0.45 when disabled).

settings.label: Custom shortcuts (list)
settings.source: ~/.config/ryoku/hypr.json (key "keybinds"); rendered to ~/.config/hypr/settings.lua as hl.bind(...) lines on Save
settings.notes: Repeater over store.keybinds. Variable-length array of {keys, action, value} objects — the whole list is the setting; the three fields below are per-row. Written via store.editList("keybinds", a) which does NOT preview (lists land on Save only). Row order is preserved to disk. No stable ids: add/remove/patch operate by array index on a sliced copy. Empty-state text "No custom shortcuts yet." when length === 0.

settings.label: (row) key combo — placeholder "SUPER + J"
settings.notes: Inline bespoke control: 200x32 Rectangle + TextInput (Theme.mono 13, DemiBold, selectByMouse, clip), ember border on activeFocus, gold border when the row conflicts. Placeholder Text child "SUPER + J" shown only when text empty AND not focused. Commits via onEditingFinished → page.patch(index, "keys", text) — i.e. on Enter/focus-loss, not per keystroke. Free text, no key-capture; user must type Hyprland syntax ("SUPER + J", "SUPER + SHIFT + Return"). Feeds conflict detection: page.normKeys() lowercases, trims, drops empties, sorts modifiers, joins with "+". Go genKeybind() silently emits nothing when keys is blank.

settings.label: (row) action
settings.def: exec (page.add() pushes action:"exec"; delegate falls back to modelData.action || "exec")
settings.notes: page.actionOpts = [{exec,"Run command"},{close,"Close window"},{fullscreen,"Fullscreen"},{togglefloating,"Toggle floating"}] — exactly 4, no more. Dropdown used bare: label:"", width 230, fieldWidth 150, onChosen → page.patch(index,"action",k). Backend genKeybind mapping: exec/"" → hl.dsp.exec_cmd(value); close → hl.dsp.window.close(); fullscreen → hl.dsp.window.fullscreen(); togglefloating → hl.dsp.window.float({ action = "toggle" }); any other value → row silently dropped from settings.lua.

settings.label: (row) command — placeholder "command to run"
settings.notes: Inline bespoke control: Rectangle + TextInput (Theme.mono 13), ember border on activeFocus (never gold — conflict styling is not applied here). Conditional visibility: rowItem.needsValue = action === "exec" || action === undefined; hidden for the three dispatcher actions but the stored value is NOT cleared when hidden and still round-trips to hypr.json. Width is computed arithmetic: parent.width - 200 - 230 - delBtn.width - 40. Commits on onEditingFinished → page.patch(index,"value",text). Go genKeybind drops an exec row whose value is blank.

settings.label: Add shortcut
settings.notes: icon "plus". List mutator — it writes the keybinds key, so a rewrite must keep it. Anchored below the intro text, above the scroller.

settings.label: (row) delete
settings.notes: 26x26 Item with Icon name:"trash" size:15, tint Theme.faint → Theme.bad on hover, HoverHandler cursorShape PointingHandCursor, TapHandler → page.remove(index) (splice by index). No confirmation.

settings.label: Clear all
settings.notes: icon "refresh". Calls store.editList("keybinds", []) — wipes every custom bind with no confirmation and no undo other than Revert. Always enabled (even when the list is already empty / not dirty). Note it is NOT a defaults-reset: it does not call resetAppearance/resetInput, and there is no per-domain resetKeybinds in HyprStore.

settings.label: Animations
settings.notes: store.edit("animations", v) -> queuePreview (80ms throttle, hyprctl eval). SHARED KEY: the identical toggle also exists on AppearancePage.qml:463. Emits `animations = { enabled = %t }` in settings.lua.

settings.label: Animate the focused window
settings.notes: store.editPlugin() — NO live preview, applies on Save only (plugin loads on reload). Gates the visibility of every other FOCUS FLASH control. When false the whole hyprfocus plugin block is omitted from settings.lua.

settings.label: Style
settings.notes: Labels: flash="Flash", bounce="Bounce", slide="Slide". visible: store.plugins.hyprfocus.enabled. Emits `mode = <str>`. Also switches which of the three sliders below is shown.

settings.label: Flash opacity
settings.notes: visible only when enabled && mode === "flash". Emits `fade_opacity`.

settings.label: Bounce strength
settings.notes: visible only when enabled && mode === "bounce". Emits `bounce_strength`. Note the floor is 0.5, not 0.

settings.label: Slide height
settings.notes: decimals: 0 (plain integer readout, percent false). visible only when enabled && mode === "slide". Emits `slide_height`.

settings.label: Curve (selector)
settings.def: page.liveCurves[0].name (currently "ryokuWobble") — first live curve reported by hyprctl, auto-selected once when selectedCurve === ""
settings.notes: NOT PERSISTED — chooses which curve the BezierEditor edits. options = page.curveNames() = live hyprctl curves + user curves (store.animCurves), de-duplicated by name, live-first. The listed options are the CURRENT machine's runtime values, not a fixed enum. fieldWidth 200.

settings.label: Bezier curve P1 x (first control point, X)
settings.def: 0.25 (curveOf() fallback for an unknown curve; a New curve is seeded 0.25,0.1,0.25,1)
settings.notes: BezierEditor.qml drag handle, clamped by ux(): Math.max(0, Math.min(1, ...)). Hyprland requires X monotonic in time. Readout "P1  x, y" to 2 decimals.

settings.label: Bezier curve P1 y (first control point, Y)
settings.notes: Clamped by uy() to [yMin=-1, yMax=2] — Y deliberately overshoots past 1 for bounce curves (like ryokuBloom).

settings.label: Bezier curve P2 x (second control point, X)
settings.notes: Second draggable handle. Readout "P2  x, y" to 2 decimals.

settings.label: Bezier curve P2 y (second control point, Y)
settings.notes: Clamped to [-1, 2]. Endpoints P0(0,0) and P3(1,1) are PINNED and not editable.

settings.label: User bezier curves (the whole list)
settings.notes: Record shape {name, x0, y0, x1, y1}. Emitted as `hl.curve("<name>", { type = "bezier", points = { { x0, y0 }, { x1, y1 } } })`, written BEFORE the anim items so items can reference them. Entries with a blank/whitespace name are skipped by the Go generator. Names are auto-generated only ('custom', 'custom1', 'custom2', ...) — there is no rename UI.

settings.label: New (add curve)
settings.notes: icon "plus". addCurve(): finds the first free name in the sequence custom, custom1, custom2, ... then upsertCurve(name, 0.25, 0.1, 0.25, 1) and selects it. This is the ONLY way to create a curve — a rewrite that drops it loses curve creation entirely.

settings.label: Delete / Reset (curve)
settings.notes: DUAL-MODE, label and icon both swap: page.selectedIsCustom ? "Delete"/trash : "Reset"/refresh. enabled: selectedIsCustom || selectedHasOverride. resetCurve(name) drops the store entry; if the deleted curve was custom it reselects liveCurves[0]. 'Reset' on a built-in name removes the user override so the live hyprctl curve shows through again.

settings.label: Reset to defaults
settings.notes: icon "refresh", always enabled. PARTIAL RESET: clears only the two anim lists via editAnim. It does NOT reset the GLOBAL Animations toggle nor any FOCUS FLASH/hyprfocus setting, and does not consult store.defaults.

settings.label: Edge
settings.notes: Repeater-generated 4-pill row (lines 156-180), model is an inline object array [{k:"top",l:"Top"},{k:"right",l:"Right"},{k:"bottom",l:"Bottom"},{k:"left",l:"Left"}] — option key `k`, visible label `l` (Top/Right/Bottom/Left). NOT the shared Segmented component: bespoke Rectangle + TapHandler cells, active = Theme.keyTop/Theme.bright, inactive = Theme.surfaceLo/Theme.dim, width = text.implicitWidth+18, height 24. No visible heading text for this setting — the pills are the whole affordance. Tap emits changed("framePopout", [k, ed.align, ed.hoverW(), ed.hoverH()]) which writes all four values at once. Default is only a read-path fallback (line 25: `... ? place.framePopout.edge : "right"`), never a written value. DIVERGENCE: the runtime consumer ryoku/shell/quickshell/pill/PluginPopouts.qml:92 falls back to "top", and validates against ["left","right","top","bottom"], silently rewriting anything unrecognised to "top". `tab` is inherited from PluginsPage's Discover|Installed segmented view; this file defines no tab chrome itself. Rendered only when card.on && card.host === "framePopout".

settings.label: Align (drag the "popout" chip along the chosen edge)
settings.notes: No discrete control exists — the ONLY input is dragging the popout chip (lines 108-152). MouseArea drag.target = the chip, drag.axis = Drag.YAxis when edge is left/right, Drag.XAxis when top/bottom. onReleased computes the chip midpoint / span and snaps: <0.5 => "start", else "end" (line 148). Centre is deliberately NOT a legal value — the middle third of every edge is reserved (top=island, left=mixer, right=power menu, bottom=kept clear for symmetry), so the middle third collapses to whichever of start/end is nearer. Chip position on read: alignPos() = ed.align === "end" ? span - size - 10 : 10 (line 126). Default "start" appears in three places: QML read fallback (line 26), bash writer default (ryoku-plugins-place line 84), runtime fallback (PluginPopouts.qml:93). Emits changed("framePopout", [ed.edge, a, ed.hoverW(), ed.hoverH()]).

settings.label: (no label — hover-zone width, carried through, not editable here)
settings.unit: px (logical; multiplied by the shell scale factor `root.s` at runtime)
settings.notes: NOT user-editable in this file and has no control — but it IS written to disk by every edit made here, so it belongs in the contract. hoverW() (line 28) reads place.framePopout.hoverW or returns 320, and both emit sites (line 149 chip release, line 175 edge tap) re-send it as arg 3 so it round-trips unchanged. If a rewrite drops it from the write call, the bash writer substitutes its own default 320 (ryoku-plugins-place line 84) and the user's hover zone is silently reset. Consumed at PluginPopouts.qml:127 as `hoverW * root.s`, falling back to 0 when absent. Truthiness read (`? :` not `!==undefined`) means a stored 0 collapses to 320. No min/max/step defined anywhere.

settings.label: (no label — hover-zone thickness, carried through, not editable here)
settings.unit: px (logical; multiplied by the shell scale factor `root.s` at runtime)
settings.notes: Same shape as hoverW: no control, no editability, but written on every edit as arg 4. hoverH() (line 29) reads place.framePopout.hoverH or returns 16. Bash writer default is also 16 (ryoku-plugins-place line 84). Consumed at PluginPopouts.qml:128 as `hoverH * root.s`, fallback 0. Stored 0 collapses to 16 via the truthiness read. No min/max/step anywhere.

settings.label: Hotspot
settings.source: none — NetworkManager runtime state of connection profile "RyokuHotspot" (nmcli connection up/down); nothing is written to any Ryoku config file
settings.notes: id hsSwitch, inside the 76px status card, NOT inside a SettingSection. The ToggleRow is given no `label` property (width 64, label rendered separately as the card's Text "Hotspot"). Bound to page.hsActive; `enabled: !page.hsBusy`. ON path: if page.hsPw.length < 8 it FIRST auto-generates an 8-char password via generatePw() (mutating the Password setting as a side effect), then calls applyHotspot() → sh -c create-or-modify + `nmcli connection up "RyokuHotspot"`. OFF path: stopHotspot() → `nmcli connection down RyokuHotspot`. Truth is read back from the system, not from the toggle: hsStateProc runs `nmcli -t -f NAME connection show --active | grep -qxF -- RyokuHotspot` on load and after every apply/down. applyHotspot() early-returns silently if hsBusy or hsPw.length < 8.

settings.label: Network name
settings.source: /etc/NetworkManager/system-connections/RyokuHotspot.nmconnection (written via `nmcli connection add|modify`, read back via `nmcli -t -s -g 802-11-wireless.ssid,802-11-wireless-security.psk connection show RyokuHotspot`)
settings.notes: CredRow{ field: "name" } — bespoke inline `component CredRow` defined inside the SettingSection, NOT a standard SettingField/ChoiceRow. Backed by page.hsName (default "Ryoku"). Read-only value text is tap-to-edit: TapHandler seeds page.hsDraft = value, sets page.hsEdit = "name", swaps in an inline right-aligned TextField (placeholderText "Network name"). Placeholder when empty: "Ryoku" (falls back to "tap to set" only if placeholder is unset). Enter (onAccepted) and focus-loss both commit via commitHotspotEdit(); Esc cancels (clears hsEdit/hsDraft). Commit rule: empty draft is silently dropped (`if (page.hsDraft.length)`). Live: commitHotspotEdit() re-runs applyHotspot() only `if (page.hsActive)` — otherwise the value sits in memory until the next apply. Passed to nmcli as positional arg $1, never spliced into the shell string. Value text uses Theme.font, color Theme.bright, elide ElideLeft, tnum.

settings.label: Password
settings.source: /etc/NetworkManager/system-connections/RyokuHotspot.nmconnection (written via `nmcli connection add|modify`, read back via `nmcli -t -s -g …802-11-wireless-security.psk connection show RyokuHotspot` — the -s secrets flag is required)
settings.notes: CredRow{ field: "pw"; secret: true } — same bespoke inline component. Backed by page.hsPw (default ""). WPA2 minimum 8 chars enforced in THREE places: applyHotspot() early-returns if hsPw.length < 8; commitHotspotEdit() SILENTLY DROPS a draft under 8 chars (`if (page.hsDraft.length >= 8)`); and an inline red (Theme.bad) warning "WPA2 needs at least 8 characters" shows while cr.tooShort (draft length 1–7, editing, pw row only). Masked display: "•".repeat(Math.max(value.length, 8)) unless cr.reveal. Per-row transient `reveal` bool driven by a Show/Hide pill (not persisted, not a setting). Placeholder when empty: "Tap to set"; editor placeholderText: "8+ characters". Rendered in Theme.mono, color Theme.ember. Auto-generated by generatePw() when the toggle is switched on with no valid password: 8 chars from the ambiguity-free alphabet "abcdefghijkmnpqrstuvwxyz23456789" (no 0/O/1/l/I). Passed to nmcli as positional arg $2, never spliced into the shell string. Live: re-applies immediately only if page.hsActive.

settings.label: hyprglass.tint
settings.notes: COLOUR FORMAT DIFFERS from every other colour key: 8-digit AARRGGBB/RRGGBBAA with NO leading '#' (Hyprland rgba() form), while glowColor/activeBorder/inactiveBorder use '#rrggbb'. A shared ColorField must not normalise this.

settings.label: hyprfocus.enabled
settings.notes: Focus-animation plugin. Explicitly called out in resetAppearance() as returned to off by 'Reset to defaults'. Not live.

settings.notes: hyprscrolling has NO `enabled` key of its own — it follows appearance.layout==="scrolling" (AppearancePage.qml:292-293 uses that as `visible:`). Do not invent an enable toggle in a rewrite.

settings.label: hyprscrolling.followFocus
settings.notes: Gated by appearance.layout==="scrolling" (AppearancePage.qml:298-299). No plugin enable key.

settings.label: env

settings.label: windowRules
settings.notes: Opaque var array of WindowRule records. editList(), no preview. Editor is WindowRulesPage.qml.

settings.label: layerRules

settings.label: appOverrides
