Whole tile is a tap target: TapHandler → page.activateDevice(modelData), which is POLYMORPHIC — disconnect() if connected, connect() if paired, else pairDevice() (the bluetoothctl flow).

PER-DEVICE RUNE TILE (40x40, radius Theme.radius, left margin 12) — fill rgba(242/255,86/255,35/255,0.16) + border rgba(...,0.40) when connected, else Theme.keyTop + Theme.line; contains an 18x18 Shape of the same BT PathSvg glyph, strokeWidth 1.7, Theme.ember when connected else Theme.subtle.

PER-DEVICE NAME + META COLUMN — name Text (14px, Theme.bright/DemiBold when connected else Theme.cream/Medium, ElideRight) resolving deviceName || name || address || "Unknown"; meta Text (11px, Theme.ember when pairing else Theme.faint, ElideRight, visible only when non-empty) showing "pairing…" while pairing, else metaFor(d) = "connected" or "paired" joined with " · " plus BluetoothDeviceState.toString(d.state).toLowerCase() when it isn't already in the list.
PAIRING PULSE DOT — 8x8 radius 4 Theme.ember, visible while that device is pairing; SequentialAnimation on opacity, loops Infinite, 0.35→1 then 1→0.35, 600ms each, Easing.InOutSine.

BATTERY PILL — visible when connected && battery >= 0; height 22, radius 999, fill rgba(242/255,86/255,35/255,0.12), border rgba(...,0.32), width text.implicitWidth + 18; text "<NN>%" Theme.ember 11px DemiBold. batteryLevel() normalises: undefined/null/<=0 → -1 (hidden); values <= 1 are treated as a 0..1 fraction and multiplied by 100; result Math.round-ed.

PAIR PILL — visible when !paired && !pairing; height 22, radius 999, fill Theme.keyTop, border Theme.subtle on hover else Theme.line; text "Pair" (Theme.bright on hover else Theme.cream, 11px DemiBold). Purely indicative — the tap target is the whole tile, not the pill.

DISCONNECT PILL — visible when connected; height 22, radius 999, fill Theme.keyTop on hover else transparent, border Theme.ember on hover else rgba(242/255,86/255,35/255,0.30); text "Disconnect" Theme.ember 11px DemiBold. Also indicative — the tile handles the tap.

PAIRING FAILED LINE — Text "Pairing failed", Theme.ember 11px Medium, leftPadding 66 (hand-aligned to the name column), visible only for the device whose address matches page.failedAddress; auto-cleared by failTimer after 4000ms.

PROCESS: pairProc — `sh -c 'timeout 30 bluetoothctl pair "$1" && bluetoothctl trust "$1" && timeout 30 bluetoothctl connect "$1"' sh <address>`; command is rebuilt per device in pairDevice(); guarded against re-entry by pairProc.running; StdioCollector on stdout and stderr (collected but never displayed); onExited clears pairingAddress and, on non-zero, sets failedAddress + restarts failTimer.
PROCESS: unblockProc — `rfkill unblock bluetooth` (/dev/rfkill is seat-writable via systemd uaccess, no root); onExited sets adapter.enabled = true unconditionally if an adapter exists.
PROCESS: svcProc — `pkexec systemctl enable --now bluetooth.service`, StdioCollector on stdout/stderr; onExited sets the serviceError string on non-zero.
TIMERS: scanTimer (25000ms, repeat false, onTriggered sets adapter.discovering = false — the scan auto-stop); failTimer (4000ms, clears failedAddress); svcErrTimer (6000ms, clears serviceError).

LIFECYCLE HOOK: Component.onDestruction — stops scanTimer and force-sets adapter.discovering = false, so leaving the subtab or closing the hub mid-scan doesn't leave BlueZ chewing the radio in the background.

TRANSIENT PAGE STATE (not persisted, not settings): pairingAddress, failedAddress, serviceError — plus the derived readonly props adapter, devices, adapterOn, discovering, hasAdapter, blocked, connectedCount, devicesSorted.
KeybindsEditor.qml: Intro help paragraph (Theme.dim, 12px, wrapped): explains that custom shortcuts layer over shipped binds, appear in the Shortcuts legend and get conflict-checked, and explicitly warns NOT to hand-edit ~/.config/hypr/user.lua (binds written there never appear in the legend and aren't conflict-checked), plus the combo syntax examples 'SUPER + J' / 'SUPER + SHIFT + Return'. This copy is load-bearing product guidance, not decoration.
Scrolling row canvas: Flickable (clip, StopAtBounds, contentWidth=width, contentHeight=rows.height) hosting a Column of 56px-tall rows with 10px spacing, width flick.width - 12.
Bespoke ScrollBar: policy AsNeeded, width 7, contentItem Rectangle implicitWidth 4, Theme.line, opacity 0.4 idle / 0.7 hovered / 0.9 pressed.
Empty state: Text 'No custom shortcuts yet.' (Theme.faint, 13px) shown when store.keybinds.length === 0.

Conflict indication (visual only, no setting): row border and key-combo field border turn Theme.gold when page.rowConflict(index) !== "". Two conflict classes computed but not distinguished in the row UI: 'shipped' (shadows a Ryoku bind from the parsed legend) and 'duplicate' (repeats another custom row).

Action bar (60px, bottom-anchored, 8px bottom margin): background and border animate to an ember tint (alpha 0.08 fill / 0.4 border) when store.dirty, otherwise Theme.surfaceLo / Theme.line, with ColorAnimation Theme.medium behaviors.
Status dot (9x9, radius 4.5) with three-way color: Theme.gold when conflictCount > 0, else Theme.ember when dirty, else Theme.ok.

Status text, three-way: '<N> conflict with a shipped or duplicate combo' (gold) / 'Unsaved shortcuts' (bright) / 'Saved' (dim). Note the string is not pluralised — it reads '1 conflict with…' and '2 conflict with…' alike.
Revert HubButton (icon 'close', enabled only when store.dirty): store.revert() re-adopts the committed doc AND shells out to `ryoku-hub hypr restore` for a full config regen+reload.

Save HubButton (icon 'check', primary, enabled only when store.dirty): store.save() shells out to `ryoku-hub hypr save <full JSON snapshot>`, which regenerates settings.lua and reloads Hyprland; it optimistically marks committed = snapshot without waiting for the process result.

HyprStore instance created locally on this page (HyprStore { id: store }): boots `ryoku-hub hypr defaults` and `ryoku-hub hypr get` on load, holds the ENTIRE Hyprland override doc (appearance, input, cursor, env, windowRules, layerRules, appOverrides, autostart, keybinds, anim, plugins) as its draft, and Save writes all of it back — not just keybinds.

Injected `categories` property (the shipped bind legend, parsed live from ~/.config/hypr/modules/binds.lua by `ryoku-hub keybinds` and passed down from Hub.qml → KeybindsPage.qml): consumed here only to build shippedKeys for conflict detection; it is rendered as the visible legend by the sibling KeybindLegend.qml on the 'Shortcuts' tab.

Sibling tab surface (parent KeybindsPage.qml): Segmented control with model [{all,'Shortcuts'},{custom,'Custom'}], default tab 'all'. This editor is the NON-default 'Custom' tab, loaded through a Loader with a fade-in NumberAnimation. The Loader destroys and rebuilds the editor (and its HyprStore, and any unsaved draft) on every tab switch.
AnimationsPage.qml: BezierEditor drag canvas (BezierEditor.qml, 300x280) — a Canvas painting the y=0/y=1 baselines (Theme.lineSoft), the two handle arms (Theme.dim), and the ember cubic curve; plus two 16px round DragHandler handles. Endpoints P0(0,0)/P3(1,1) are pinned. Repaints on x0/y0/x1/y1/width/height change. This is a bespoke direct-manipulation surface with no declarative equivalent.
P1 / P2 live coordinate readouts — mono text 'P1   0.25, 0.10' / 'P2   0.25, 1.00', Theme.cream, updates as handles drag.
Helper text 'Drag the two handles.' (Theme.dim, 12px) next to the bezier editor.
Helper text 'Curves are shared by name. Animations below reference them.' (Theme.faint, 11px, 200px wrapped).

Helper text 'Briefly flashes, bounces, or slides a window when it gains focus. Applies on Save.' — conditionally visible only when store.plugins.hyprfocus.enabled; the 'Applies on Save' wording is load-bearing (hyprfocus is the one group on this page that does NOT preview live).
Empty state 'No tunable animations reported.' (Theme.faint, 13px) — visible when page.liveAnims.length === 0, i.e. hyprctl is unreachable or reports nothing overridden.

Action bar (id: bar, 60px) — dirty-state chrome: background and border animate between Theme.surfaceLo/Theme.line and an 8%-alpha ember wash with 40%-alpha ember border via ColorAnimation over Theme.medium.
Dirty status dot (9px) — Theme.ember when dirty, Theme.ok when clean.
Dirty status text — 'Previewing unsaved changes' (Theme.bright) vs 'Saved · live on your desktop' (Theme.dim).
HubButton 'Reset to defaults' (icon refresh) — clears anim.items and anim.curves only; always enabled.

HubButton 'Revert' (icon close) — enabled only when store.dirty; calls store.revert(), which re-adopts the committed doc AND shells out `ryoku-hub hypr restore` to reload the compositor (eval cannot push some keywords back to a default).
HubButton 'Save' (icon check, primary) — enabled only when store.dirty; shells out `ryoku-hub hypr save <full-json-doc>` and optimistically re-commits the snapshot.
HubButton 'New' (icon plus) in CURVES — creates custom/custom1/custom2... seeded at 0.25,0.1,0.25,1.
HubButton 'Delete'/'Reset' (icon trash/refresh) in CURVES — dual-mode label+icon+enablement.
AnimRow table chrome (AnimRow.qml) — 46px rows, elided leaf name that dims (Theme.dim) when the row is disabled, right-aligned control cluster. Reads as a table, not a stack of setting rows.
Inline `component Step` in AnimRow.qml — the bespoke 24x26 -/+ stepper cells with mono glyphs (U+2212 minus, plus), hover tint and TapHandler.
Inline `component Handle` in BezierEditor.qml — the draggable control-point puck with active-state ember fill.
Flickable + custom 7px ScrollBar (contentItem Rectangle, opacity 0.4/0.7/0.9 by hover/press state).

Process animProc — `hyprctl animations -j`, runs ONCE at page construction; parses d[0] into liveAnims (filtered) and d[1] into liveCurves. This is the sole source of the ANIMATIONS table model and the curve list.
HyprStore's five background Processes — `ryoku-hub hypr defaults` (defProc), `hypr get` (getProc), `hypr preview` (previewProc), `hypr save` (saveProc), `hypr restore` (restoreProc).
Live-preview throttle — 80ms Timer in HyprStore with a previewPending coalescing flag; every scalar/anim edit re-serialises and re-previews the WHOLE document.

`readonly property previewDirty: store.dirty` — a cross-component contract read by Hub.qml go() (line 148): navigating away while dirty fires `ryoku-hub hypr restore` to undo the live preview. A rewrite that drops this property silently strands preview edits on the desktop.
PluginPlacementEditor.qml: Screen-proportioned stage preview (lines 34-153): the placement canvas. width = Math.min(ed.width, ed.height*1.6) (~16:10), height = ed.height-28, Theme.radius, vertical gradient #191320 -> #241a16, 1px Theme.line border, clip:true, horizontally centred and top-anchored.
Faint inner "screen frame" rectangle (lines 50-57): anchors.fill with 8px margins, transparent, 1px border rgba(1,1,1,0.06). Purely semantic — makes the stage read as "your display".
"LIVE PLACEMENT" caption (lines 59-65): top-left of the stage, 10px margins, Theme.dim, Theme.mono, 9px, DemiBold, letterSpacing 2.

Reserved-band overlay (lines 71-104): visualises the centre-third of the chosen edge as undockable while dragging. Geometry flips on edge — thickness 64 when vertical (left/right) vs 60 when horizontal, span = parent.width/3 or parent.height/3, margin m=10, fill rgba(1,1,1,0.03), Theme.radius.

Dashed border on the reserved band (Canvas, lines 85-97): imperative onPaint — ctx.reset(), strokeStyle Theme.dim, lineWidth 1, setLineDash([4,3]), strokeRect(0.5, 0.5, w-1, h-1); repainted via onWidthChanged/onHeightChanged => requestPaint().
"reserved" label centred in the band (lines 98-103): Theme.dim, Theme.mono, 9px, letterSpacing 1.5.

Popout body chip preview (lines 108-152): the draggable proxy for the real popout. 64x96 when vertical / 96x60 when horizontal, Theme.radius, fill rgba(0,0,0,0.5), 1px Theme.ember border, docked to the edge with margin m=10, positioned along the edge by alignPos(). Contains a centred "popout" label (Theme.ember, Theme.mono, 10px).

Drag-arrange interaction on the chip (lines 139-151): MouseArea with cursorShape PointingHandCursor, drag.target = the chip, axis locked to the edge (Drag.YAxis for left/right, Drag.XAxis for top/bottom). The drag itself is a live local preview with no writeback; only onReleased commits, snapping to start/end.

Edge-selector pill row (lines 156-180): bottom-anchored, horizontally centred Row, spacing 6, four Repeater-generated cells. Each cell has a TapHandler (commit) and a HoverHandler (PointingHandCursor). This is the control surface for the Edge setting, but the hover/active styling and the row chrome itself must survive.

Fixed layout contract: implicitHeight 230 (line 31); the stage consumes ed.height-28 and the edge row takes the remaining 28px at the bottom. The parent (PluginsPage.qml:439) sets only width, so the component owns its own height.

Component interface contract: `pluginId` (string), `place` (var, the plugin's placement object from discover.sh), and `signal changed(string field, var args)`. The parent wires it as onChanged: (field, args) => page.place(card.modelData.id, field, args[0], args[1], args[2], args[3]) (PluginsPage.qml:443). Not a setting, but a required seam.

Visibility gate (PluginsPage.qml:440): visible only when card.on && card.host === "framePopout". Desktop-widget plugins (clock, weather, and any manifest with host desktopWidget) never render this editor — they are placed by dragging on the wallpaper (left-drag to move, right-click for menu), and PluginsPage shows a static hint Text instead (PluginsPage.qml:446-454).
HotspotTab.qml: Subtab placement: HotspotTab is NOT a standalone page and NOT the default tab — ConnectionsPage.qml:39 mounts it via `Component { id: hsComp; HotspotTab {} }` behind a Segmented bar with model [{wifi,"Wi-Fi"}, {bluetooth,"Bluetooth"}, {hotspot,"Hotspot"}]; ConnectionsPage defaults to `sub: "wifi"`, so this page is only reachable by selecting the third segment. The Loader destroys/recreates the page on every subtab switch, so all page state (hsName/hsPw/hsActive) is re-read from nmcli each visit.

Header explainer paragraph (Column id: head): "Share this machine's connection as a Wi-Fi hotspot. NetworkManager owns the profile (named RyokuHotspot); changes to the network name or password apply at once when the hotspot is live." — interpolates page.hsCon at runtime, Theme.faint, 12px Medium, width capped at Math.min(parent.width, 640).

Big status/toggle card (Rectangle, width=form.width, height 76, radius Theme.radius): background animates Theme.surfaceLo → Theme.frameBg and border Theme.line → Theme.ember when hsActive; hosts the wifi Icon glyph (name "wifi", size 22, weight 1.8, tint Theme.subtle → Theme.ember, animated), the "Hotspot" title (15px DemiBold, Theme.bright), the status readout, and the ToggleRow.

Live status readout (Text under the "Hotspot" title): three-state — "Working…" while hsBusy, else "Active on " + hsIface when hsActive, else "Off". Color animates Theme.dim → Theme.ember. hsIface is a derived readout: the first Networking device with type === DeviceType.Wifi, `.name` with a "wlan0" fallback.

Tap-to-edit inline editor idiom: the read-only value Text carries a HoverHandler (PointingHandCursor) + TapHandler; tapping seeds page.hsDraft from the current value, sets page.hsEdit to the field id, and Qt.callLater(crField.forceActiveFocus). The editor is a bare TextField (background: null, padding 0, right-aligned, selectByMouse, selectionColor Theme.ember, selectedTextColor Theme.onAccent) that replaces the value in place. Row background lights up (transparent → Theme.surfaceLo, border Theme.lineSoft → Theme.ember) while editing.

"Show" / "Hide" reveal pill (Rectangle id: revealBtn) — password row only; `visible: cr.secret && !cr.editing && cr.value.length > 0`. Hover-lit (transparent → Theme.surface, border Theme.line → Theme.ember, text Theme.subtle → Theme.bright), 24px tall, width = text implicitWidth + 18, toggles the transient per-row cr.reveal. Action, not a persisted setting.

Inline validation message "WPA2 needs at least 8 characters" (Theme.bad, 10px) anchored to the row bottom — password row only, visible only while cr.tooShort (editing with a 1–7 char draft). Advisory only; the actual rejection is the silent drop in commitHotspotEdit().
Password generator action (generatePw()) — fires implicitly from the toggle's ON path, no button of its own. 8 chars from "abcdefghijkmnpqrstuvwxyz23456789".

nmcli side-effect processes (four Process objects, all must survive): hsApplyProc — dynamically-built `sh -c` that branches on `nmcli -t connection show "$c"`: existing → `nmcli connection modify` (ssid, key-mgmt wpa-psk, psk), missing → `nmcli connection add type wifi ifname "$3" con-name "$c" autoconnect no 802-11-wireless.ssid "$1" 802-11-wireless.mode ap 802-11-wireless-security.key-mgmt wpa-psk 802-11-wireless-security.psk "$2" ipv4.method shared`, then `nmcli connection up "$c"`; hsDownProc — `nmcli connection down RyokuHotspot`; hsStateProc — active-state probe with StdioCollector; hsReadProc — SSID+PSK read-back with StdioCollector splitting stdout on newline into hsName/hsPw.
Entry refresh: `Component.onCompleted: page.refreshHotspot()` fires hsStateProc + hsReadProc, so the page adopts system truth on every mount. Both apply and down re-fire it via onExited.
Busy gate: page.hsBusy is set by applyHotspot()/stopHotspot() and cleared in each Process's onExited; it disables the toggle, blocks re-entry into both functions, and drives the "Working…" readout.

Non-user-facing keys the apply writes and a rewrite must keep emitting: 802-11-wireless.mode=ap, 802-11-wireless-security.key-mgmt=wpa-psk, ipv4.method=shared, autoconnect=no, ifname=<hsIface>, con-name/type wifi. None are exposed as settings but the profile is broken without them.
Constant, not a setting: `readonly property string hsCon: "RyokuHotspot"` — the owned NM profile name, interpolated into the header text and every nmcli command.
HyprStore.qml: NO UI WHATSOEVER: HyprStore.qml is a headless `Item` with zero visual children — no PageHeader, no tabs, no SettingSection, no ToggleRow/SliderRow/ChoiceRow/ColorField, no Repeaters, no inline `component` blocks. The task framing ('This is a settings page') is wrong: this is the shared engine behind every Lua-editing settings page. The 94 entries above are backing draft properties + the defaults contract, not rendered controls. Real controls live in AppearancePage.qml, InputPage.qml, AnimationsPage.qml, AutostartPage.qml, LayerRulesPage.qml, WindowRulesPage.qml, KeybindsEditor.qml, AppOverridesPage.qml, EnvironmentPage.qml.

Live-preview pipeline: previewNow() -> Process previewProc running `ryoku-hub hypr preview <full-doc-json>`. Flash-free scalar preview via the backend's `hyprctl eval`. Serializes the ENTIRE doc on every keystroke.

Preview throttle: Timer `throttle` (interval 80ms) + `previewPending` bool + queuePreview(). Leading-edge fire then trailing coalesce (restart-while-pending loop). Mirrors ShellSettingsPage's throttle — a rewrite must keep the 80ms cadence or sliders will flood hyprctl.

Save action: save() -> Process saveProc running `ryoku-hub hypr save <json>`. Stops the throttle, clears previewPending, regenerates settings.lua and reloads (which locks in list changes/removals that eval cannot undo).

Revert action: revert() -> Process restoreProc running `ryoku-hub hypr restore`. Re-adopts `committed` AND shells a full regen+reload, because eval cannot push some keywords back to a default and cannot re-derive wallpaper border colours.

Reset actions, deliberately per-domain so one page never clobbers another's settings: resetAppearance() (appearance keys + cursor keys + resetPlugins()), resetInput() (input keys only), resetPlugins() (plugins subtree only).
Startup fetch #1: Process defProc, `ryoku-hub hypr defaults`, running:true at load, StdioCollector -> JSON.parse into `defaults`. Silently swallows parse errors (empty catch).

Startup fetch #2: Process getProc, `ryoku-hub hypr get`, running:true at load, StdioCollector -> adopt(o), deep-copies snapshot into `committed`, sets `ready = true`. Logs 'hub: hypr get parse failed' on error.
Action-bar state contract consumed by the pages: `ready` (bool) and `dirty` (bool). The Save/Revert/Reset buttons themselves live on the pages, not here.

Mutator API the pages call — the load-bearing public surface of this file: edit(key,value) [scalar + live preview], editList(key,arr) [no preview], editAnim(key,arr) [previews], editPlugin(section,key,value) [deep-copy reassign, no preview], save(), revert(), resetAppearance(), resetInput(), resetPlugins(), adopt(o), snapshot().

JSON marshalling contract with the Go backend: snapshot() emits {appearance, input, cursor, env, windowRules, layerRules, appOverrides, autostart, keybinds, anim:{items,curves}, plugins}; adopt() consumes the same shape and expects a COMPLETE doc (Go always marshals every field after overlaying a partial store on the defaults).

Key-list metadata: appearanceKeys (30), inputKeys (25), cursorKeys (4) readonly arrays + cursorProp() name-mangling helper. These drive snapshot/adopt/reset generically — they are the mechanism by which a property becomes a setting.

LauncherPage.qml: Write-on-edit, not write-on-save. Every control calls page.edit(k,v), which writes launcher.json immediately (throttled: flush at once, then at most once per 70ms while values keep changing, with a trailing write via writePending). The Save button only re-snapshots the committed baseline - it does not gate disk writes. A rewrite that makes controls buffer until Save silently changes the product's live-preview behavior.

Component.onDestruction reverts. Leaving the page with dirty edits writes committedVals back through the adapter, so a preview is never left applied. A declarative settings framework with no page-teardown hook loses this, and abandoned previews become permanent.

Three-way external merge. adoptExternal() runs on every reload after the first load: it pulls file changes only into keys the user has NOT locally edited, leaving edited keys alone. This is not a plain binding to the file - it needs the draft/committed/file triple.

Dirty check is stringly-typed: sameVal(a,b) compares String(a) === String(b) across all 9 keys against a freshly-rebuilt committedVals object (rebuilt so bindings on the baseline re-evaluate). Float formatting (0.6 vs 0.60 vs 0.6000000000000001) decides whether the page reads dirty; heroStrength/heroPosX/heroPosY are the exposed risk.

heroPosX / heroPosY have no labelled control. They exist only as drag math on the preview, gated per-axis on overflow > 1px. Any rewrite that expresses settings as a list of rows will drop them unless the preview canvas survives as a first-class surface.

heroImage uses an empty-string sentinel for "shipped art" and stores a file:// URL otherwise. Not null, not a bool + path pair. Three UI states (empty placeholder, filename readout, reset link) key off heroImage.length.

Defaults are hand-mirrored. The `defaults` object literal at the top of the page duplicates the canonical values in /home/nero/Work/ryoku-arch/ryoku/shell/quickshell/launcher/Singletons/LauncherConfig.qml (comment says so explicitly), and the JsonAdapter here duplicates them a third time. Three copies must stay in sync; the Hub deliberately seeds nothing (only LauncherConfig seeds, and only when file.text() is empty).

resetDefaults() goes through edit(), so it is a live preview that leaves the page dirty - it does not save. "Reset to defaults" followed by leaving the page undoes itself via onDestruction. That is intentional and surprising.

flush() writes ALL 9 keys on every single edit, so any key the rewrite forgets to carry is not just missing from the UI - it gets clobbered on disk with the adapter default the first time the user touches any other control.
bgBlur is `int` in the JsonAdapter but SliderRow.modified emits `real`; the coercion is implicit at the adapter boundary. Same class of issue for radius (`real` adapter, decimals 0 field).

FileView with blockLoading + watchChanges + atomicWrites + printErrors:false. onLoadFailed still calls adopt() and sets loaded = true, so a missing/corrupt file yields a working draft seeded from adapter defaults - and the first edit then writes the whole file. Silent failure by design.

The throttle Timer restarts itself from its own handler while writePending - a self-perpetuating loop tied to drag smoothness. Replacing it with a naive debounce loses the leading-edge write (the first frame of a drag would not preview).

NumberField's typed-entry path only fires modified() on editingFinished (focus loss / Enter), while the steppers fire per tap and on a 90ms repeat timer. Two different commit rhythms behind one control.
ChoiceRow options are [{key,label}] objects whose labels carry non-ASCII glyphs (°C / °F). Reducing the enum to a bare key list loses the display text.

The page hint, the section help texts and the status bar copy all encode the launcher's out-of-process contract ("open it with Super+Space to see them"): nothing on this page shows the real effect, so the preview and the copy are the only feedback the user gets.
UpdatesPage.qml: ONE setting, four non-setting views. Roughly 90% of this file is a live process mirror (phase machine, stage bar, log console, consent wizard, error/rollback flow). A settings-schema rewrite that only models `update_interval` silently deletes the entire update experience. The declarative surface here is one Segmented; everything else needs escape-hatch/custom rendering.

The setting does NOT go through whatever config store other Hub pages use. It is a subprocess round-trip: Process `ryoku-hub config get update_interval` at load, Process `ryoku-hub config set update_interval <k>` on select. A rewrite that binds it to an in-process config singleton changes the write path and can desync from ~/.config/ryoku/hub.toml.

Default is duplicated across two languages and can drift: QML `property string interval: "daily"` (line 18) and Go defaultConfig() (backend/config.go:25). Neither is a `defaults` object literal or a draft QtObject — a rewrite that expects one will find nothing.

STARTUP RACE / FLASH: interval initialises to "daily" and is only corrected when the loadInterval Process' stdout stream finishes. If the stored value is "off", the Timer briefly runs at daily cadence and the Segmented pill visibly slides from Daily to Off. Any rewrite that makes the load synchronous or the property a required binding changes observable behaviour.

Silent-drop validation: loadInterval only accepts exactly off|hourly|daily|weekly; any other stdout (error text, empty, whitespace after trim) leaves the property at "daily". intervalMs' switch ALSO folds unknown values into 24h via its default branch. Two independent fallbacks to daily — a schema with strict enum validation would surface an error where the current code silently heals.
"off" is a mode, not a duration. It is expressed by `running: page.interval !== "off"` on the Timer while intervalMs still returns 24h for it. A naive enum->number mapping loses the distinction.

setInterval() early-returns on an unchanged value, and saveInterval is a single shared Process whose `command` is reassigned then `running=true`. Rapid clicks can stomp an in-flight command; there is no completion handling, no error surface, no rollback if `ryoku-hub config set` fails — the UI shows success unconditionally.

Segmented hard-codes segW: 86 and sizes itself as `count * segW + 8`, so the control's width is welded to exactly these 4 fixed-width options. Adding a fifth option or a longer label silently overflows/misaligns rather than reflowing. indexOfKey() returns 0 (="off") for an unknown key, so an unrecognised value parks the pill on Off while page.interval says otherwise.

page.promptOptions is a runtime string array from the CLI's JSON. The option set, count, and labels are unknown at design time and cannot be expressed as a static enum. Index 0 carries semantic weight (rendered as the affirmative/primary stamp) purely by position.
page.steps and page.logLines are likewise runtime-shaped. Step `state` strings (running|ok|failed|skipped|<anything else>=pending) are an implicit contract with the CLI, never declared in QML.

This UI WRITES the CLI's run-state file: dismiss() shells out to overwrite $XDG_RUNTIME_DIR/ryoku-update.json with {"phase":"idle"}, and answer() writes to $XDG_RUNTIME_DIR/ryoku-update-answer. FileView is watchChanges + atomicWrites:false against a file two processes touch — a rewrite must preserve both the paths and the XDG_RUNTIME_DIR-or-/tmp fallback.

answer() optimistically sets phase="running" before the CLI has read the answer; the next state reload may contradict it. Deriving phase purely from the file (the declarative instinct) would reintroduce a button-flicker this code deliberately avoids.

Terminal is hard-coded to `kitty` in both startUpdate() and rollback(). rollback() additionally depends on `ryoku rollback` printing and exiting, with a `read -r _` to hold the window. Quoting is deliberately positional ($1/$2 via sh) so labels/snapshot ids containing quotes cannot break out — a rewrite that string-interpolates reintroduces an injection.

startUpdate() passes RYOKU_UPDATE_UI=hub, which is what makes the CLI publish the run-state file this page mirrors. Drop the env var and the whole progress/prompt/error surface goes dead with no error.

applyState()'s catch-all resets phase/progress/steps/logLines/errorMsg on any JSON parse failure but NOT snapshot/promptTitle/promptDetail/promptOptions — stale prompt/snapshot values can survive a bad parse.

`page.progress` (line 77) is parsed from the state file and never rendered anywhere — determinate progress is drawn from `steps` instead. It is either dead state or an unfinished contract; a rewrite will be tempted to drop it.

The Updates singleton is global, shared with the top-right island mentioned in the header comment, and holds lastChecked purely in memory ("checked <ago>" resets to "not yet" on restart) with its own 30s tick Timer. Page-local rewrites of check scheduling can double-fire or desync the island.

Repeaters over `page.steps` / `page.logLines` / `page.promptOptions` reassign whole JS arrays on every file change, so every delegate is destroyed and rebuilt — the running segment's pulse animation restarts on each state write. Behaviour that a model-diffing rewrite would inadvertently change.

Layout coupling: the idle header is a manual Item whose height is Math.max(status.implicitHeight, autoCol.implicitHeight) with the setting anchored right of UpdateStatus (anchors.right, rightMargin 32). The setting and a live readout share one row — a SettingSection-per-row rewrite will break this editorial two-column header.
WidgetsPage.qml: Live-preview-before-save is the core model and is not declarative: every control calls page.edit(k, v), which writes ~/.config/ryoku/widgets.json immediately. Save does NOT write - it only moves the committed baseline forward. A naive "setting -> config key" rewrite that writes on Save would invert the product's behaviour.

Component.onDestruction rewrites committedVals back to disk if the page is dirty. Leaving the section or closing the hub silently un-applies the preview. Any rewrite that loses this leaves previews stuck applied on the user's wallpaper.

Three-state value model (draft QtObject / committedVals map / adapter) plus a derived `dirty` property computed by looping page.keys. There is no per-setting dirty flag - it is whole-page. Declarative per-row binding to a config key cannot express this.

adoptExternal() is bidirectional-ownership merge logic: the desktop widgets write the same file (drag, right-click, Config.setFree/setAnchor/toggle). On an external reload, only keys the user has NOT locally edited are pulled in and re-baselined; locally-edited keys keep the draft and stay dirty. This must survive or drags will fight the hub.

sameVal() compares with String() coercion, so 1 vs 1.0 vs "1" are equal. The dirty flag depends on this loose comparison - a strict-equality rewrite would flag reals (clockScale 1.0, opacity 1.0) as permanently dirty.

Anchor / X / Y are a coupled tri-state, not three independent settings: X and Y only render when anchor === "free", and "free" is normally produced by dragging the widget on the desktop (Config.setFree writes anchor+x+y in one shot), not by choosing it in the Dropdown.

Conditional visibility rules that must be preserved literally: Corner radius hidden when <prefix>Bg === "none"; X and Y hidden unless <prefix>Anchor === "free". Hidden rows keep their values - they are not reset.

The `defaults` object literal is a hand-maintained mirror of ryoku/shell/quickshell/widgets/Singletons/Config.qml. It already drifts: Config.qml documents calDesign as "month | minimal | agenda | week" while the hub Dropdown offers a fifth key, "heat" (label "Heatmap").

The same 40 keys are declared four times in this file: page.keys (string array), the draft QtObject, the JsonAdapter, and the defaults literal. All four must stay in lockstep; keys is the loop driver, so a key missing from it is silently never saved, reverted, or reset.

Reset to defaults routes through edit() rather than save(), so it writes live but leaves the bar dirty - Revert after Reset returns to the last saved look, not to defaults. This subtlety is easy to lose.

70ms throttle with a manual writePending trailing-write loop (throttle.restart() from inside onTriggered). save() and revert() must stop the timer and clear writePending or a queued write resurrects stale values after the commit.

Scale, Opacity and Corner radius have no representation in the hub previews - the preview components only take design/format/accent/unit/scope/animate/weekStart. Those settings are only visible on the real desktop, so a rewrite cannot assume preview coverage of every control.

Control choice is deliberate and inconsistent by widget: clockDesign and calDesign are Dropdowns (5 options each) but weatherDesign is a ChoiceRow (3 options); the same conceptual "Design" setting uses two different components. Normalising these would change the UI.
weatherUnit option labels are the degree glyphs °C / °F while keys are bare C / F; calDesign label "Heatmap" maps to key "heat". Key/label divergence must not be collapsed.

SliderRow emits raw slider values via onMoved with no quantisation to `step` at the model layer (unlike NumberField, which clamps and rounds via clampq); the written scale/opacity precision depends on the Slider implementation.

FileView path is computed at runtime from XDG_CONFIG_HOME (falling back to HOME + "/.config") and is duplicated verbatim in the widgets' Config.qml singleton - both must resolve identically or the hub edits a file the widgets never read.

Config.qml has `Component.onCompleted: if (!file.text()) file.writeAdapter();` - the widgets seed the file. The hub's onLoadFailed instead adopts adapter defaults into the draft without writing, so first-run ordering between the two processes matters.
WindowRulesPage.qml: Polymorphic value key: one config key (windowRules[].value) is driven by THREE different controls depending on the sibling `action` - hidden entirely for 19 of the 25 actions, a free-form mono TextInput for [opacity, size, move, workspace], and a Dropdown (with two different option sets) for [idleinhibit, suppressevent]. A declarative schema that maps one key to one control cannot express this; it needs a discriminated union keyed on `action`.

Cross-field side effect: page.setAction() does not just write `action`, it also overwrites `value` with page.actionValueDefault(key) ('always' for idleinhibit, 'maximize' for suppressevent, '' for everything else). Changing one setting silently mutates another - not expressible as an independent binding.

Action-dependent placeholder: rrow.valueHint is a computed ternary chain (opacity->'0.0 - 1.0', size->'1200x800', move->'100,60', workspace->'2'). The hint is the ONLY guidance the user gets and it is not a static property of the field.

No validation, no min/max/step/unit anywhere: opacity is semantically a 0..1 real and size/move are numeric pairs, but all are stored as free-form strings. Coercion happens server-side in Go (parseFloat(value, 1), parseWxH(value)). A rewrite that adds a SliderRow/NumberField for opacity would change the on-disk type from string to number and break the Go unmarshal (Value is `string`).

List edits do not preview live, unlike every scalar page backed by the same HyprStore. store.editList() deliberately skips queuePreview() because rule changes need a full reload. The status text hardcodes 'applies on save'. Any rewrite that routes these through the live-preview path will fire `ryoku-hub hypr preview` on every keystroke.

Text commits on onEditingFinished, never per keystroke, and this is load-bearing: the Repeater rebuilds its delegates on every model change, so a per-keystroke patch() would destroy and recreate the TextInput the user is typing into (losing focus and caret). The file comment at lines 63-65 calls this out explicitly.

Whole-array replacement on every mutation: store.editList wants a fresh JS reference to trigger reactivity, so patch/setAction/addRule/removeRule all slice() + Object.assign() a new array. Mutating in place will silently fail to update the UI or the dirty flag.

Index-based identity: rows have no stable id. Delete/reorder shifts every subsequent index, and the Go backend derives each rule's Lua name from its position (`ryoku-user-<i+1>`). Any keyed/ID-based list model would change the generated names.

Silent drops with no UI feedback: genWindowRule returns "" (and the rule vanishes from settings.lua) when action is empty OR when BOTH class and title are empty. The UI happily lets you create and save such a row - it looks saved but does nothing.

Stored key != emitted Lua field. windowRuleField remaps nodim->no_dim, noanim->no_anim, noblur->no_blur, noshadow->no_shadow, nofocus->no_focus, stayfocused->stay_focused, keepaspectratio->keep_aspect_ratio; norounding becomes `rounding = 0` and noborder becomes `border_size = 0` (not booleans at all). The Go comment warns that a wrong field name errors inside settings.lua and silently disables everything after it.

Shared-store blast radius: HyprStore holds the ENTIRE hypr.json document. store.dirty is a JSON.stringify diff of the whole snapshot, so Save on this page persists appearance, input, cursor, env, keybinds, plugins and anim too, and Revert reverts all of them plus runs `ryoku-hub hypr restore`. The Save/Revert enable state reflects document-level dirt, not this page's edits - editing Appearance and then visiting Window Rules shows 'Unsaved changes' here.

Hand-rolled layout arithmetic: rrow.tfW = (width - 24 - ddW - 30 - gap * (needsValue ? 4 : 3) - (needsValue ? valW : 0)) / 2, with magic constants ddW 150, valW 110, gap 10, 24 (row padding), 30 (delete button). The two match fields split the remainder and the row reflows when needsValue flips. A Layout-based rewrite will not reproduce these widths exactly.

Two controls share one slot: the free-form value Rectangle and the choice Dropdown are both children of the same Row with mutually exclusive `visible` bindings. Row layout depends on visible correctly excluding the hidden one.
`class` is a JS reserved word, so the model must be read as modelData["class"] (bracket access) and written as a[i]["class"] - a dot-access rewrite will break at runtime, not compile time.
Backend silently re-clamps out-of-range enums (idleinhibit -> 'always', suppressevent -> 'maximize'), so a UI bug that writes a junk value is invisible in the product but changes behaviour.
'Clear all' destroys every rule with no confirmation and no undo other than Revert (which only works before Save).

Defaults are asymmetric across three places: the QML addRule() literal seeds action 'float', page.actionValueDefault() seeds the enum values, and the Go defaultOverrides() seeds only the empty list. There is no `defaults` object literal or QtObject draft block in this file - per-row defaults are scattered across function bodies.
AppOverridesPage.qml: NOT a settings page — a record-list editor. There is no fixed set of keys: the user creates an unbounded array of cards, each with the same 10-field schema. A declarative flat key→control table cannot express this; the rewrite needs a repeatable record schema plus add/remove/clear verbs.

No SettingSection anywhere, and no tabs. Group titles are hand-rolled eyebrow Text blocks ("LOOK", "EFFECTS") with their own mono/10px/letterSpacing-2 styling and trailing helper paragraphs. Porting onto SettingSection changes the visual structure and drops the helper prose unless carried explicitly.

Sentinel encoding -1 = "inherit" for all three numeric fields, tested as `value >= 0`. 0 is a LEGAL custom value (square corners / no border) and must stay distinguishable from -1. Any rewrite using null/undefined/optional, or a falsy check instead of `>= 0`, will silently turn "Corners: 0" into "inherit".

Custom→Inherit is lossy and irreversibly so: switching to Inherit writes -1 over the previous custom value, so the `ov.value >= 0 ? ov.value : ov.customDefault` restore branch is dead code — coming back to Custom ALWAYS lands on customDefault (0.9 / 8 / 2), never the value you had. Faithfully reproducing this needs the same amnesia, or it's a behavior change.

The five EFFECTS rows are NOT uniform: blur/shadow/dim/anim use offChoice [inherit|off], but `opaque` uses onChoice [inherit|on]. Collapsing them into one tri-state helper writes "off" into a key whose only valid non-inherit member is "on", and genAppOverride() would then silently emit nothing.
None of these are booleans despite looking like toggles — they are 2-member enums of STRINGS ("inherit"/"off"/"on"). A ToggleRow/bool rewrite destroys the third state the whole page is built around.

Zero live preview. `editList()` deliberately does NOT call queuePreview() (unlike `edit()` and `editAnim()`), so the entire page is Save-only — window rules can't be pushed via `hyprctl eval`. Every other scalar page previews live; wiring this one into a live-preview framework would be wrong.

MatchField commits on onEditingFinished, never per keystroke, and this is load-bearing: `patch()` replaces the whole array by identity, the Repeater rebuilds every card, and a live text binding would steal focus/keystrokes mid-typing. The file's own comment flags this. Any two-way `text:` binding regresses it.

Every mutation (patch/addApp/removeApp) does slice() + Object.assign() to force a fresh array reference, because HyprStore.dirty is a JSON.stringify diff of the whole snapshot. In-place mutation compiles fine and silently never marks the page dirty — Save stays disabled.

Silent no-op records: genAppOverride() returns "" when match is empty (no class AND no title) OR when no prop diverges (all-inherit). Such a card persists in hypr.json, renders in the UI, and produces no rule — visible state with no effect, and no validation or warning anywhere.

Rule names are positional: `ryoku-app-<i+1>`. Deleting or reordering renumbers every downstream rule in settings.lua. Card identity is the array index (`required property int index`) — there is no stable id.
No dedupe or validation: two cards can match the same class with conflicting props; resolution is left to Hyprland precedence (last rule wins). Nothing in the UI surfaces the conflict.

Cross-field semantics live only in prose: "Force opaque… (overrides Opacity)". Both can be set simultaneously and both are emitted; the interaction is Hyprland's, not the page's. A schema with per-field independence loses this.

Opacity's UI floor is 0.2 but the Go guard accepts `>= 0 && <= 1` — the widget range is narrower than the contract. A hand-edited hypr.json with opacity 0.05 is valid to the backend, applies as a rule, but the slider clamps/misrepresents it on load.

JSON↔Lua name skew the generator hides: `borderSize` → `border_size`, and the four off-toggles invert into NEGATED props (`no_blur`, `no_shadow`, `no_dim`, `no_anim`) while `opaque` stays positive (`opaque = true`). The file's comment warns a wrong field name breaks settings.lua outright.

Revert has side effects beyond the draft: store.revert() re-adopts committed AND spawns `ryoku-hub hypr restore`, a full config regen + compositor reload, because `hyprctl eval` can't push some keywords back to a default. It is not a pure UI rollback.

openClasses is dynamic, external, fetched once, and failure-silent: a `hyprctl clients -j` Process whose parse is wrapped in an empty `catch (e) {}`. The Dropdown's option set is not static, is never refreshed after Component.onCompleted, and the control vanishes entirely (`visible`) when the list is empty.

The pickWin Dropdown is not a select: `current: ""` is a constant, so it never reflects a selection and re-picking the same class re-adds a duplicate card. It is an action menu wearing a Dropdown, and a rewrite that binds `current` to state will change its behavior.

Layout is hand-anchored, not layout-managed: classField width is the literal `(parent.width - 30 - 20) / 2`, labels are fixed 104px, the readout 42px, Segmented is a fixed 86px-per-segment control (implicitWidth = count*86+8), and `intro` re-anchors depending on pickWin.visible. Dropping these into declarative rows will reflow the card.

modelData is read defensively throughout (`card.modelData["class"] || ""`, `card.modelData.blur || "inherit"`) because records loaded from an older hypr.json may lack keys — the Go loader overlays partials onto defaults, so absent/empty must keep coercing to "inherit". `class` in particular must be bracket-indexed: it is a reserved word.
DictationPage.qml: The enum options do not exist in the QML. page.presets is filled at runtime from `ryoku-hub voxtype get`, sourced from voxtypePresets() in /home/nero/Work/ryoku-arch/ryoku/hub/backend/voxtype.go. A declarative QML-only rewrite cannot enumerate whisper-fast / whisper-accurate / openai statically without duplicating the Go list, and every card field (label, provider, size, detail, cloud, keyKind, present) is backend-shaped data.

The selected preset is persisted as a TOML COMMENT — `# ryoku-preset: <key>` — not a real key. selectedPreset() reads it back by string-scanning for that prefix. Any generic/declarative TOML writer will drop the comment on rewrite and the page will silently fall back to "whisper-fast" forever, quietly resetting the user's engine.

"Voice typing" has no config key at all. It is systemd user-unit enablement (enable+restart / disable --now, read via is-enabled). It is a side-effecting action against the service manager, not a value in a file — it cannot round-trip through a config-file settings model.

config.toml is rendered WHOLESALE from a Go string template on every set; it is not a per-key writer. buildVoxtypeConfig() hardcodes a large set of keys no control on this page exposes, and all of them are silently reasserted on every toggle/select/key-save: state_file="auto"; [hotkey] enabled=false; [audio] device="default", sample_rate=16000, max_duration_secs=60, pause_media=true; [whisper] translate=false (+ mode/model/language/remote_endpoint/remote_model); [output] mode="type", fallback_to_clipboard=true, type_delay_ms=1; [output.notification] on_recording_start=false, on_recording_stop=false, on_transcription=false; [osd] enabled=false. A rewrite that maps only the 3 visible settings to keys will destroy these. The header comment states hand edits are overwritten by design.

The API key is write-only and asymmetric. The UI never reads it back (only the bool openaiKeySet); blank means "keep the stored value", and the backend re-reads remote_api_key from disk to preserve it. A declarative two-way-bound `value` model would send "" on any unrelated save and wipe the user's key. Also: apply() ALWAYS sends openaiKey:"" — correctness depends entirely on the backend's keep-on-empty rule.

openaiKeySet is true if EITHER remote_api_key is in config.toml OR env VOXTYPE_WHISPER_API_KEY is set. The env case is unwritable and unclearable from the UI, so "A key is saved" can be true with nothing on disk, and the toggle can be unlockable with no file state to point at.

Cross-field dependency gating: the Voice typing toggle's enabled state is derived from the SELECTED preset (usable = sel.cloud ? keyOnFile : sel.present), which itself depends on filesystem probing and the key. Three settings are entangled; none is independently expressible.

choose() is not a setter — it is a conditional side effect. Clicking an undownloaded local model starts a download and RETURNS without selecting; selection only happens later in dlProc.onExited when code === 0. Selection is therefore async, long-running (1.6 GB), and failable.
Global single-flight download mutex: download() early-returns if page.downloading !== "", so one row's in-flight state suppresses every other row's action. Row state is not row-local.

Model presence is filesystem probing (~/.local/share/voxtype/models/ggml-<model>.bin) with no watcher — `present` only refreshes on reload(), i.e. Component.onCompleted, window re-focus, or a set/rm completing. A model deleted out-of-band shows as downloaded until focus bounces.

Error surfacing is stderr text-scraping (this.text.trim() into page.busyError) plus a synthetic exit-code message. JSON parse failure of `get` is only console.log'd — page.loaded stays false and the page renders the settings shell with an empty preset list and no error at all.

dlProc streams voxtype's real download progress to stdout (backend wires cmd.Stdout = os.Stdout for exactly this reason: "streams progress to stdout so the Hub can show it"), but the QML attaches NO stdout collector and shows only an indeterminate Spinner. A rewrite must not assume a percentage exists in the UI, nor that the stream is unused by contract.
The notice text encodes a real product caveat that is not a setting: a freshly downloaded model may need a REBOOT to take effect. Losing this string loses the only warning the user gets.

Both package operations are detached kitty one-liners (gpk needs a tty for AUR build + sudo). removeVoxtype is a compound shell string with && plus rm of the unit file and a daemon-reload — unrepresentable as a declarative setting, unobservable (fire-and-forget, no exit code), and recovered from only by the window-focus re-probe.

previewDirty is a hardcoded `readonly ... : false` constant, and there is no save/apply/revert model — every control writes immediately. A rewrite onto a draft/QtObject + Save architecture would contradict this page's shell contract.

No `defaults` object literal and no QtObject draft exist in the QML. Defaults are implicit and triple-sourced: QML `selected: "whisper-fast"` / `voiceOn: false`, backend selectedPreset() fallback "whisper-fast", and voxtypeEnsure() seeding whisper-fast. There is no single declared default surface to port.

Deliberate omissions that look like missing features: Parakeet and the ONNX engines are intentionally absent because selecting them would rewrite the root-owned /usr/bin/voxtype symlink, which the Hub cannot do without a terminal sudo prompt. A rewrite that "completes" the engine list would ship a broken option.

ensureVoxtypeUnit() writes an unlisted resilience drop-in (~/.config/systemd/user/voxtype.service.d/ryoku-resilient.conf: StartLimitIntervalSec=0, Restart=always, RestartSec=2) as an invisible side effect of enabling the toggle. It is not a setting but must not be dropped — it prevents a mic crash-loop from permanently killing dictation.

The card delegate uses `width: parent ? parent.width : 0` and `required property var modelData` — the Repeater delegate is untyped model data with a null-parent guard; porting to a typed/declarative row model changes both the binding shape and the null-safety assumption.
PluginsPage.qml: No settings primitives are used anywhere. Zero SettingSection, ToggleRow, SliderRow, NumberField, ChoiceRow, Dropdown, ColorField, SettingField, Segmented, HubButton — despite all of them existing next door in the same directory. Every control is a hand-rolled Rectangle + TapHandler/MouseArea with its own geometry and Behavior animations, and there are NO group titles to map onto SettingSection. A declarative rewrite has to invent the grouping, and the visual result will not be pixel-identical.

The settings are data-driven, not static. There is no fixed row set: the Installed cards are a Repeater over whatever `discover.sh --all` returns at runtime, and each card's "Show as" options come from that plugin's own manifest.json `hosts`. The config keys are all `<pluginId>.*` — a per-instance namespace unknown until a subprocess answers. Nothing here can be enumerated at build time.

framePopout is one atomic 4-positional write, not four settings. `ryoku-plugins-place <id> framePopout <edge> <align> <hoverW> <hoverH>` has no per-field verb, so edge and align cannot be written independently and hoverW/hoverH — which have no control on this page — must be read back and re-sent on every single edit. Model each as an independent declarative key and the two hover values get clobbered to 320/16.

align has no control at all: it exists only as the outcome of a drag gesture plus a snap rule (nearest half, middle third collapses, centre is unrepresentable). There is no declarative widget whose value is "align" — the setting is an emergent property of a physics-ish interaction on a preview canvas.

The drag permanently breaks its own binding. body.x/body.y are declared as bindings on ed.edge/ed.align, but `drag.target: parent` writes x/y imperatively, destroying those bindings for the life of the delegate. onReleased only emits the value — it never restores the binding or snaps the chip back — so the preview stays wherever it was dropped and stops tracking the persisted value until the delegate is rebuilt.

The enabled toggle is not a pure boolean write. Setting it true makes the backend run seed_entry(), which injects the plugin's entire manifest preset block (metadata.settings[].default, or legacy metadata.defaultSettings) and a default host into plugins.json. A declarative `enabled: bool` binding hides a schema-materializing side effect.

Default-host disagreement between UI and disk. The card falls back to "framePopout" when a manifest has no defaults.host (PluginsPage.qml:295-296), but seed_entry in ryoku-plugins-place falls back to "desktopWidget". For such a plugin the UI shows one host selected while the file gets the other — a rewrite that declares a single `def` per key cannot express this fork.

The `host` and `framePopout` verbs both force `enabled: (existing // true)` as a side effect, so a placement edit on a disabled plugin silently enables it on disk. The UI hides those controls behind `visible: card.on`, which papers over it — but the coupling is in the backend, not the QML.

Everything is live with no apply/cancel/undo. Each tap shells out immediately; the Registry file-watches plugins.json and retunes every surface with no reload. There is no draft object, no defaults QtObject, no dirty state, no revert. A form-with-Save rewrite changes the product's behavioural contract, not just its look.

Two writers race on one file. ryoku-plugins-place mktemp+renames precisely because a desktop drag can commit while Settings flips enabled; its own comment concedes the worst case is a lost update. State is also read back only via a full subprocess re-scan (placeProc.onExited -> refresh()), so the UI is eventually-consistent with disk, never bound to it.

The whole page is a subprocess shell, not a config view. Nothing renders until `discover.sh --all` and `ryoku-hub extras plugincatalog` answer; the script path is itself resolved from $RYOKU_SHELL_DIR with an XDG fallback, and catProc deliberately re-derives asset paths from $XDG_DATA_HOME because "the hub process may lack RYOKU_SHELL_DIR". These environment escapes are load-bearing.

storeMode is a live-but-dead code path. StorePage.qml is the only caller and it passes storeMode: true, which hides the tab row and the refresh button — so in the shipped product the entire Installed view (the only part of this file containing settings) is unreachable from here; Add-ons owns it via AddonsPage.qml + PluginSettingsForm.qml. The Installed half is either duplicated logic to reconcile or a rewrite's dead weight; either way, deciding is a product call, not a mechanical one.

Name collisions that resist mechanical refactoring: `place` is simultaneously a function on `page`, a readonly property on the card delegate (modelData.placement), and a property on PluginPlacementEditor. Inside the delegate only the explicit `page.place(...)` qualification keeps it working — and `pragma ComponentBehavior: Bound` plus `required property var modelData` are what make the nested Repeaters resolve `card` at all.

The Update affordance is derived, not stored: it exists only when cmpSemver(catalog.version, manifest.version) > 0, i.e. it depends on joining two independently-fetched async sources. It also reflows a sibling — the description Text width is hand-computed as `card.width - (updateFor(...) !== "" ? 210 : 120)`, so a version bump upstream silently changes text elision on an unrelated row. And `busyId` is a single page-wide string, so any in-flight install disables every Update button on the page at once.

Mixed input idioms with different semantics: TapHandler+HoverHandler on most controls, but MouseArea+containsMouse on exactly the two that need an enabled/disabled state (updMa, actMa, rmMa). Unifying them changes hover and hit-test behaviour.

Third hosts are silently dropped. The "Show as" filter hard-codes `h === "framePopout" || h === "desktopWidget"`, so a manifest declaring any other host loses it with no UI trace — while PluginStoreDetail's "PLACES WHERE IT LIVES" chips render manifest.hosts unfiltered. The store advertises hosts the manager refuses to offer.
GpuPage.qml: Mode is INFERRED, never stored. There is no `mode` key on disk. gpumode.go readAQValue() regex-scrapes `AQ_DRM_DEVICES", "([^"]*)"` out of gpu.lua, then classifyMode() splits the value on ':', takes the primary segment, and matches it against the LIVE PCI slots of the detected dGPU/iGPU (slot 0000:01:00.0 -> udev token 0000-01-00-0) to decide hybrid/performance/passthrough. A declarative 'read key -> show value' binding is impossible: reading the current value requires a hardware probe, and 'hybrid' is represented by the ABSENCE of the line.

Writing is asymmetric and not a value assignment: hybrid runs cmd_disable (deletes the pin), performance runs cmd_persist with RYOKU_GPU_FORCE=1 (pins whatever ryoku-gpu decides is strongest — the Hub does not choose the slot), passthrough runs cmd_persist_solo on the discovered iGPU. The same 'setting' maps to three different write strategies, and the written value is computed by an external shell script, not by the page.

The write can be REFUSED after the click. setGpuMode() re-probes hardware and rejects passthrough when wouldStrandDisplay() (dGPU drives the screen, iGPU does not) with a paragraph-long BIOS/MUX message on stderr. Because ChoiceRow binds `current: page.mode` and page.mode only updates after a reload round-trip, the segment never moves optimistically — the control is request-then-re-read, not bind-and-write. Any rewrite that adds optimistic local state will show a mode the machine is not in.

No preview and no immediate effect: the change lands in gpu.lua but Hyprland reads env only at login, so the truth of the setting is deferred to the next session. previewDirty is hardcoded false. The backend even computes a `cost` field (relogin | reboot) that the QML throws away.

gpu.lua is declared read-only in the Hub's own ConfigViewer ('written by ryoku-gpu; regenerated on GPU or driver changes'), so this setting is a write-through to a file the settings system otherwise disowns — and ryoku-gpu can regenerate the file underneath it on a GPU/driver change.

The entire passthrough section is gated on a runtime verdict from a subprocess that can time out. `ryoku-hub gpu caps` shells out to `ryoku-gpu detect --json` under a 12s timeout with process-group kill + 2s WaitDelay (a wedged nvidia-smi can hold the stdout pipe). Nothing on the page has a static enabled/disabled state — visibility is a function of caps.enabled, caps.verdict, and four transient page booleans (planning, enabling, showChecks) plus two error strings (actionError, capsError).

Button visibility rules are compound and mutually exclusive in ways a declarative row list cannot express: 'Review changes' needs caps.enabled !== true && verdict !== 'incapable' && !planning && !enabling; 'Disable passthrough' needs caps.enabled === true; Enable/Close only exist while planning; Recheck only while enabling. Three of these depend on state that exists only because an external terminal is running.

The enable path leaves the process entirely. It execDetached()s a kitty window running `ryoku-hub gpu apply enable` (needs a TTY for the makepkg/AUR Looking Glass + kvmfr DKMS build and for pkexec), then a 4s Timer polls caps until the stack appears. There is no exit code, no progress, no failure signal back into the page — only the poll and a manual Recheck. A settings framework has nowhere to put 'spawn a terminal and poll'.

The plan console renders unstructured stdout from `--dry-run` (package lists, 'write /etc/...' / 'skip /etc/... (kvmfr module not installed)' lines, AUR notices). It is prose from a Go program, mono-wrapped at WrapAnywhere — not modellable as fields.

The readiness dossier is a variable-length, backend-authored table (9 possible check ids, 3 levels, per-machine label/value text, conditional rows). Its `hint` field — the only remediation text for every fail/warn — is carried in JSON and silently dropped by the page. A literal rewrite preserves a known information leak; a 'fix' changes product behaviour.

The action-error banner has no close affordance; it is dismissed by tapping the banner body (TapHandler), and its height is a clamped Math.min(implicitHeight + 22, 110) with 5-line elision — raw stderr can be truncated with no way to read the rest.

The layout is not a settings column. The page is a hard two-column split (fixed-width hero + Flickable), the caps-failure column anchors to the hero's edges rather than the content flow, and the error banner is anchored to the page bottom outside the Flickable. Dropping GpuPage into a generic scrolling settings shell would break all three.

GpuCard and CheckRow are inline/bespoke components with Canvas painting, cursor tracking, and parallax transforms — they carry the page's identity but have zero settings semantics, and GpuCard's sizing (0.4 * parent.width) is coupled to the page's own layout.

Copy is heavily interpolated with runtime hardware names (page.dgpuName falls back to 'the discrete GPU', page.renderName to 'your GPU') across six verdict strings, three mode explainers, and the section intro. These strings are product-critical guidance (BIOS/MUX instructions, the ryovm scope boundary) and cannot be reduced to generic setting descriptions.
AddonsPage.qml: The entire plugin settings surface is RUNTIME-DEFINED, not static. `PluginSettingsForm` renders whatever `manifest.metadata.settings` declares — keys, labels, types, options, mins, maxes, defaults, groups all come off disk from third-party manifests. No rewrite can enumerate these as declarative settings; the schema-driven renderer itself is the contract and must survive intact, including its type dispatch (choice/toggle/slider/image/text+unknown-fallback) and its per-type fallbacks (min 0, max 1, step 0.01, decimals 2).

There are NO shipped plugin manifests anywhere in this repo (`find . -name manifest.json -path '*plugin*'` returns nothing) — plugins live in ~/.local/share/ryoku/plugins or $RYOKU_PLUGINS_DIR at runtime. So the concrete option sets, defaults, and ranges of the schema-driven rows are unverifiable from source. A rewrite has no fixture to test against unless one is authored.

"Show as" is an enum whose option list is data, not code: it is manifest.hosts mapped through a two-case pretty-printer. Only framePopout and desktopWidget have display names; a plugin declaring a third host renders its raw key. Hard-coding a two-value enum in a rewrite would silently break any plugin with a novel host.
"Show as" is conditionally invisible (hosts.length > 1). A declarative settings model with no visibility predicate will surface a dead single-option control for most plugins.

Default drift between two writers: the QML shows enabled=false for an absent entry, seed_entry writes `enabled: // false`, but the `host`, `desktopWidget`, and `framePopout` subcommands of ryoku-plugins-place write `enabled: // true`. The effective default of `enabled` depends on which command touched the entry first — changing "Show as" on a never-enabled plugin silently turns it ON.

Enabling has a side effect beyond its own key: `enabled true` triggers seed_entry, which materialises the plugin's whole default settings block into plugins.json. A rewrite that models `enabled` as a plain bool write loses the seeding and the plugin comes up with no settings.

Every write is an out-of-process shell invocation (`ryoku-plugins-place`) followed by a full re-read (`discover.sh --all` reparses every manifest on disk). There is no in-process config object, no binding, no optimistic state at the page level — the round-trip IS the state update. PluginSettingsForm papers over this with a local deep-cloned copy; AddonsPage's own two rows do not.

`page.sel` is a detached snapshot of a list element, kept in sync by `reselect()` scanning page.plugins by id after each refresh. If an id disappears (removal, manifest error) sel goes stale and the detail view renders against dead data. This is an identity/lifetime problem a declarative model has to solve explicitly.

Settings are written one key at a time as a JSON string built by `JSON.stringify({[key]: value})` and merged jq-side. Types survive only because jq --argjson reparses them; the `enabled` toggle already round-trips a bool through a string. Any rewrite touching serialisation risks writing "true" as a string into plugins.json.

REMOVE is irreversible, unconfirmed, and dual-path: it drops the config entry (`forget`) and uninstalls the files (`ryoku-hub extras pluginremove`) as two independent processes with no transaction. If the second fails, the plugin remains on disk with its placement erased.

`updateFor()` depends on a hand-rolled semver comparator and on catProc having completed. Before the catalogue loads, or when either version string is missing, the UPDATE button silently does not exist — it is not a disabled state, it is absent.

`busyId` is a single page-wide string acting as a mutex across install and remove. It gates the UPDATE TapHandler (`enabled: page.busyId === ""`) but NOT the REMOVE TapHandler — remove is clickable mid-update.

Detail view vs grid view is a bare string state machine (`page.view`) with both Flickables always instantiated and toggled by `visible`. It is not a tab bar, not a StackView, and has no history — the header tap is the only way back.

The image field writes a `file://` URL (from FileDialog.selectedFile) while the text field writes a bare string. Whatever consumes these settings must tolerate both shapes; the form does no normalisation.

SliderRow's `percent` unit affordance exists but is never wired from the schema, so a plugin cannot declare a percentage slider — a latent gap a rewrite may be tempted to "fix" and thereby change existing readouts.

The desktopWidget/framePopout geometry (x, y, scale, locked, edge, align, hoverW, hoverH) is real persisted placement state in the SAME plugins.json entries, but this page deliberately does NOT expose it — it defers to wallpaper drag and a right-click menu, and merely prints a hint. A rewrite "completing" the placement section would duplicate a surface that lives elsewhere (PluginPlacementEditor.qml).
FastfetchPage.qml: THE INFO ROWS ARE NOT A FIXED SETTING SET. The Repeater's model is page.model.rows — a runtime list read off the user's own config.jsonc, of arbitrary length, with per-row kind. There is no defaults literal for it in the QML. A declarative rewrite cannot enumerate these rows at design time; it must keep the data-driven list. The seeded set (shipped ryoku/apps/fastfetch/config.jsonc) is: break, title, tagline ("RYOKU · 力 · a hand-built Arch desktop"), break, header VITALS, cpu/CPU, gpu/GPU (detectionMethod pci), memory/MEMORY, disk/DISK, break, header SYSTEM, command/OS, command/BRANCH, kernel/KERNEL, command/WM, shell/SHELL, packages/PKGS, break, header SESSION, uptime/UPTIME, command/AGE, terminal/TERM, break, colors (symbol circle, paddingLeft 2), break. The no-config fallback (backend defaultFastfetchModel) is instead: os/OS, kernel/KERNEL, cpu/CPU, memory/MEMORY, uptime/UPTIME with logo.kind=builtin.

RAW JSON PASSTHROUGH. Every row carries an opaque `raw` blob of its original JSON so unknown fields survive (gpu's detectionMethod, a command module's `text`, colors' paddingLeft). ffBuildRow starts from Raw and only overlays `key`. A rewrite that regenerates rows from typed fields WILL silently drop hand-added modules and their extras. The shipped config's four `command` rows (OS, BRANCH, WM, AGE) exist ONLY as raw — the Hub has no concept of a command module, cannot create one, and cannot edit its shell text.

DISABLING A ROW IS DATA LOSS. `enabled` has no on-disk representation; buildFastfetch drops disabled rows from modules[] entirely. Toggle off + Save = the row cannot be recovered by reloading. A declarative model with a persisted bool per row would change behaviour; keeping the current behaviour means a toggle that silently deletes.

SAVE REWRITES THE WHOLE FILE AND LOSES COMMENTS. Load runs stripJSONC over config.jsonc; save re-marshals via json.MarshalIndent. The shipped file's explanatory comments in the `display` block, its key ordering, and its inline formatting are destroyed by the first Save. The file is nominally "user-owned and hand-editable", but the round-trip is not comment-preserving and not byte-idempotent.

CUSTOM LINES ARE REBUILT FROM TEMPLATES, NOT PRESERVED. tagline and header rows discard their original format string and regenerate it from ffTaglineFormat/ffHeaderFormat with hardcoded brand colours (tan 143;135;112, bright 243;237;225, dim 58;46;36) and a 32-rune header rule. Any hand-tuned styling on a custom line is overwritten on Save.

KIND INFERENCE IS A HEURISTIC THAT CAN FLIP. A `custom` module is classified header vs tagline purely by whether its ANSI-stripped format contains "─". Type a "─" into a tagline's text and on the next load it comes back as a header (and gets re-rendered with the header template). Round-trip stability depends on user text content.

LOGO TYPE MAPPING IS LOSSY IN BOTH DIRECTIONS. chafa / sixel / iterm / kitty-icat / raw all read back as "image" and are written back as "kitty-direct"; file-raw / data / data-raw all become "file"; "small" becomes "builtin". A user who chose chafa loses it on the first Save even if they never touch the emblem.
LOGO PADDING TOP/RIGHT ARE HARDCODED TO 5 ON WRITE and only padding.left is read back. Custom top/right padding is clobbered every Save.

IMPORT USES A FIXED DESTINATION FILENAME (~/.config/fastfetch/ryoku-logo.<ext>). Importing a second image with the same extension overwrites the first, and because logo.source is byte-identical the QML Image will not reload from cache — the preview keeps showing the old art. SVG always lands on ryoku-logo.png regardless of the original name.

NO ERROR SURFACE ANYWHERE. `get` parse failure only console.logs (the page stays on its placeholder model and looks empty); save/import/preview Process objects have no stderr collector and no exit-code handling. A missing SVG rasterizer ("no SVG rasterizer found"), an unwritable config, or a missing `fastfetch` binary all fail silently in the UI.
SAVE OPTIMISTICALLY MARKS CLEAN. page.save() sets committed = clone() and bumps rev immediately, before saveProc has run, so the bar reads "Saved · config.jsonc" even if the write failed.

HAND-ROLLED DIRTY TRACKING. `dirty` is a JSON.stringify deep-compare of two untyped JS blobs, artificially invalidated by `void page.rev` and a manual rev++ in every mutator. page.model is a plain JS object, not QML properties — every setting is a path into an untyped blob (m.logo[k], m.rows[i][k]) reassigned wholesale via clone/commitModel. A per-property declarative model is a rewrite of the entire state layer, not a control swap.

DELEGATE CHURN vs THE INLINE EDITOR. Every commit reassigns page.model, so the INFO Repeater rebuilds all delegates and destroys the TextInput being edited. The inline editor commits on editingFinished only; reordering or toggling a row while a field is focused can drop the in-flight edit. Rows have no stable id — identity is the array index.

PREVIEW IS A SUBPROCESS PER EDIT. Each change serialises the whole model to argv and forks `ryoku-hub` -> a temp file -> real `fastfetch`, throttled at 90ms. Whole-model-as-argv has a size ceiling; the preview also cannot be made synchronous or pure.

THE PREVIEW IS NOT THE ARTEFACT. It runs `--logo none`, so logo width/height/padding never appear; the emblem is a fixed 84x84 QML Image, not the kitty graphics protocol or chafa output the terminal actually uses. parseAnsi handles only 38;2;r;g;b / bold / reset — background colours, 256-colour, italics or underline in a hand-added custom line render wrong or vanish.

ACCENT IS THREE THINGS AT ONCE: an ANSI triple in the model, a hex colour in the control, and simultaneously display.color.keys AND a baked-in substring of every custom line's format. It cannot be expressed as one leaf write.

COLORFIELD'S PRESETS ARE WRONG HERE. The component hardcodes 8 dark shell-surface swatches (#1a1b26 etc.) that are useless as a readout accent; the brand default #e2342a is not among them. Fixing this requires changing the shared ColorField or not using it.
ROWS WITH NO RAW ARE SILENTLY DROPPED. ffBuildRow returns nil for title/colors/raw kinds lacking Raw, so such a row disappears on Save without a warning.
A MODULE KEY CANNOT BE CLEARED (`if r.Key != ""` skips the overlay, leaving the stale key in Raw).

CATALOG/SHIPPED OVERLAP CONFUSION. Adding "os" from the Add menu creates a real `os` module, but the shipped "OS" row is a `command` running `ryoku version` — same visible label, different mechanism. Likewise "__colors" adds {type:colors, symbol:circle} without the shipped row's paddingLeft:2. Five catalog modules (host, de, battery, localip, board) appear in no shipped config.
THE `title` ROW IS VISIBLE AND REORDERABLE BUT NOT EDITABLE (rowEditable excludes it); its user@host format string is preserved only as Raw.
UNSAVED EDITS DIE SILENTLY ON NAVIGATION. Component.onDestruction only stops the throttle — no prompt, no persistence, despite the comment acknowledging it.

TWO COMPETING WRITERS. The Hub page and the Hub header's "Edit config.jsonc" (opening the raw file) both write config.jsonc, with no file-watch, no reload-on-change and no conflict detection: an external edit made while the page is open is overwritten by Save.
CreditsPage.qml: There is nothing to migrate to a settings model: the page defines zero config keys, zero defaults, and zero persistence. Any rewrite that treats this as a settings page will invent state that does not exist. The correct rewrite target is a content/showcase page.

projects[] and testers[] are hardcoded `readonly property var` literals inside the QML, not data loaded from a config/JSON file. Moving them to disk would change them from compile-time constants into user-mutable state (and into something a user could edit to fake a credit). If they stay inline, the rewrite must transcribe all 13 project entries and all 5 tester entries exactly — names, authors, role strings, and URLs are load-bearing attribution.

Empty-string `url` is meaningful semantics, not missing data: inir, Omarchy, and CachyOS are deliberately QUIET credits (comment: "no home was given, and we don't invent one"). A rewrite that fills in plausible URLs, or that treats "" as null/absent and hides the row, breaks the intent. `linked` gates the arrow glyph, the hover cursor, and the tap handler together.

The Three Graces left-edge dissolve is baked into the PNG's alpha ramp (art/three-graces.png). It cannot be reproduced with an opacity binding or a gradient overlay — the comment explicitly forbids a fade rectangle. The asset must ship with the rewrite, and the negative rightMargin (-parent.width * 0.02) plus width==height square box are what put the bleed off the right edge.

Editorial design constraints are expressed only in comments and are invisible to any automated diff: "never boxed cards" for CreditLine, one vermillion accent "used as a dot, never a wash", testers made special "by its own section and the ringed initial, not by any colour wash". A rewrite can pass every structural check and still violate the page's whole reason for existing.

Layout is driven by a hand-tuned formula, not a layout engine: leftW = Math.min(page.width * 0.60, page.width - 430) reserves exactly 430px for the art at wide sizes and flips to 60% when narrow. Grid cellW = (width - columnSpacing) / 2 depends on it. Substituting a Layout will silently change the art/text balance.

A single shared `Process { id: opener }` is reused for every xdg-open. Rapid taps on two different rows mutate `command` on a running process; there is no queue, no error surface, and no feedback if xdg-open fails. Behavior under concurrent taps is undefined and will differ if the rewrite swaps in per-invocation processes.

TesterChip's TapHandler is NOT gated on url length (CreditLine's is), and its HoverHandler is always enabled — so an empty-url tester would show a pointer cursor and a dead click. Currently masked because all 5 testers have URLs. This asymmetry is easy to "fix" or to accidentally cement during a rewrite.

Repeater-generated rows use `required property var modelData` under `pragma ComponentBehavior: Bound`; the two inline components (CreditLine, TesterChip) reach out to `page.openUrl` and to the outer `hov`/`thov` ids. Extracting them into separate files breaks those scope references and the pragma contract.

Japanese glyph rendering depends on Theme.fontJp for 感謝 and 力; the mixed-script Row relies on bottom-alignment with a 6px optical nudge on the Latin word. A font substitution or a Layout change wrecks the baseline relationship. Text is hardcoded English/Japanese with no i18n hook.

Roughly twenty distinct Theme tokens (bright/sun/cream/subtle/dim/faint/brand/ember/line/lineSoft/lineStrong/surfaceLo/radius/quick/font/fontJp/mono/display) form an implicit hierarchy — cream vs bright on hover, line vs lineSoft vs lineStrong for hairline weight. These read as interchangeable greys but encode the whole visual ladder.

Every hover transition is a Behavior/ColorAnimation on Theme.quick (name, arrow, hairline, chip fill, chip border, ring border, monogram). They are per-property and animate together; a rewrite to states/transitions changes the feel even when the end colors match.
DisplaysPage.qml: NO STATIC DEFAULTS EXIST. There is no `defaults` object literal and no QtObject draft property anywhere in the file (grep-confirmed).
