Every default — Enabled, Resolution, Scale, Rotation, Adaptive sync, Mirror of, X, Y — is produced by clone() from live `ryoku-monitor list` (i.e. `hyprctl monitors all -j`) at load. A declarative rewrite has nothing to declare a default *to*; the only fixed value in the whole system is the shipped catch-all in monitors.lua: `hl.monitor({ output = "", mode = "highrr", position = "auto", scale = 1 })`.

SETTINGS ARE PER-MONITOR RECORDS, NOT SINGLETON KEY PATHS. There is no `displays.scale` — there is `monitors[i].scale`, and the array is addressed on disk by HARDWARE IDENTITY (`id` = "make|model|serial", built in cmd_list), not by connector name or index. A settings framework keyed on flat paths cannot express this; profile matching, applied-layout recall, and DP-1/DP-2 swap tolerance all depend on that id.

THE PAGE IS TRANSACTIONAL, NOT AUTOSAVING. Every control writes only to `page.draft`; nothing touches hardware or disk until Apply/Save. This is a deliberate safety property, stated in the file's header comment ("edits stage in the canvas + only touch real displays on Apply, so fiddling never nukes your screens"). A framework that persists on change would destroy the page's core guarantee — you cannot make these settings live.

THE `tick` REACTIVITY HACK. draft is a plain JS array mutated IN PLACE (setField/setMode/dragMonitor mutate; draft is never reassigned after load), so QML sees no property change. Every single binding launders itself through `void page.tick;` to force re-evaluation — present in `dirty`, `view()`, `mirrorOptions()`, and the value/current binding of all 8 per-monitor controls. A declarative rewrite needs a real model type (QAbstractListModel / ListModel / value-typed struct); dropping `tick` without that silently freezes the entire UI.
TWO CONTROLS, ONE DISK KEY: X and Y both serialise into the single `position` string "<x>x<y>". Neither exists independently on disk.

ONE CONTROL, FOUR DRAFT KEYS: setMode() writes mode + width + height + refresh. width/height/refresh are derived state (never emitted to disk) that nonetheless drive footW/footH, the canvas tile geometry, snapping, and the tile's sub-label.

TWO DYNAMIC, HARDWARE-DERIVED ENUMS. `Resolution` options come from the panel's advertised availableModes (parsed, deduped, sorted at runtime); `Mirror of` options are SELF-REFERENTIAL — derived from the other rows in the draft and re-filtered by the current selection and by other monitors' Enabled state. Neither can be a static option list.

SILENT SCALE MISMATCH. The Scale field offers 0.5–3 @ 0.25, but Hyprland accepts only 1/120 multiples yielding whole logical pixels for that panel and snaps otherwise (helper's snap() searches 120/120..360/120). So the UI value is a request; the applied value is read back from hardware on the next list. 0.5 is unreachable — below the helper's snap floor of 1.0. The page never surfaces the discrepancy.

STRING/INT TYPE LAUNDERING. Rotation and Adaptive sync are ChoiceRows whose keys are STRINGS ("0"/"1"/"2"/"3") but whose disk values are INTs — parseInt(k) on write, String(...) on read. A typed enum binding must reproduce the round-trip exactly.

OMIT-WHEN-ZERO / OMIT-WHEN-EMPTY EMISSION. The Lua writer emits `transform` only when != 0, `vrr` only when != 0, and `mirror` only when != "" and != "none". Absence IS the default. A writer that emits every key unconditionally would change generated monitors.lua for every user.

MIRROR IS TRI-SPELLED. The helper's `list` emits "none", the draft/UI uses "", and the Lua emitter omits the key entirely — three encodings of one state, converted at clone() (`(mon.mirror && mon.mirror !== "none") ? mon.mirror : ""`) and again in apply_specs.

ENABLED IS INVERTED AND LOSSY. Label "Enabled", key `disabled`, negated in both directions. When disabled=true the emitter writes ONLY `{ output, disabled = true }` — mode/position/scale/transform/vrr/mirror are dropped from monitors.lua, though they DO survive in the draft and in the JSON snapshot.

VRR=2 IS NOT ROUND-TRIP STABLE. `apply`/`save` (write_monitors_explicit) preserve `vrr = 2`, but write_monitors_conf — the `persist`/`autoscale`/`mirror` path — can only ever emit `vrr = 1` (it branches on truthiness: `if .vrr then ", vrr = 1"`). A Fullscreen choice silently degrades to On after any later autoscale.

DIRECT MANIPULATION IS A PEER WRITER OF x/y. The drag canvas mutates the same draft fields as the X/Y NumberFields, then post-processes them with edge snapping (threshold max(8, 22/k)) and a global normalize() re-base. Geometry is not a view of the settings — it is a co-equal input, with its own frozen-viewport state machine (page.dragging/page.frozen). This cannot be expressed as a declarative field binding.

X/Y BOUNDS ARE ADVISORY. The NumberFields declare from:0, but dragMonitor() adds raw deltas with no clamp, so x/y go negative mid-drag; only endDrag()'s normalize() re-bases them. The 20000 ceiling exists only in the fields, not in the drag path.

QUICK ACTIONS BYPASS AND CLOBBER THE DRAFT. Mirror / Extend / DPI auto-scale hit hardware immediately, then listRefresh (700ms) reloads the draft — silently discarding unapplied edits with no dirty check and no confirmation, even while the action bar reads "Unapplied layout changes".

DPI AUTO-SCALE AND EXTEND ARE DESTRUCTIVE TO PERSISTED STATE. Both reach `cmd_autoscale --no-profile`, which `rm -f`s ~/.config/ryoku/monitors-applied.json — deliberately discarding the user's last Apply so DPI wins at next login. A button labelled "DPI auto-scale" deleting the saved layout is non-obvious and has no confirmation.

APPLY IS OPTIMISTIC AND UNVERIFIED. page.committed is set BEFORE applyProc completes; no exit status, stdout, or stderr is ever inspected. A failed apply still clears the dirty flag and reports "Layout matches your displays". There is no error surface anywhere on the Apply path (the only failure UI in the file is the `list` parse catch).

SAVE AND APPLY PERSIST TO DIFFERENT PLACES. Apply writes monitors.lua + monitors-applied.json. Save writes monitors.lua + monitors/<name>.json but NOT monitors-applied.json. Both clear the dirty flag identically, so the UI presents two divergent persistence paths as one state.

PROFILE NAMES ARE UNSANITISED FILENAMES. saveProfile() only rejects whitespace-only and calls .trim(); the string then becomes `$monitors_dir/$name.json` in the helper. Slashes, dots, and shell-ish names reach the filesystem. (The Lua injection vector is separately defended — apply_specs uses jq's @json for .output/.mirror — but the filename path is not.)

TIMER-BASED REFRESH RACES THE HELPER. listRefresh (700ms) and profileRefresh (300ms) are fixed guesses standing in for process completion; nothing waits on Process exit. A slow hyprctl reloads stale state.

THE SECTION TITLE IS DATA. The per-monitor SettingSection's title is page.sel.name (the connector, e.g. "DP-1"), with the literal "DISPLAY" only as a null fallback — a group header bound to runtime state, which most declarative section schemas assume is static.

SELECTION IS AMBIENT, INDEX-BASED STATE. All 8 per-monitor controls read/write through page.selected (an int index into draft) rather than being bound to a record. selected survives reloads only via a bounds check (`if (page.selected >= d.length) page.selected = 0`), so a hotplug can silently retarget every control at a different monitor.

THE FILE IT WRITES IS FULLY REGENERATED AND IS NOT THE USER-EDITABLE ONE. ~/.config/hypr/monitors.lua is rewritten in full on every apply/save ("Edits may be overwritten"). User overrides live in a separate hand-written ~/.config/hypr/monitors_user.lua, require()d afterwards so it wins — and any output named there is PINNED: autoscale and settle_modes skip it entirely. This page has no awareness of pinning; it will happily present and stage settings for an output whose values monitors_user.lua silently overrides. (Hub.qml, not this page, provides the Edit-overrides/View-config affordance for those two files.)

DERIVED GLOBAL SIDE EFFECT NOT SHOWN IN THE UI: every write recomputes GDK_SCALE into monitors.lua (floor(scale) only when every enabled monitor shares one identical whole scale >= 2, else 1) and pushes it live via `hyprctl eval hl.env(...)`. The Scale control silently drives a global env var for GTK/XWayland apps with no UI representation.

SILENT PARSE FAILURES THROUGHOUT. modeOptions() drops any advertised mode not matching /^(\d+)x(\d+)@([\d.]+)/; the profiles StdioCollector swallows JSON errors into `profiles = []` with no message (unlike the list path, which at least sets listFailed and logs). Malformed helper output degrades to an empty UI rather than an error.
InputPage.qml: kbLayout is NOT a scalar — it is a composed comma string ("fr,us") multiplexed across two dropdowns (Layout = slot 0, Second layout = slot 1) via primaryLayout()/secondaryLayout()/setLayouts(). Worse, kbVariant is coupled to it positionally: setLayouts writes "<variant>," (trailing comma) whenever a secondary exists, because xkb aligns variants to layouts by position and the secondary must keep an empty slot. A naive per-key declarative rewrite breaks both.

kbOptions is a SINGLE comma string multiplexed across FOUR controls plus a free-text escape hatch. Caps Lock, Swap Alt/Super, Compose key and Switch layouts each own a 'family' of xkb tokens (capsIds/composeIds/grpIds/swapId); setOption() removes every member of a family and appends the chosen one; 'Extra options' round-trips everything knownIds() does not recognise so power users lose nothing. This is a set-membership model over a string, not four independent settings.

grpIds contains "grp:caps_toggle" but the 'Switch layouts' ChoiceRow offers only "", grp:alt_shift_toggle and grp:win_space_toggle. A caps_toggle set from outside the hub is therefore invisible (stripped from 'Extra options' as 'known', shown nowhere) and destroyed the first time the picker is touched. Any rewrite that derives the option list from grpIds will accidentally expose a fourth choice; any rewrite that derives knownIds from the option list will start leaking caps_toggle into the free-text field. The asymmetry is load-bearing and must be reproduced deliberately.
pickFrom() returns the FIRST recognised token, so a hand-written kbOptions with two members of one family silently shows only one and drops the other on the next edit.

Dropdown option models are populated asynchronously by subprocesses, so `current` may not resolve to a label for a beat. The Layout dropdown works around this with `placeholder: page.primaryLayout()` — the raw code renders until the model lands. That placeholder is a live binding, not a static string.

Variant labels are computed at runtime by variantLabel() with a regex (/^[^(]+\((.+)\)$/) that strips the redundant language prefix and title-cases the qualifier: "French (AZERTY)" -> "AZERTY", "Belgian (alt.)" -> "Alt.". Labels are derived, never enumerable at build time, and the model is 100% machine-dependent (parsed from /usr/share/X11/xkb/rules/base.lst, evdev.lst fallback).

refreshVariants() is guarded by variantsProc.forLayout — it refuses to refetch for the same layout string, and it early-returns on an empty layout. It is driven by a Connections onKbLayoutChanged plus Component.onCompleted. Rebuilding this as a plain reactive binding will either refetch on every keystroke or never refetch at all.

WHAT IS WRITTEN TO DISK IS DIVERGENCE-ONLY. genConfig (hypr.go:783) diffs every leaf against defaultOverrides() and emits ONLY the keys that differ, so a setting equal to its default writes literally nothing to settings.lua. The exception is kb_layout/kb_variant/kb_options: once o.inputSaved is true they are pinned unconditionally, because keyboard.lua loads BEFORE settings.lua and a diffed-away "us" would let it silently win over what the UI shows. Any rewrite that emits all keys, or that emits none when they match defaults, breaks a documented ordering bug.

The QML defaults are DUPLICATED, not derived: HyprStore.qml:56-80 hardcodes the input draft values and a comment says they mirror defaultOverrides() in the Go backend (hypr.go:288-299). Two sources of truth already drift-prone; a rewrite must keep both in step. The real reset baseline is a third source — the async `ryoku-hub hypr defaults` fetch into store.defaults.

Three settings have no config leaf at all. workspaceSwipe + swipeFingers together synthesise a whole `hl.gesture({ fingers = N, direction = "horizontal", action = "workspace" })` statement, present only when enabled, with the finger count floored at 3 in Go. They cannot be expressed as key=value.

Domain-shared mutable store: HyprStore holds the ENTIRE override document (appearance, cursor, lists, plugins, animations) and is instantiated per-page. dirty is a JSON.stringify deep-compare of the whole snapshot against `committed`, re-triggered by a manual `rev` counter (`void store.rev`) rather than by real property tracking. Save/Revert/Reset all operate on that whole-document snapshot; only Reset is domain-scoped.

Live preview has session-scoped side effects that outlive the page: edits are pushed into the running compositor immediately, and the ONLY thing that undoes them is the Hub noticing previewDirty on navigation away, or Revert shelling out to `ryoku-hub hypr restore`. Preview is not a pure function of the draft.
store.save() is optimistic — it sets committed = snapshot() immediately and never inspects saveProc's exit code. A failed save reports 'Saved · live on your desktop'.

Int-versus-string round-tripping: followMouse and swipeFingers are ints in the store but ChoiceRow keys are strings, so every read is String(...) and every write is parseInt(...). Type-preserving generic binding will produce "2" instead of 2 and silently diverge from committed, leaving the page permanently dirty.

Four TOUCHPAD rows and one KEYBOARD row use `visible:` (not `enabled:`) conditional gating, so they vanish and the section reflows. The gated grp: token behind 'Switch layouts' is NOT cleared when the second layout is removed — it stays in kbOptions, orphaned and unreachable.

Two label collisions that are NOT duplicates: 'Natural scroll' and 'Scroll speed' each appear once under POINTER and once under TOUCHPAD, backed by different keys (input.natural_scroll / input.scroll_factor vs input.touchpad.*). Deduping by label loses two real settings.

'Apply system-wide' escapes the whole draft/preview/save model: it is a privileged out-of-band localectl write to /etc (polkit prompt, cancellable), gated on the page being clean, with a self-clearing 6s transient status. It also passes the composed comma layout string and an empty model argument. It has no draft, no revert and no dirty state.

TextFieldRow's commit semantics are deliberate and easy to lose: commits on editingFinished only (not per keystroke, which would fight the tokeniser mid-word), and re-binds `text` to the draft on focus loss specifically so Reset/Revert can refresh the shown text after a manual edit.

The 80ms preview throttle with its previewPending coalescing flag exists to keep slider drags from flooding hyprctl eval; save() must stop the timer and clear the flag or a queued preview fires after the save.
ShellSettingsPage.qml: LIVE-WRITE MODEL, NOT APPLY-ON-SAVE: every edit() writes all three JSON files immediately (throttled 70ms with a trailing write, atomic). The "preview" IS the user's real desktop — the frame around the window and the bar riding it. Save only moves the committed baseline; it does not cause the write. A declarative form that writes on submit silently changes the product's core behaviour.

REVERT-ON-LEAVE LIFECYCLE: Component.onDestruction rewrites committedVals to disk when leaving dirty. Leaving the section or closing the hub undoes the preview. A rewrite needs an equivalent destruction/unmount hook or unsaved edits become permanent.

THREE FILES, THREE WATCH SEMANTICS, ONE FORM: shell.json (watchChanges:false), visualizer.json (watchChanges:true + onFileChanged:reload + adoptExternal merge), brand.json (watchChanges:false). page.ready gates on all three having loaded (or failed). Every flush() writes all three regardless of which key changed.

THREE-WAY MERGE (adoptExternal): when visualizer.json changes on disk (the shell deck flipping the visualiser on/off), only keys whose draft still equals its baseline are adopted forward; locally edited keys keep both draft and baseline. This is real conflict-resolution logic, not a binding.

GHOST KEYS WITH NO UI: islandEdge, islandAlong, islandHidden are in shellKeys — dirty-tracked, written by every flush(), restored by Revert, and RESET by "Reset to defaults" — but have no control anywhere on this page. The shell writes them when the user drags the Delos island (pill/DelosIsland.qml, pill/Singletons/IslandDock.qml). Because cfgShell has watchChanges:false, dragging the island while this page is open will be clobbered by the page's next flush, and "Reset to defaults" silently re-centres/unhides the island. A naive rewrite that only models visible controls will drop these three keys from the written file entirely.

onLoadFailed ADOPTS THE ADAPTER DEFAULTS AND MARKS LOADED: a missing or corrupt config is indistinguishable from a default one, and the first flush then writes a full file. Error state is deliberately invisible (printErrors:false).

HAND-MAINTAINED DEFAULTS MIRROR: page.defaults duplicates the shells' canonical defaults (ryoku/shell/quickshell/pill/Singletons/Config.qml + the visualizer Config.qml) a THIRD time — the QtObject draft and the JsonAdapters each restate the same values, so every default lives in 3-4 places in this one file. Verified in sync with pill Config.qml today; nothing enforces it. Only "Reset to defaults" reads page.defaults.

STRINGIFY-BASED DIRTY CHECK: sameVal(a,b) = String(a) === String(b). Arrays compare by their comma join, so islandModules/sidebarPanes dirty-detection is order-sensitive and type-blind; colors compare by their string form; numbers compare 1 vs "1" as equal. Any typed/deep-equal rewrite will change which edits register as dirty.

ORDERED LISTS EXPOSED AS CHECKBOXES: islandModules, sidebarLeftPanes and sidebarRightPanes are ordered (the order IS the pane/tab order, per the explainer text), but the UI only offers membership. Enable appends to the END, so toggling a pane off and back on silently moves it to the end of the user's tab order. There is no reorder affordance to preserve — but the append-to-end semantics must be.

CONDITIONAL SECTION: the whole ISLAND SettingSection is visible: draft.barStyle === "delos" — 8 settings (islandRadius + 7 module toggles) vanish on the other 9 bar styles while remaining live and resettable.

DYNAMIC, ASYNC ENUM: fontOptions is not a static list — it is fontCatalog filtered against an fc-list scan that lands after the page opens, with the current value force-injected (as a synthesised hint:"custom" entry, unshifted to the front) when it is not in the catalog. So the option set is machine-dependent and time-dependent, and a hand-set custom family must survive.

TYPE COERCION AT THE EDGE: fps is an int behind a string-keyed ChoiceRow (String(draft.fps) out, parseInt(k) in). surfaceColor is a QML `color` in the draft/adapter but a string on disk. markImage is a file: URL from FileDialog, but the preview accepts bare paths too, so both forms exist in the wild.

PERCENT SLIDERS OVER NON-0..1 RANGES: percent:true on gain (0.5-2 → reads 50-200%) and reflection (0-0.3 → tops out at 30%); frameSmoothing is the only non-percent SliderRow and overrides decimals to 0. The percent flag is presentation, not storage — the raw real is what is written.

BARE, COLLIDING KEY NAMES: the visualizer file uses enabled / height / bars / style / shape / position / segments at top level, and page.keys is a flat concat of shellKeys + vizKeys + brandKeys. There is no namespacing — a flat declarative key space will collide (e.g. viz `enabled` vs shell `barEnabled`, viz `height` vs `barHeight`) unless the file split is preserved.

RESET TO DEFAULTS = 66 SEQUENTIAL edit() CALLS, each of which can trigger a flush of all three files; it relies entirely on the throttle to coalesce, and it resets the three ghost island keys along with everything else.

PREVIEW/RUNTIME SPLIT: VizPreview deliberately models only some keys (style, shape, position, mirror, bars, height, thickness, bloom, reflection, enabled, peaks, segments) and deliberately ignores fps, adaptive, smoothing, gain and idleWave because they are runtime-only. It also clamps bars to 2..96 while the control allows 16..128, and only reflects for bars+bottom. Its colour ramp is representative, not the wallust ramp.

BRAND SCOPE CARVE-OUT: brand.json is consumed by the desktop shell and doctor, but Ryoku's own apps (the hub itself, ryowalls, ryovm) deliberately ignore it. Also note Hub.qml's "Edit config" affordance for this section lists only shell.json and visualizer.json — brand.json is not offered there.
Qt5Compat.GraphicalEffects ColorOverlay in the brand swatch is a legacy-module dependency inside an otherwise plain-QML page (the rest of the codebase's VizPreview uses QtQuick.Effects).

Draft state is page-level while tab bodies are Loader-instantiated Components (ComponentBehavior: Bound), so unsaved edits survive tab switches only because draft outlives the subtree — the crossfade re-runs on every switch.
RicesPage.qml: Everything is imperative subprocess I/O, not declarative config. Eleven Process objects shell out to `ryoku-hub rice` list|catalog|apply|restore|capture|delete|fork|install|setwall|files|export plus `xdg-open`. A declarative settings framework has nowhere to put any of it — there is no key to bind, only verbs to invoke.

EVERY onExited ignores its exit code. `(code, status) => page.reload()` at :151-157 — a failed apply, capture, delete, fork, install or setwall is indistinguishable from success; the UI just reloads and the rice silently didn't change. There is no error surface anywhere except the catalog's parse-failure flag.

All state is JSON parsed out of stdout inside try/catch with silent empty fallbacks (:126-131, :140-147, :162-169, :176-182). A torn/garbage payload reads as "you have no rices", not as an error. Only catalogProc sets an error flag (page.catalogError), and it flags PARSE failure, not process failure — a non-zero exit that prints nothing valid shows "Couldn't reach the rice store" only by accident of the parse failing.

implicitHeight is a two-way switch: `(selectedSlug !== "" && detailLoader.item) ? detailLoader.item.implicitHeight : browse.implicitHeight` (:47-49). The page is embedded in AppearancePage's outer Flickable and MUST size itself by content — a rewrite into a fixed-height or internally-scrolling settings list breaks scrolling for both the grid and the detail.

selectedSlug is navigation state doing double duty as a data trigger: onSelectedSlugChanged (:40-44) clears exportedTo AND fires loadFiles() — but only when !browseMode, so selecting from the store grid relies on browseMode being set false FIRST (:403-404) for the files fetch to happen at all. Order-dependent.
Destructive Delete with no confirmation, no undo, no destructive styling — one tap from the detail wipes ~/.config/ryoku/rices/<slug>/.

Layer plumbing exists but is not exposed. applyRice(slug, layers) concats layers into argv and the backend allowlists each of the 7 riceHyprLayers keys, yet the UI always passes ALL of the rice's keys (detail.layerKeys) and capture hardcodes the string "all" (:79). Looks like an unfinished multi-select. A rewrite will be tempted either to add per-layer toggles (behavior change, unrequested) or to drop the layers argv (silently stops applying keybinds/input/env/etc).

The rice IS a bundle of other pages' settings — hypr.json appearance/cursor/anim/plugins, ~30 shell.json keys (frameRadius…fontScale), launcher.json hero keys, theme.json palette. This page deliberately shows them as an opaque prose summary (changeSummary()) and a touched-file list. There is no per-key UI here and there must not be one; the inventory of those keys lives in the other Appearance tabs, not this contract.

`home` in exportPicker.startFolder (:451) is unqualified and resolves to ImagePicker's own `readonly property string home` — a scope leak that works only because the binding is evaluated inside the ImagePicker instance. Inlining or reparenting the picker in a rewrite silently yields "file://undefined".

file:// handling is string surgery: `("" + p).replace("file://", "")` at :441 and :453 — replaces the FIRST occurrence anywhere in the string, no URL decoding, so percent-encoded or oddly-named paths break.

The pickers/viewer are `anchors.fill: parent`, z:100 overlays scoped to THIS page's bounds, not to the Hub window. They dim only the Rices tab area. Both pickers manage `active` by hand (open() sets it; each onPicked/onCanceled clears it), so a rewrite that centralises modal state must preserve the manual close on both paths.

Store tile onOpened is overloaded — install when !installed, navigate when installed (:401-408) — one gesture, two very different outcomes, and it keys off modelData.id while the My-rices grid keys off modelData.slug.
Grid width is hardcoded 3-up: Math.max(280, (width - 14*2)/3) subtracts exactly two gaps regardless of how many tiles the Flow actually fits per row, so spacing drifts at other column counts.
Both galleries render the entire model with no search, no filter, no pagination and no virtualisation (Flow + Repeater) — every tile Image is realised at once (sourceSize.width 720 each).

Preview mockups read deep, optional, defaulted paths (rice.look.hypr.appearance.activeBorder, look.shell.surfaceColor, appearance.rounding) with two fallback tiers each, ending at Theme values and a literal rounding default of 8 (RiceTile.qml:22-45, RiceDetail.qml:31-54). Imperative getters that cannot be expressed as bound settings.

Two independent capture entry points that must stay in sync — Enter (onAccepted) and the Save button — and page.capturing simultaneously drives the wizard's visibility, the blurb's visibility, the empty state's visibility, the Save-current-setup button's enabled state, and the TextInput's focus grab.

Theme.* (radius, ember, line, quick, medium, ease, surfaceLo, bright, faint, dim, cream, subtle, keyTop, brand, ok, bad, hair, font, mono) is a Singleton dependency threaded through every visual in the page and both child components.
LayerRulesPage.qml: WHOLE-DOCUMENT SAVE: HyprStore holds the ENTIRE hypr override doc (appearance, input, cursor, env, windowRules, appOverrides, autostart, keybinds, anim.items, anim.curves, plugins) and save() marshals store.snapshot() — all of it — to `ryoku-hub hypr save`. A rewrite that only models layerRules and posts a partial doc will silently clobber every other page's settings. Same for revert(), which re-adopts the whole committed doc.

NOT A SETTINGS PAGE IN THE DECLARATIVE SENSE: zero SettingSection groups, zero tabs, zero ToggleRow/SliderRow/NumberField/ChoiceRow/ColorField/SettingField/Segmented. The entire page is one unbounded ordered list editor. There are no N fixed keys to enumerate — a schema-driven rewrite has to special-case a repeatable row type.

ORDER IS SEMANTIC: index i becomes the generated Lua rule name `ryoku-layer-<i+1>`. Reordering, filtering, or keying rows by identity instead of array index changes the emitted file. The UI offers no drag-reorder, so index is purely positional/append-order.

SIDE-EFFECTING ENUM: setAction() is not a plain field patch — it rewrites a SIBLING field, seeding value="0.5" for ignorealpha and clearing value="" for every valueless action, specifically so nothing stale lingers in the draft. A naive `patch(i, "action", k)` binding loses this and leaves orphaned values in hypr.json.

CONDITIONAL FIELD DRIVEN BY A LOOKUP TABLE: `value` exists only when action is in valueActions === ["ignorealpha"]. The comment on line 23 records that dimaround was recently demoted from valued to plain bool — so valueActions is a moving target, and hardcoding "ignorealpha only" in a rewrite re-freezes a list that is meant to be data.

COMMIT-ON-BLUR: both TextInputs write via onEditingFinished (blur/Enter), never per keystroke. Text typed into a still-focused field is NOT in the draft, so it does not mark the store dirty and is NOT included if the user clicks Save while the field holds focus. Any rewrite that changes to live binding changes dirty-tracking semantics; any that keeps it inherits the lost-keystroke bug.

DIRTY IS A DEEP JSON DIFF: `dirty` = JSON.stringify(snapshot()) !== JSON.stringify(committed), gated on store.ready and manually re-triggered by a `rev` counter (void store.rev). It is key-order-sensitive and depends on patch()/setAction() rebuilding objects via Object.assign in a stable key order. A rewrite using different object construction can produce phantom-dirty or never-dirty states.

NO LIVE PREVIEW BY DESIGN: editList() deliberately omits queuePreview() (unlike edit() and editAnim(), which do preview). The file header and the status text both promise "applied on Save". The store is fully capable of previewing — the restraint is intentional and easy to 'helpfully' break in a rewrite.

SILENT NO-OP RULES: a rule with an empty/whitespace namespace or an empty action is happily stored in hypr.json but genLayerRule returns "" and emits nothing. The UI gives no validation, no warning, no indication — the intro text's "a namespace that matches nothing has no effect" is the only hint. Rules are also never verified against actually-present layer-shell namespaces.

LEGACY "noshadow" ORPHAN: the backend still switches on Action=="noshadow" for layer rules and deliberately returns "" (layer surfaces lost the shadow effect in Hyprland's rule rewrite; emitting the field would kill the whole generated file). It is NOT in page.actionOptions — so an existing hypr.json carrying it renders the Dropdown as the bare placeholder "Select…" (labelFor falls through) with no explanation, and the rule silently does nothing. Note "noshadow" IS still a valid WINDOW-rule action in WindowRulesPage.qml, so the two pages' option sets diverge on the same key name.

STRINGLY-TYPED NUMERIC: value is stored as a string ("0.5"), its 0.0-1.0 range exists only as placeholder hint text, and nothing clamps or validates it client-side. Range enforcement is the backend's parseFloat(r.Value, 0.5) fallback. Modelling this as a real/min/max SliderRow in a rewrite silently changes both the on-disk type and the coercion behaviour.

IMPERATIVE LAYOUT MATH: nsW = width - 24 - ddW - 30 - gap*(needsValue ? 3 : 2) - (needsValue ? valW : 0). The namespace field's width is hand-derived from the sibling set and reflows when the action enum changes. A Layout-based rewrite must reproduce the same reflow-on-enum-change behaviour.

REVERT IS NOT A DRAFT RESET: store.revert() re-adopts committed AND shells out to `ryoku-hub hypr restore`, forcing a full config regen + compositor reload — because hyprctl eval cannot push some keywords back to a default or re-derive wallpaper-derived border colours. It is a global, cross-page side effect fired from this page's button.

NO PER-DOMAIN RESET: the store exposes resetAppearance()/resetInput()/resetPlugins() but NO resetLayerRules(). This page's only 'reset' is the destructive "Clear all" (empty the list). There is no shipped-defaults baseline to restore to, which is also why Hub.qml gives this page an empty `view` file list.

ASYNC READINESS: rules render from store.layerRules while `ready` is still false (the `hypr get` Process has not returned), and adopt() replaces the array wholesale when it lands. Rows can appear, then be swapped underneath an in-progress edit; dirty stays false until ready flips.

DROPDOWN POPUP INSIDE A FLICKABLE+REPEATER: the option list is a QtQuick Controls Popup positioned relative to its field (y below, right-aligned), living inside a clipped, scrollable Repeater delegate. Stacking, clipping and scroll-while-open behaviour are fragile and will not survive a naive re-parent.
ProfilePage.qml: The whole page is an anchor chain hung off `hero`, not a Row/Layout: the gutter rule and ProfileStats both anchor to hero.right / hero.top / hero.bottom. Nothing here is expressible as a declarative setting; reflowing it into a layout container silently changes the composition (the dossier is deliberately height-bound to the card, not to the page).

`cardW` is load-bearing layout math with a three-way clamp: `Math.round(Math.min((page.height - 44) * 0.585, page.width * 0.42, 440))`. It is height-driven (the card is height-bound, width derived by the 0.585 aspect ratio), capped at 42% of page width so the dossier has room, and hard-capped at 440. The magic numbers 44 / 0.585 / 0.42 / 440 have no config key and no defaults object anywhere — they only exist as this expression.

The left-edge fade hardcodes `Theme.bgBot` as its opaque gradient stop. It only works because it matches the ShowcaseBackdrop's bottom color; if the backdrop's palette or gradient direction changes, the fade turns into a visible colored bar over the art. This is an implicit cross-component coupling that no schema will capture.

The Justice art is intentionally clipped off-canvas via a NEGATIVE anchor margin (`anchors.rightMargin: -parent.width * 0.04`) combined with `width: height` + PreserveAspectFit. A naive rewrite that 'fixes' the negative margin or the square sizing destroys the bleed-off-the-edge motif.

Two `layer.enabled: true` + MultiEffect wrappers (dim/desaturate on the art, drop shadow with autoPaddingEnabled on the hero). Layer effects are render-pipeline state, not properties: they interact with clipping, sizing (autoPaddingEnabled grows the item), and GPU cost, and they cannot be toggled from a config value without restructuring.

ProfileCard's tilt/foil is imperative pointer-driven state (onPositionChanged writes five root properties; onExited resets them). It is a hover interaction with no on/off key and no default — there is no `defaults` object literal or QtObject draft anywhere in the Profile subtree to derive one from.

Relative asset path `art/marble-justice.png` resolves against the QML file's own directory. Moving ProfilePage.qml during a rewrite breaks the image with no compile-time error — it just renders empty.

`pragma ComponentBehavior: Bound` at the top: required because the page is loaded as a Component by Hub.qml. Dropping it during a rewrite changes id-resolution semantics inside any delegate/Component scope.
AppearancePage.qml: TWO SAVE MODELS ON ONE PAGE. Look/Borders/Cursor are a draft + dirty action bar (Save/Revert/Reset to defaults). Wallpaper/Comfort/Rices apply INSTANTLY and have no bar at all (gated by `storeTab`). Theme palette, wallpaper, brightness and night light never touch store.dirty. A uniform declarative settings page erases this split.

TWO PREVIEW MODELS INSIDE the store tabs. store.edit() previews live via `ryoku-hub hypr preview` (hyprctl eval) throttled at 80ms; store.editPlugin() does NOT preview — plugin rows only land on Save/reload (hence the four "Applies on Save." texts). Visually identical rows in the SAME section behave differently: SHAPE has live `rounding` sitting directly above save-only `hyprscrolling.columnWidth`.

WHOLE-DOCUMENT STORE. HyprStore holds the ENTIRE hypr.json (appearance, input, cursor, env, windowRules, layerRules, appOverrides, autostart, keybinds, anim, plugins) and save() marshals snapshot() — all of it — through `ryoku-hub hypr save`. This page owns its own HyprStore instance; saving Appearance rewrites domains it does not display. Two hub pages open on the same store can clobber each other.

PHANTOM KEYS THAT MUST STILL BE WRITTEN. store.plugins is one `var` JS object serialized whole, but this page exposes only some of it. plugins.hyprfocus.* (enabled/mode flash|bounce|slide/opacity 0.8/bounce 0.95/slide 20), plugins.imgborders.sizes ("8,8,8,8") and .insets ("0,0,0,0"), plugins.hyprglass.tint ("8899aa22"), plugins.dynamicCursors.magnify (4) have NO control here. A rewrite to per-key declarative properties will silently drop them from hypr.json on the next Save.

"Reset to defaults" IS WIDER THAN THE PAGE. resetAppearance() restores appearance + cursor keys AND calls resetPlugins(), which replaces the whole plugins object — including hyprfocus, which has no control on this page (deliberate per the comment, but it means a button on Appearance silently resets another page's settings). It also queues a preview but does NOT save.

CROSS-TAB, CROSS-STORE VISIBILITY GATE. Borders/FIXED COLOURS (activeBorder + inactiveBorder) renders only when page.scheme !== "follow", and scheme is a Wallpaper-tab control backed by a DIFFERENT file (theme.json) via a different command. In the shipped default (follow) those two colour settings are unreachable. Also: the Borders tab only re-queries the scheme in onGroupChanged, so it can render stale.

A FOURTH, UNSELECTABLE ENUM STATE. currentScheme() can return "custom" (a theme owns a fixed palette) but applyScheme() only accepts follow|light|dark. The ChoiceRow then highlights nothing and a dedicated help string appears. The control must render a value it cannot produce.

KEY/LABEL AND PROP/KEY MISMATCHES. windowStyle "gnomed" -> "Gnome"; hyprglass "high_contrast" -> "Contrast". Cursor props are stored as cursorTheme/cursorSize/cursorInactiveTimeout/cursorHideOnKeyPress but written as cursor.theme/size/inactiveTimeout/hideOnKeyPress via HyprStore.cursorProp() string surgery. Nothing is derivable from the label.

NON-ENUMERABLE OPTION LIST. The cursor Theme Dropdown's options come from a Process (`ryoku-hub hypr cursors`) that scans installed icon sets at runtime; placeholder falls back to store.cursorTheme so an unlisted theme still shows. It cannot be expressed as a static enum, and the list may be empty when the page first renders.

SETTINGS WITH NO CONFIG KEY AT ALL. Brightness is hardware (brightnessctl); night light on/off IS the presence of a hyprsunset process (`pgrep -x`); night temperature lives as a bare integer in ~/.local/state/ryoku-nightlight written only as a side effect of turning the light ON; wallpaper is a path in ~/.local/state/ryoku-wallpaper owned by ryoku-shell. Four "settings" with no declarative home.

THE TEMPERATURE SLIDER IS A NO-OP WHILE THE LIGHT IS OFF. setNightTemp() only restarts the 300ms debounce `if (page.nightOn)`, yet the row is always visible and fully interactive; the chosen value is never persisted and is lost on reload (status reports a temp only while hyprsunset runs). Its 4000 default is duplicated in QML and in the shell script.

THE BRIGHTNESS SLIDER LIES DURING LOAD. `value: page.brightness < 0 ? 1 : page.brightness/100` shows a confident 100% before brightnessctl returns, and the binding can produce values below the slider's own `from: 0.05`. It is a read-modify-write over live hardware, not a config value.

AD-HOC PER-ROW VISIBILITY, 20+ TIMES. Rows gate on sibling values via inline `visible:` (dimInactive, animations, glowEnabled, animatedBorder, layout==="scrolling", and five plugin `enabled` flags), and the help Texts carry their own copies of the same conditions. But the gating is INCONSISTENT and must be copied literally: blurEnabled does NOT hide Size/Passes/X-ray/Vibrancy/Noise, and shadowEnabled does NOT hide Shadow range/sharpness — while glowEnabled DOES hide Range/Colour. Rows are hidden, not removed.

BESPOKE CONTROL FOR A STRING SETTING. plugins.imgborders.image is not a SettingField — it is a hand-rolled Row (ElideMiddle path Text with an empty-state string and a colour swap) + HubButton + a page-level ImagePicker modal whose overlay is deliberately hoisted out of the borders column. The picker writes the raw path through editPlugin.

COMPONENT-OWNED, NOT SETTING-OWNED, PRESETS. ColorField hardcodes 8 DARK swatches (#1a1b26 … #232136) and is reused for all three colour settings, including glowColor (default #ee33cc, a magenta) where a dark-surface preset strip makes no sense. Its hex validator is 6-digit RGB only — no alpha — while the backend's hyprglass.tint is RRGGBBAA.

SLIDERROW IS SEMANTICALLY OVERLOADED. Same control spans percent readouts (opacity, dim, column width, glass opacity, brightness), raw floats at 1/2/3 decimals (blur strength, vibrancy, noise), and integers-as-floats (borderAngleSpeed decimals:0, nightTemp step 100). Kelvin is never shown as a unit; "Passes" and "Shadow sharpness" have no unit either. Unit/precision metadata is inconsistent and partly absent.

REVERT IS NOT A PURE UNDO. store.revert() re-adopts committed AND shells `ryoku-hub hypr restore` (a full reload) because eval cannot push some keywords back to a default nor re-derive wallpaper-derived border colours. The comment notes it depends on the page still being ALIVE for the process to run — a lifetime coupling a rewrite can quietly break.

LOADER-PER-TAB DESTROYS STATE. Tab content is created/destroyed on every switch (with a fade), and refreshes are driven imperatively from onGroupChanged rather than by binding, so scroll position and in-flight field edits vanish and freshness depends on that switch hook firing.

THE RICES TAB IS A DIFFERENT UNIVERSE. Tab 1 of 6 is not settings at all: it is a whole embedded page (galleries, a capture wizard, modals, a config viewer, install/apply/delete/fork/export actions) sharing this page's Flickable via implicitHeight, with no action bar and its own Segmented inside the tab body. A settings-schema rewrite has nowhere to put it.

DUPLICATED DEFAULTS IN THREE PLACES. HyprStore's QML draft properties, the Go defaultOverrides(), and the shipped Lua modules all restate the same baseline (the code comments say "keep these in step"). resetAppearance() reads store.defaults (fetched via `ryoku-hub hypr defaults`), so the QML initialisers are only a pre-adopt placeholder — a rewrite that treats them as the source of truth will drift.

ONLY DIVERGENCE IS PERSISTED. The backend diffs against defaults and writes only what differs, so an untouched setting deliberately falls through to the shipped base module (and inherits future changes) rather than being pinned. A rewrite that writes every key on save silently freezes the whole surface.
EnvironmentPage.qml: Not a settings form at all: env is an open-ended, ordered array of user-authored NAME=value pairs. There is no enum of keys, no per-key default, no type, no min/max. A declarative rewrite that models pages as a table of (key → control → default) cannot express this — it needs a first-class repeatable/array editor primitive with add/remove/reorder-free semantics.

The focus-vs-rebind trap is documented in the file's own comment and WILL bite a rewrite: editList swaps the whole array, so the Repeater rebinds and tears down + rebuilds the delegate that owns the focused TextInput. Rows therefore commit onEditingFinished ONLY, and every helper hands editList a fresh slice instead of mutating the live list. A naive declarative binding (two-way text binding, or onTextChanged → patch) destroys the field mid-keystroke and loses input.

Index-based mutation: patch(i,…) / remove(i) address rows by the Repeater's `required property int index`. Any rewrite that reorders, filters, sorts, or virtualises the model breaks the index↔array correspondence silently. There are no stable row IDs.

Two-level identity semantics: patch() does a[i] = Object.assign({}, a[i]) — a shallow copy of the row inside a fresh slice of the array. Reactivity depends on BOTH the array and the row object changing identity. A rewrite using a QAbstractListModel or in-place mutation loses the dirty check, which is a JSON.stringify(snapshot) !== JSON.stringify(committed) comparison.

No live preview, and the status text lies about it. store.editList explicitly does not queuePreview (lists land on Save), and env is doubly deferred: Hyprland reads its env only at session startup, so even after Save + hyprReload the variables do not take full effect until next login. The action bar still reads "Saved · applies on save", which contradicts the intro copy's "take full effect at next login". A rewrite must not wire env into the live-preview path, and should reconcile that copy.

The store is the WHOLE override document, not this page's slice. store.save() serialises snapshot() — appearance, input, cursor, all rule lists, keybinds, anim, plugins — so Saving from Environment persists whatever else is in this store instance. Symmetrically, Revert re-adopts the entire committed doc and fires `ryoku-hub hypr restore` (a full hyprctl reload plus a setcursor re-assert), which is far wider than "undo my env edits". Any rewrite that shares one store across pages inherits this blast radius.

No per-domain reset exists for env. HyprStore has resetAppearance() and resetInput() (both guarded to avoid clobbering other domains) but no resetEnv(); "Clear all" empties the list rather than restoring defaults. Since the default IS [], these coincide today — but a rewrite that adds shipped default env vars would make Clear-all and reset diverge.

Validation lives in the backend, not the UI, and fails silently. genConfig skips any entry with strings.TrimSpace(e.Key) == "", so a blank-NAME row marks the store dirty, survives Save into hypr.json, and is then dropped when settings.lua is rendered — no error, no UI feedback. Duplicate NAMEs are also accepted with last-write-wins. There is no name validation (no shell-identifier check) and no value escaping surfaced to the user (luaStr handles quoting).

Cross-domain env collision: the Cursor domain emits hl.env(XCURSOR_THEME/XCURSOR_SIZE/HYPRCURSOR_THEME/HYPRCURSOR_SIZE) into the same settings.lua — but only when the cursor diverges from defaults, and BEFORE the user env loop. So a user row named XCURSOR_THEME silently overrides the Cursor page's setting, conditionally. Two UI surfaces write the same on-disk keys with an order-dependent winner.

save() is fire-and-forget and optimistic: it sets store.committed = snapshot() immediately after launching the Process, so `dirty` clears whether or not `ryoku-hub hypr save` actually succeeded. There is no exit-code check, no error surface, no rollback. A rewrite that adds real error handling changes observable behaviour.

Layering is invisible in the UI: settings.lua is loaded after the base modules but before user.lua, so a hand-written user.lua hl.env still wins over anything this page writes, and the page shows no indication of a shadowed variable. Likewise `hypr get` re-emits settings.lua as a side effect (to heal a deploy that wiped it), so merely opening the page touches disk.

Bespoke inline controls, not the shared component vocabulary: the key/value fields are raw TextInputs in hand-styled Rectangles rather than SettingField, and the delete affordance is a hand-built Item with HoverHandler/TapHandler rather than a HubButton. Their look (mono 13px, Theme.surfaceLo, ember focus border, placeholder-child pattern, 0.94 press scale) is reproduced by hand here and must be preserved or deliberately re-based onto shared components.

Proportional layout math is hand-rolled (keyW = round(fieldsW * 0.42), delW 32, gap 10, fixed 48px row / 32px field heights, col.width = flick.width - 12). A rewrite onto a layout-managed row component will shift these proportions unless the ratio is carried over.

Destructive actions have no confirmation and no undo once saved: per-row trash deletes immediately, and "Clear all" wipes the entire list in one click. Revert is the only escape hatch and only while still dirty.
ConnectionsPage.qml: Scope trap: an inventory of this file is NOT the Connections feature contract. All user-facing settings live in WifiTab.qml / BluetoothTab.qml / HotspotTab.qml. Rewriting this file against an empty settings array is safe; treating the Connections *section* as settings-free would silently drop ~1,690 lines of controls across three sibling files.

Loader fallback is a nested ternary, not a declarative key->component map. Only "wifi" and "bluetooth" are matched by name; EVERY other value of `sub` (including a typo, an empty string, or a future 4th key) falls through to hsComp and silently renders Hotspot. A rewrite to a lookup map changes this behavior from 'silently shows Hotspot' to 'shows nothing / errors'.

Existing divergence between the two consumers of `sub`: Segmented.indexOfKey() returns 0 for an unknown key (pill parks on Wi-Fi) while the Loader's else-branch resolves to Hotspot. For an unrecognized `sub`, the pill and the body already disagree. Preserve or deliberately fix — do not assume it is unreachable.

Loader destroys the outgoing tab on every subtab switch. All in-tab state is discarded: scan results, scroll position, in-flight connect/pair dialogs, typed passwords, pairing PINs. Keeping all three alive, adding a cache, or using asynchronous loading changes observable behavior (and re-triggers whatever scanning the tabs start on construction).
`page.sub` is view state with no persistence: the page always opens on Wi-Fi. Do not 'helpfully' promote it to a stored config key during a rewrite — that is a product decision, not a refactor.

Segmented geometry is fixed and label-hostile: readonly segW 86, implicitHeight 34, implicitWidth = count*86+8, with labels centered and unelided. Longer labels, a translated locale, or a 4th tab will clip or overflow rather than reflow. Tab labels are effectively locked to the current widths.

`pragma ComponentBehavior: Bound` at the top of this file, paired with `required property var modelData` in Segmented's delegate. Dropping the pragma or the required declaration during a rewrite breaks delegate resolution at runtime, not at build.

The subtab bar's 3-entry model is an inline JS array literal inside the Segmented instantiation — no enum, no shared constant. The string keys "wifi"/"bluetooth"/"hotspot" are duplicated between the model, the Loader ternary, and `sub`'s initial value; they must stay in sync by hand.

Per the file's own header comment, the tabs are backed by the Quickshell Networking/Bluetooth services *plus nmcli and bluetoothctl shell-outs*. The Connections surface is therefore imperative device I/O (scan, pair, connect, up/down), not declarative config that can be diffed and applied — a settings-shaped rewrite will not fit the sibling tabs even though it fits this shell.

`import "Singletons"` is present in this file but nothing here references a Theme member directly (only Segmented.qml does). A linter or an eager cleanup pass may flag it as unused; it is load-bearing for the sibling imports' path resolution style used across the hub.
RashinPage.qml: The single toggle is NOT a config-key binding. It shells out to `ryoku-rashin enable|disable`, which both writes rashin.json AND enables/starts a systemd user unit (or falls back to a detached spawn / SIGTERM by pidfile). A declarative 'write this key to this file' setting model would produce a toggle that flips a boolean and starts nothing. The CLI call is the contract, not the key.

Optimistic-then-reconciled state with silent revert: onToggled assigns page.daemonEnabled = c immediately, the CLI runs, then onExited -> refresh() overwrites daemonEnabled from `status --json`. enableProc/disableProc wire only onExited — exit code and stderr are discarded, so a failed enable surfaces ONLY as the switch snapping back with no message. Any rewrite that adds a proper error path is changing behavior; one that keeps a naive two-way binding will fight the refresh.

All truth is an out-of-process CLI JSON snapshot, not a watched file. rashin.json is never read or watched by this page. An external `rashin enable`, a systemd state change, or a crashed daemon will not update the page until something calls refresh() — and refresh only fires on Component.onCompleted, after enable/disable exits, and on setup phase 'done'. There is no poll timer and no reload on page re-entry.

'not installed' is inferred, not reported: statusProc is wrapped in `sh -c` purely so a missing binary still closes stdout and fires onStreamFinished; empty or malformed JSON is then caught and read as installed=false, which disables every control on the page. This is load-bearing and easy to destroy by 'cleaning up' the command to a bare binary invocation or by tightening the try/catch.

Dashboard port is hardcoded TWICE in the QML (the execDetached URL at line 122 and the caption text at line 307) while rashin.json has a real `port` key (default 3600, clamped to 1..65535 by LoadConfig). Change the port in config and this page opens and advertises the wrong URL. The bug exists today; a rewrite should not enshrine it, but also must not silently invent a port setting.

rashin.json carries keys this page deliberately does NOT expose: `port`, `quick.model`, `quick.baseUrl`, `quick.keyEnv`, and `habits.history` (opt-out semantics — nil means enabled, so an absent key keeps history mining ON). A rewrite generated from the config schema rather than from this page will wrongly surface them. Their absence here is intentional.

`enable --at-boot` (loginctl enable-linger, needs polkit auth or root once) is reachable from the CLI but never from this page — the toggle always calls bare `enable`, i.e. start-at-login only. Modelling the toggle as a tri-state or adding an at-boot option is NEW surface, not preserved surface.

The Hermes setup is an external wizard, not an in-page flow. It depends on three things outside the QML: the `ryoku-rashin-setup` kitty window class, the hyprland rule 'float-ryoku-rashin-setup' that floats it, and a runtime JSON file for progress. There is no in-page fallback if kitty is missing, and execDetached reports nothing back.

setupPhase/setupDetail/setupOk are transient and never cleared: once setup runs, the progress line stays visible for the life of the page (visible is keyed on setupPhase !== ""). FileView.path is evaluated once at construction from XDG_RUNTIME_DIR (fallback /tmp), so it will not follow a changed runtime dir, and onLoadFailed swallows silently.

StatusRow's `level` color encodes multi-state logic, not decoration: Hermes is a 3-tier (ok = configured / ember = installed-only / dim = absent), Agents wired is a 3-tier (ok = all wired / ember = some / dim = none), Daemon is a 3-tier across two booleans (ok / dim / faint). Flattening these into a declarative label+value readout loses the semantics.

Layout is parent-dependent and inconsistent: StatusRow uses `width: parent ? parent.width : 0` (a null-guard because it is instantiated inside SettingSection's default-property Column), ToggleRow is given `width: parent.width` explicitly, and the HubButtons set no width at all (implicit-width chips). A layout rewrite that normalises widths will visibly change the buttons.

No `defaults` object literal and no draft QtObject exist in this file — the default (enabled=false) lives in Go (ryoku/rashin/backend/config.go:32 `defaultConfig()`). There is nothing in QML to diff a rewrite's defaults against, and no Save/Apply step: the toggle is apply-on-touch.

The page has no tabs, no Repeaters, no set-membership toggles, and no ChoiceRow/Slider/NumberField/ColorField/Dropdown/Segmented/ImagePicker. It is ~90% host/report surface with exactly one boolean. The rewrite risk here is inflation (inventing settings from the config file or the CLI verbs) far more than omission.
PerformancePage.qml: lowPowerMode implication is NOT materialised on disk or in the UI. The page writes only the raw key; consumers OR it in (`lowPower || adapter.X`). So with lowPowerMode on, the four LOW POWER sub-toggles still render UNCHECKED while behaving as on. A rewrite that computes `checked:` from a derived value, or that writes the derived values through to JSON, would change both the visible state and the file's shape (and would make un-toggling lowPowerMode unable to restore the user's prior individual choices).

The implication scope is asymmetric and the top label overstates it. lowPowerMode implies reduceMotion, disableBlur, disableShadows, freezePillWhenIdle, freezeVisualizerWhenIdle — but NOT unloadWidgetsWhenCovered, unloadVisualizerWhenSilent, unloadLauncherWhenIdle, unloadOverviewWhenIdle (the Go watchers call bare perfFlag with no lowPower OR). The label's phrase "implies all four toggles below" is scoped to the LOW POWER section, while the file header comment claims it implies "every reduce/disable switch". A declarative `impliedBy: lowPowerMode` model must reproduce this exact, uneven membership.

reloadCompositor() fires on only 3 of 10 toggles. A uniform generated onToggled handler would either spam `hyprctl reload` on shell-only keys or drop it from the compositor-visible ones. The set {lowPowerMode, disableBlur, disableShadows} is load-bearing and must be per-setting metadata, not a page-level rule.

Defaults are duplicated across six independent JsonAdapters/readers with partial key subsets: this page (all 10 keys), pill/Singletons/Performance.qml (5), visualizer/Singletons/Performance.qml (3, freezeVisualizerWhenIdle: true), widgets/Singletons/Performance.qml (2), launcher/Singletons/Performance.qml (2), pill+launcher+overview Motion.qml (2), plus Go perfFlag (missing -> false) and perfFlagDefault("unloadWidgetsWhenCovered", true). The two default-TRUE keys (freezeVisualizerWhenIdle, unloadWidgetsWhenCovered) must survive verbatim in every copy — a centralising rewrite that changes either default silently regresses an NVIDIA animation leak and the widget memory reclaim.

cfg.writeAdapter() serialises the whole JsonAdapter object. Any key present in performance.json but absent from the adapter's property list is dropped on the next write, and any key the rewrite renames orphans the old one while consumers (Go + Lua + 5 QML singletons) keep reading the old name. The page is the file's only writer, so a key dropped here is unrecoverable for the user.

decoration.lua reads performance.json at Hyprland parse time with a raw io.open plus a text pattern match, not a JSON parser (perf_flag). Reformatting the file — pretty-printing, key reordering, nesting keys under a group object, or emitting non-boolean values — can break compositor blur/shadow stripping without any QML-side symptom.

ToggleRow renders its label as single-line Text with elide: ElideRight and no wrapping, sized `parent.width - track.width - 14`. The very long labels on this page (notably lowPowerMode) already depend on that geometry. Splitting label vs. description, or moving prose into SettingSection.description, is a visible layout change, not a refactor.

Group title "BAR" vs. key freezePillWhenIdle vs. singleton property pillFrozen — the user-facing noun (bar, glowing bead) and the internal noun (pill) diverge. Label/key generated from a single source of truth will rename one of them.

blockLoading: true means the config read is synchronous on page construction, and the seeding write depends on cfg.text() being empty at Component.onCompleted. A rewrite that makes loading async, or that seeds before the first read resolves, can clobber an existing performance.json with defaults.

These toggles are opt-in optimisations with user-visible costs stated in their own labels ("brief delay on the next open", widgets vanish, visualiser stalls). Anything that reorders, regroups, or auto-enables them changes a behaviour contract the labels promise; the unload*/freeze* keys in particular are default-off on purpose except the two noted.
LockscreenPage.qml: THE OPTION SET IS NOT STATIC AND CANNOT BE HARDCODED. Options are fetched per page load from the GitHub git-tree API for Darkkal44/qylock@main. Only clockwork/orbital and clockwork/tape appear literally in source (as curated copy, not as an option list). Freezing this into a declarative enum destroys the page's entire purpose ("new skins show up without a Ryoku release").
THE WRITE IS NOT A KEY/VALUE WRITE. ~/.config/qylock/theme is a bare newline-terminated slug — no key, no JSON, no conf section. A settings framework keyed on `file + key` has nowhere to put this.

ONE CHOICE, THREE WRITES, TWO PRIVILEGE DOMAINS. Applying writes the user pref AND recursively copies the skin to /usr/share/sddm/themes/ryoku AND writes /etc/sddm.conf.d/99-ryoku.conf, with a chown/chmod normalisation pass (without a+rX the sddm user cannot read the theme and SDDM silently falls back to its embedded theme on every boot). Not expressible as "assign value to property".

PARTIAL-FAILURE SKEW IS BAKED INTO THE ORDER. setLockSkin() calls escalateGreeter() BEFORE setLockSkinIn(), so a cancelled polkit prompt leaves the pref untouched (good), but a successful greeter install followed by a failed pref write leaves the login screen and the in-session lock on different skins. A declarative binding has no transaction here.
APPLYING CAN BE DENIED BY THE USER. pkexec cancel -> non-zero exit -> page.error. Any rewrite needs an error channel per-apply; a plain ChoiceRow has none.

SELECTING IS THE COMMIT — THERE IS NO DRAFT AND NO SAVE. No `defaults` object literal and no QtObject draft property exists anywhere in this file. The default (clockwork/orbital) lives only as a shell fallback in lock.sh, and the page offers no reset.

THE STORED VALUE AND THE EFFECTIVE VALUE CAN DISAGREE. If the named skin's Main.qml is gone (uninstalled skin still named in the config, broken themes_link), lock.sh silently substitutes clockwork/orbital at lock time while the config keeps the dead slug. The Hub keeps showing the dead slug as active.

PER-ITEM LIFECYCLE, NOT A VALUE. Each option carries not-installed -> installing -> installed -> active, plus in-flight async state (page.pendingSlug / page.pendingInstall gate select(), the Refresh button, tile busy overlays, and the Preview chip). No toggle/choice primitive models this.

"PREVIEW" IS DESTRUCTIVE. The chip locks the user's real screen (execDetached of lock.sh, which also `killall -9 hyprlock swaylock wlogout`). It must not be rewritten into an inline preview widget, and it must stay installed-skins-only — the lock needs the files on disk.

THE LAZY-LOAD BINDING RELIES ON A DELIBERATE SIDE-CHANNEL. tile.onScreen reads viewport.contentY and viewport.height purely to force binding re-evaluation on scroll (the values are discarded). A rewrite that "cleans up" those two dead statements silently loads and animates every remote gif at once.

MASONRY IS AN IMPERATIVE HEIGHT GUESS. buildColumns() estimates tile height as 300 + ceil(blurb.length/30)*16 and greedily packs the shortest column. There is no declarative layout equivalent; the estimate is coupled to the tile's exact font sizes, paddings and 2-line blurb clamp.

ORDINALS ARE POSITIONAL AND UNSTABLE. Assigned as catalog index+1 by mutating the parsed JSON objects; catalog sort is active-first, then installed-first, then name — so a tile's displayed number changes when you install or activate anything.

THE MODEL IS UNTYPED JSON MUTATED IN PLACE. page.skins holds plain JS objects from JSON.parse with an `ordinal` field grafted on; tiles read modelData.active / .installed / .slug / .preview / .sizeKB / .tags / .name / .summary / .blurb defensively (|| fallbacks everywhere). No schema, no typed model.
loadFailed CONFLATES "parse error" WITH "zero skins". Both render the same "Install qylock to add some." message, even when the real cause is a malformed catalog payload.
REFRESH IS IMPLICIT AND UNCONDITIONAL. Every apply/install exit re-runs the full catalog fetch (network round-trip) regardless of outcome, including on failure.

GIF-TO-SLUG MATCHING IS FUZZY AND ALIAS-PATCHED. Preview resolution normalises names by stripping non-alphanumerics and matching the slug's top segment, with a hand-maintained alias table for the upstream names that still diverge. Previews are best-effort and can resolve to nothing (empty source -> lock-icon placeholder).
StorePage.qml: `store.tab` (default "plugins") is TRANSIENT view state, not a setting. It has no config key, no source file, and no persistence. A rewrite must resist the temptation to invent a key like `store.defaultTab` — the current product forgets the tab on every page construction, and synthesizing persistence would be a behavior change, not a faithful port.

The header refresh reaches imperatively into child internals across three different mechanisms: it WRITES a child property (`pluginsPage.refreshing = true`), and CALLS three child methods (`pluginsPage.loadCatalog()`, `pluginsPage.refresh()`, `extrasPage.reload()`). None of this is expressible as a declarative binding. Note `refreshing` is set by the parent for the plugins path but NOT for the bundles path — ExtrasPage.reload() sets its own `loading` internally. Any refactor to a uniform `child.refresh()` interface silently changes which flag drives the spinner.

`refreshBtn.spinning` is a readonly ternary binding onto two DIFFERENT properties on two DIFFERENT child types (`pluginsPage.refreshing` vs `extrasPage.loading`). The two children have no shared interface or base type — the polymorphism exists only in this one expression.

Both child pages are ALWAYS instantiated; only `visible` toggles. PluginsPage runs `Component.onCompleted: { refresh(); loadCatalog(); }` and ExtrasPage runs `Component.onCompleted: page.reload()`, so BOTH catalogues fetch eagerly the moment the Store is constructed, even if the user never opens the Bundles tab. Rewriting to a Loader / StackLayout with lazy instantiation would change fetch timing, drop the eager warm cache, and reset each page's scroll/detail state on every tab flip. This eager double-fetch is current behavior and must be preserved deliberately or changed deliberately.

Header `z: 3` and the header is NOT hidden when PluginsPage enters `view === "detail"`. Standalone PluginsPage hides its own refresh + tab strip on detail (`visible: page.view !== "detail"`), but StorePage's header has no such guard — the refresh button and Plugins/Bundles switch stay floating over the plugin detail showcase. Tapping refresh while a detail is open re-pulls the catalogue underneath it. This is either an intentional escape hatch or a latent bug; either way a rewrite will silently "fix" or break it.

storeMode is a property defined on the CHILDREN (PluginsPage.qml:29, ExtrasPage.qml:21), not here. StorePage's entire identity is `storeMode: true` on two pages — its behavior is invisible from this file alone and is negated at four separate `visible:` bindings and one `topPadding:` binding scattered across the children. Changing this file without auditing those five sites will produce duplicated chrome.

ExtrasPage installs run external processes in a FLOATING TERMINAL and watch a per-bundle report file under `$XDG_RUNTIME_DIR/ryoku-extras` (falling back to /tmp). PluginsPage shells out to `discover.sh --all` resolved from `RYOKU_SHELL_DIR` (falling back to `XDG_CONFIG_HOME`/`$HOME/.config` + `/quickshell/plugins/discover.sh`). These are filesystem/process side channels with env-dependent path resolution — nothing here is declarative or testable without the surrounding system.

Installed plugin state mutates on disk (plugins.json) and the running shell retunes live via a plugin-runtime watcher. Install actions reachable from this page have effects outside the Hub process that no config-key inventory can capture.

Segmented.qml hardcodes `segW: 86` and computes `implicitWidth: count * segW + 8`. Labels longer than the fixed 86px segment will overflow the sliding pill — the control does not measure its own text. Any relabeling of "Plugins"/"Bundles" in a rewrite is size-constrained.
The file has no PageHeader, so there is no in-file page title to port; the title is supplied by the nav rail. Grepping this file for its own name will find nothing.
RecordingPage.qml: fps is int-in-JSON but string-in-UI. `current: String(adapter.fps)` / `adapter.fps = parseInt(k)` is the only thing keeping the file numeric. A declarative rewrite that models this as a plain string enum (like the other four Dropdowns) will silently write "fps": "60", and the consumer's `cfg_get '.fps' 60` will hand a quoted value to gpu-screen-recorder's -f.

Every change writes the ENTIRE adapter to disk instantly (cfg.writeAdapter() in each onChosen/onToggled). There is no draft object, no Save, no Reset, no revert, and no per-field dirty state — so there is nothing to bind a "live preview before save" model to. Any rewrite introducing a draft/commit layer changes observable behaviour (recording.json currently changes on every click).

Defaults are duplicated across a language boundary and must not drift: the QML JsonAdapter (fps 60, framerateMode "cfr", quality "very_high", codec "h264", encoder "gpu", cursor true) and the shell consumer's cfg_get/cfg_bool fallbacks at /home/nero/Work/ryoku-arch/ryoku/hyprland/scripts/ryoku-cmd-screenrecord:406-410, 429-434, 481-482. They agree today. A defaults table generated only from the QML side leaves the shell's copy unreconciled.

Env vars outrank the file for five of six settings (RYOKU_SCREENRECORD_FPS / QUALITY / CODEC / FRAMERATE_MODE / ENCODER, plus RYOKU_SCREENRECORD_BACKEND / RECORDER / WF / WF_CODEC / WF_DEVICE / SLURP / NAME_FORMAT). The Dropdowns show the file's value, which may not be what actually records. The UNDER THE HOOD readout DOES resolve env overrides, so the page can legitimately contradict itself — the readout is right and the controls are wrong. `cursor` has no env override, breaking the symmetry.

The wf-recorder fallback backend (multi-GPU machines) reads only fps and quality — codec, framerateMode, encoder and cursor are silently ignored on that path. Four of the six settings on this page are conditionally inert depending on hardware the page never states, and nothing in the UI disables or annotates them.

The --info probe is an async shell-out with a time-boxed gsr probe (the file comment says it "can take a moment on first open"). It is fire-once at page load with no refresh, no retry, no timeout handling and no error surface — an empty `catch (e) {}` swallows everything. Not expressible as a declarative setting; it is an imperative side effect with a latency tail.
Backend rendering is a two-value guess: any infoBackend that is not exactly "gsr" renders as "wf-recorder", so a third backend or an error string would display as a confident lie.

Option labels are editorial content, not derivable from keys — "Constant (smooth, recommended)", "Variable (smaller, can look choppy)", "AV1 (best, newer GPUs)", "H.264 (most compatible)", "CPU (libx264)". A rewrite that prettifies keys ("cfr" -> "Cfr") loses the product's recommendation semantics. Same for the key/label mismatches: quality key `very_high` vs label "Very high", codec key `hevc` vs label "HEVC / H.265 (crisper)".

No cross-field validation exists and its absence may be load-bearing: encoder=cpu with codec=av1/hevc is freely selectable. Adding constraint logic in a rewrite is a behaviour change, not a fidelity fix.

FileView uses blockLoading: true and printErrors: false — load/parse/write failures are invisible by design. A rewrite that surfaces errors, or that loads async, changes both the failure UX and the first-paint ordering (the Dropdowns currently have real values on first frame).
Container is hardcoded to MP4 in both the readout string and the recorder invocation (-c mp4). It reads like a setting in the UNDER THE HOOD block but is not one, and must not become one by accident.

Page-level `readonly property var` option lists (fpsOptions, fmOptions, qualityOptions, codecOptions, encoderOptions) are plain JS arrays of {key,label} consumed by Dropdown's norm() helper, which also accepts bare strings and an optional `hint` field. This page uses no hints — a rewrite onto a stricter model type must keep the key/label shape.
AutostartPage.qml: COMMIT-ON-EDIT-FINISH IS LOad-BEARING, NOT A STYLE CHOICE. The header comment spells it out: rows must commit onEditingFinished because replacing the list rebuilds the Repeater's delegates mid-type. Any rewrite that binds the field to a per-keystroke setter (the obvious declarative shape) will destroy the focused TextInput on every character and make the field untypable. This is the single most likely regression.

THE PAGE SAVES THE ENTIRE DOCUMENT, NOT JUST autostart. store.save() serialises snapshot() — appearance, input, cursor, env, windowRules, layerRules, appOverrides, autostart, keybinds, anim, plugins — and writes all of it. Each Lua page instantiates its OWN HyprStore, so two pages open at once each hold an independent copy loaded at their own mount time; whichever saves last wins and can clobber the other's edits with its stale snapshot. A per-setting/per-page save model does not exist here and cannot be assumed.

A list is not a scalar setting. store.editList() deliberately skips queuePreview(), unlike edit() and editAnim(). Autostart cannot be expressed as a live-previewed declarative field — there is no hyprctl eval path for it, it lands only via Save → settings.lua regen → reload.

Even Save does not make it take effect. Commands are emitted into an hl.on("hyprland.start") hook, so they run at NEXT LOGIN despite Save reloading the config. "Saved · applies on save" in the action bar is therefore misleading for this specific page (it is copy inherited from Shell Settings), and the intro text contradicts it with "new commands start at your next login".

Blank commands are a silent-no-op trap: accepted by the UI, persisted to hypr.json, then skipped by genStartHook's TrimSpace check. A row can look saved and do nothing forever. Any validation added in a rewrite changes observable behaviour for existing configs.

Only divergences from defaults are written (hypr.go header). Autostart's default is [], so an empty list writes nothing and settings.lua's start hook may vanish entirely — genStartHook returns "" when it has no lines.

The generated start hook is SHARED with an unrelated setting. genStartHook conditionally prepends a `hyprctl setcursor <theme> <size>` line (when Cursor.Theme/Size diverge from defaults) ahead of the user commands. Autostart does not own its output block; a rewrite that regenerates this hook in isolation will drop the cursor line.

Identity-free list model. The Repeater is modelled on a plain JS array with no stable ids — index IS the identity. patch/remove/add all address by index and reassign the whole array. Reordering, async refresh, or a keyed delegate model would silently rewrite the wrong row.

dirty is a whole-document JSON.stringify deep-compare gated on ready, and ready only flips after the async `ryoku-hub hypr get` process returns. Before that, edits are possible but dirty stays false — so Save/Revert sit disabled and changes can be lost. Key ORDER in the stringify also matters: patch() uses Object.assign({}, a[i]) which preserves it, but a rewrite that reconstructs row objects differently can produce a permanently-dirty page.
save() optimistically sets committed from the snapshot immediately, without waiting for saveProc to exit or checking its status. A failed write leaves the UI reporting "Saved" while disk disagrees.

Command strings pass through luaStr() escaping into generated Lua. Quotes/backslashes/newlines in a command are a code-generation surface, not inert data — arbitrary user text is being compiled into an executable config file.
"Clear all" is destructive, always enabled, unconfirmed, and irreversible except via Revert (which itself only works while dirty and triggers a full session restore).

revert() spawns a process from the page; its own comment notes this only works because "page is alive here" — tying correctness to component lifetime. A rewrite that unmounts the page faster (or reverts on close) breaks the restore.

Bespoke inline chrome throughout: the row, its focus ring, the placeholder, the trash button, and the scrollbar are all hand-rolled rather than SettingField/ToggleRow/etc. Porting to the shared control vocabulary is a visual and behavioural change, not a like-for-like swap — this page deliberately does not look like a SettingSection form.
ExtrasPage.qml: There is nothing to declare. This page has zero config keys, zero defaults, zero writes to disk. A settings-shaped rewrite has no rows to bind — if the rewrite framework assumes 'page = list of SettingSections of bound controls', this page cannot be expressed in it at all and must stay a bespoke Item.

All content is remote and dynamic. Bundles come from `ryoku-hub extras catalog`, which fetches raw GitHub (defaultExtrasBase = https://raw.githubusercontent.com/neur0map/ryoku-extras/main, overridable via RYOKU_EXTRAS_BASE) and caches under $XDG_CACHE_HOME/ryoku/extras (see /home/nero/Work/ryoku-arch/ryoku/hub/backend/extras.go). Card and detail fields (id, name, description, tagline, sources, icon, accent, preview, screenshots, items[]) are catalogue data, not enumerable options. The row set changes without a code change.

Install/remove are irreversible side effects in a detached external process, not value writes. No draft, no apply, no undo, no revert-to-default. Any rewrite that adds save/cancel or dirty-state semantics will mismodel this page.

Three-way status truth is load-bearing and fragile: live report (only while armed) > statusMap from `ryoku-extras-install status` > "absent" fallback. onStatusesChanged clears armed/live, so a fresh status query always wins back. Collapsing this to one source will either freeze stale progress or drop live progress.

The status vocabulary is a de-facto enum spread across three files with no single declaration: present|absent|installing|installed|removing|removed|failed|deferred|skipped (documented on ExtraItemRow.qml line 15). BOTH "present" and "installed" mean here — installedCountFor() and isHere() each check both, and dropping either silently breaks every count and badge.

Item type strings gate which action renders and are catalogue data, not a closed set in code: "package" and "nautilus-pack" get Remove, "plugin" defers to the Plugins page, others show "Manual". A new type published upstream changes behavior with no code change.
loadFailed is inferred from bs.length === 0, so a valid-but-empty catalogue is indistinguishable from a fetch failure and shows the error state with a Try again button.
storeMode is an embed flag, not a preference. If a rewrite mechanically converts properties into settings, this becomes a bogus user-facing toggle. It only hides the refresh chip.

The page is not routed by Hub directly — it exists only inside StorePage's Bundles tab. There is no PageHeader and no SettingSection scaffolding; layout is anchor-driven with two mutually exclusive views (Flow grid vs Loader detail) keyed off selectedId. Wrapping it in a standard settings shell will change its geometry and duplicate StorePage's refresh/tab chrome.

Bespoke chrome that a component library will not reproduce: hand-rolled ScrollBar contentItem (width 7 / 4px thumb / 0.4-0.7-0.9 opacity), MultiEffect hover shadow on cards, spinning RotationAnimation bound to page.loading, dual scrim gradients, and the 力 brand mark.
Hero images are remote URLs from the catalogue (asynchronous + cache Image with sourceSize 720/1200) with icon fallbacks gated on status !== Image.Ready. No local asset is guaranteed to exist.
The kitty terminal is hardcoded, along with --class ryoku-extras. Window/layer rules and the report-file handshake depend on that exact class, and there is no fallback if kitty is absent.
reportDir depends on XDG_RUNTIME_DIR at construction time with a /tmp fallback; the FileView path uses bundle.id || "_", so a bundle with no id silently watches a shared _.json.
